quotes.quote: We present ALMA Band 6 ^ 12 CO(2--1) line and rest-frame 232 GHz continuum observations of the nearby Compton-thick Seyfert galaxy NGC 5643 with angular resolutions 0.11-0.26 ( 9-21 pc). The CO(2--1) integrated line map reveals emission from the nuclear and circumnuclear region with a two-arm nuclear spiral extending 10 on each side. The circumnuclear CO(2--1) kinematics can be fitted with a rotating disk, although there are regions with large residual velocities and/or velocity dispersions.

The CO(2--1) nuclear kinematics can be fitted with a rotating disk which appears to be tilted with respect to the large scale disk. There are strong non-circular motions in the central 0.2-0.3 with velocities of up to 110 km s ^ 1 . In the absence of a nuclear bar, these motions could be explained as radial outflows in the nuclear disk. We estimate a total molecular gas mass for the nuclear disk of M( H _2)=1.1 10^7 M_ and an H _2 column density toward the location of the AGN of N( H _2) 5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , for a standard CO-to-H _2 conversion factor. We interpret this nuclear molecular gas disk as the obscuring torus of NGC 5643 as well as the collimating structure of the ionization cone.

sources.summary: The paper reports that prior NuSTAR fits exist for NGC5643, identifies it as reflection dominated, and notes its moderate Compton-thick column density.

quotes.quote: For many other sources, e.g. NGC1068, Circinus, NGC424, NGC3079, NGC4945, NGC5643, NuSTAR spectral fits have been presented elsewhere bauer2015, arevalo2014, balokovic2014, puccetti2016, koss2015, puccetti2014, annuar2015, marchesi2018 . In general, our results are consistent with the work cited above. For three objects the coronal temperature could not be constrained (2MFGC2280, NGC5643, NGC7130).

Four sources appear to be reflection dominated as the reflection radiation fraction is near unity. These are NGC1068, Circinus, NGC424 and NGC5643. A typical spectrum of a source with a strong reflection component (NGC5643) is shown in Fig. spectra . There is not a clear correlation between the strength of the reflection component and the column density in these sources. For example, NGC424 and NGC5643 have 'moderate' Compton-thick column densities 1.3 10^ 24 cm^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5643 as the shared spiral host of two normal Type Ia supernovae, close enough for primary distance calibration and showing little host-galaxy reddening at the supernova positions.

quotes.quote: We present photometric and spectroscopic observations of SN 2013aa and SN 2017cbv, two nearly identical type Ia supernovae (SNe Ia) in the host galaxy NGC 5643. The optical photometry has been obtained using the same telescope and instruments used by the Carnegie Supernova Project . This eliminates most instrumental systematics and provides light curves in a stable and well-understood photometric system. Having the same host galaxy also eliminates systematics due to distance and peculiar velocity, providing an opportunity to directly test the relative precision of SNe Ia as standard candles.

Both SN 2013aa and SN 2017cbv are located in the outer parts of NGC 5643 on opposite sides, having a host offset of 17 kpc and 14 kpc, respectively. We therefore expect minimal to no host-galaxy dust reddening for either object. However, NGC 5643 is at a relatively low galactic latitude ( l = 15.03 ) with a predicted Milky-Way color excess of E(B-V)_ MW = 0.15 mag Schlafly:2011 .

sources.summary: NGC 5643 is discussed as a 22 GHz systemic maser source whose 183 GHz spectrum shows only systemic features but higher isotropic luminosity than at 22 GHz.

quotes.quote: NGC 5643 The 22 GHz maser system in NGC 5643 was discovered by Greenhill_2003b . The 22 GHz spectrum contains only systemic features, and thus may not be associated with an edge-on disk; VLBI observations have also not yet been carried out on this system. Our 183 GHz spectrum of NGC 5643 is shown in the lower lefthand panel of fig:hex2 , along with a 22 GHz spectrum reproduced from Greenhill_2003b (see Fig. 1 in that paper).

The 22 GHz spectrum was observed with the 70 m Deep Space Network dish at Tidbinbilla. The 183 GHz spectrum of ESO 269-G012 shows three prominent sets of maser features, coinciding in velocity with the three groups of maser features seen in the 22 GHz spectrum and matching the expected spectral structure for an edge-on disk system. The line complexes in the 183 GHz spectrum of ESO 269-G012 are relatively rich compared to many of the other spectra in our sample, with each of the three sets of lines containing multiple identifiable maser features. The peak flux density of the systemic features in the 183 GHz spectrum is 17.2 mJy, which is comparable to the peak flux density of the 22 GHz systemic masers. The peak flux density of the high-velocity features is 30.8 mJy, which is a factor of 3 fainter than the peak flux density seen in the 22 GHz high-velocity masers. Adopting a Hubble law distance of 72 Mpc, the isotropic luminosity of the 183 GHz maser emission from ESO 269-G012 is L_ iso 1337 L _ . This value is roughly 2.5 times larger than the 496 L _ isotropic luminosity observed at 22 GHz Greenhill_2003b,Pesce_2015 . CGCG 074-064 The 22 GHz maser system in CGCG 074-064 was discovered by the MCP, and VLBI observations presented in Pesce_2020 confirm that it is a disk maser system. Our 183 GHz spectrum of CGCG 074-064 is shown in the middle lefthand panel of fig:hex2 , along with a 22 GHz spectrum reproduced from Pesce_2015 (see Fig. 1 in that paper). The 22 GHz spectrum is an average of multiple epochs of spectral monitoring observations taken with the GBT. The most prominent component in the 183 GHz spectrum of CGCG 074-064 is the central set of systemic maser features, which span a velocity range of 60 around a central velocity of 6910 . The peak flux density of the systemic features is 17.9 mJy, which is a factor of 11 fainter than the typical peak flux density of the 22 GHz systemic masers. A second component in the 183 GHz spectrum of CGCG 074-064 is a weaker (peak flux density of 6.7 mJy) feature spanning a velocity range of 20 around a central velocity of 6387 . This spectral feature coincides with the most redshifted end of the blueshifted complex of high-velocity maser features seen at 22 GHz. Adopting the symmetrized 87.6 7.5 Mpc distance measurement from Pesce_2020 , the isotropic luminosity of the 183 GHz maser emission from CGCG 074-064 is L_ iso 484 L _ . This value is a factor of 2 lower than the 851 L _ isotropic luminosity observed at 22 GHz Pesce_2015 . NGC 5495 The 22 GHz maser system in NGC 5495 was discovered by Kondratko_2006 , and VLBI observations presented in Gao_2017 confirm that it is a disk maser system. Our 183 GHz spectrum of NGC 5495 is shown in the middle righthand panel of fig:hex2 , along with a 22 GHz spectrum reproduced from Pesce_2015 (see Fig. 1 in that paper). The 22 GHz spectrum is an average of multiple epochs of spectral monitoring observations taken with the GBT. The only component seen in the 183 GHz spectrum of NGC 5495 is the central set of systemic maser features, which span a velocity range of 15 around a central velocity of 6787 . The peak flux density of the systemic features is 9.9 mJy, which is a factor of 12 fainter than the typical peak flux density of the 22 GHz systemic masers. Adopting a Hubble law distance of 96 Mpc, the isotropic luminosity of the 183 GHz maser emission from NGC 5495 is L_ iso 163 L _ . This value is a factor of 4 lower than the 625 L _ isotropic luminosity observed at 22 GHz Pesce_2015 . NGC 5643 The 22 GHz maser system in NGC 5643 was discovered by Greenhill_2003b . The 22 GHz spectrum contains only systemic features, and thus may not be associated with an edge-on disk; VLBI observations have also not yet been carried out on this system. Our 183 GHz spectrum of NGC 5643 is shown in the lower lefthand panel of fig:hex2 , along with a 22 GHz spectrum reproduced from Greenhill_2003b (see Fig. 1 in that paper). The 22 GHz spectrum was observed with the 70 m Deep Space Network dish at Tidbinbilla. The only component seen in the 183 GHz spectrum of NGC 5643 is the central set of systemic maser features, which span a velocity range of 90 around a central velocity of 1275 . The peak flux density of the systemic features is 32.9 mJy, which is a factor of 6 fainter than the typical peak flux density of the 22 GHz systemic masers. Because NGC 5643 is nearby enough that the Hubble law distance is expected to be very uncertain, we instead adopt a Type Ia Supernova distance of 12.4 0.5 Mpc to this system from Burns_2020 .

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE observations of NGC 5643 to map ionized gas, identifying a double-sided ionization cone, nuclear outflow, bar dust lanes, and possible outflow-triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: We study the ionization and kinematics of the ionized gas in the nuclear region of the barred Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 5643 using MUSE integral field observations in the framework of the MAGNUM (Measuring Active Galactic Nuclei Under MUSE Microscope) survey. The data were used to identify regions with different ionization conditions and to map the gas density and the dust extinction. We find evidence for a double sided ionization cone, possibly collimated by a dusty structure surrounding the nucleus.

In this paper we present observations of the nuclear region of NGC 5643, a barred, radio-quiet Seyfert 2 galaxy at a distance of 17.3 Mpc seen almost face on ( i 27 5 , de Vaucouleurs et al. RC2cat76 ), with a well known ionization cone extending eastward of the nucleus, parallel to the bar (Schmitt et al. schmitt94 , Simpson et al. simpson97 , Fischer et al. fischer13 ). A three color image (B,V, I bands) from the Carnegie-Irvine Galaxy Survey (CGS, Ho et al. ho11 ) is shown in Fig. 3colors , panel a . As usually observed in barred spiral galaxies, sharp, straight dust lanes extend from the sides of the central nucleus out to the end of the bar, roughly parallel to its major axis and being more clearly visible to the East of the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper accounts for a ULX contaminant in NGC 5643, fits the nucleus as heavily obscured, and finds strong reflection from its AGN.

quotes.quote: XMM-Newton observations ( Gua04 ; Mat13 ) showed that NGC 5643 contains a ULX, which is located at 0.9' from the nucleus and cannot be resolved with the Suzaku beamsize ( 2' ). Because the nucleus is heavily obscured, the ULX emission may contaminate the XIS spectra at energies below 10 keV. Hence, we take into account the ULX spectrum (see Appendix) in our spectral analysis. The base-line model plus the ULX component and two apec models well reproduces the spectra ( ^2/d.o.f = 109.5/114).

The large hydrogen column density of N_ H = 9.4^ +6.1 _ 3.2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and strong reflection strength of R_ torus 0.9 confirm that NGC 5643 has a heavily obscured AGN. We obtain an (apparent) scattered fraction of f_ scatt = 10.1^ +23.2 _ 7.4 , although it strongly depends on the assumed flux level of the ULX.

sources.summary: The paper studies multiphase feedback in NGC 5643, finding molecular and ionized outflow components, an east-west outflow aligned with the radio jet, and evidence for mixed AGN feedback modes.

quotes.quote: We study the multi-phase feedback processes in the central 3 kpc of the barred Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 5643. We use observations of the cold molecular gas (ALMA CO(2-1) transition) and ionized gas (MUSE IFU optical emission lines). We study different regions along the outflow zone which extends out to 2.3 kpc in the same direction (east-west) as the radio jet, as well as nuclear/circumnuclear regions in the host galaxy disk.

The CO(2-1) line profiles of regions in the outflow and spiral arms show two or more different velocity components, one associated with the host galaxy rotation and the others with out/inflowing material. In the outflow region, the [ O iii ] 5007 emission lines have two or more components: the narrow component traces rotation of the gas in the disk and the others are related to the ionized outflow. The deprojected outflowing velocities of the cold molecular gas (median V_ central 189 km s ^ 1 ) are generally lower than those of the outflowing ionized gas, which reach deprojected velocities of up to 750 km s ^ 1 close to the AGN, and their spatial profiles follow those of the ionized phase.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5643 as a face-on barred Seyfert 2 whose ionization cone and low-luminosity radio jet align east-west, while MUSE finds perpendicular line-width enhancement.

quotes.quote: NGC 5643 ( D 16 Mpc; 1 '' 78 pc) is a barred radio-quiet ( leipski2006 ) Seyfert 2 galaxy seen almost face-on. It hosts an ionisation cone extending over a few kiloparsec eastward of the nucleus (e.g. schmitt1994 , simpson1997 ) and inclined by 40 to the galaxy disc, according to the model by . An outflow along the cone was found in [ O iii ] with MUSE by (as part of our MAGNUM survey), who also detected the western side of the ionisation bi-cone.

The galaxy also hosts a low-luminosity bipolar radio jet aligned with the ionisation cones and on similar scales, spanning 25--30 '' ( 2 kpc; morris1985 , leipski2006 ), which is interacting with the galaxy disc ( Cresci:2015aa , Alonso-Herrero:2018aa , Garcia-Bernete:2020aa ). Figs. fig:maps_n5643_1 and fig:maps_n5643_2 display our MUSE flux and kinematic maps for NGC 5643, respectively.

In contrast, high values of the [ O iii ] line width (Fig. fig:maps_n5643_2 b), up to W70 700 km/s, are observed in the N-S direction, perpendicular to the ionisation cones and radio jet, over a distance of 1.5 kpc per side ( 3 kpc in total).

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 5643 as an outlier with a possibly variable nucleus, uncertain Compton-thick or Compton-thin absorption, and uncertain intrinsic AGN luminosity.

quotes.quote: fig:loiv_lbol , we highlight two AGNs from the D<15 Mpc sample (NGC 4945 and 5643) which appear to be significant outliers of the observed correlation. NGC 5643 possibly harbours a variable central source. From the detection of strong Fe K emission, maiolino98 suggest from using BeppoSAX data that NGC 5643 is possibly Compton thick (N _ H > 10^ 25 ); however, Guainazzi et al. (2004) find using XMM-Newton data, that it may be Compton thin with N _ H (6 - 10) 10^ 23 . Thus, from current available data, the true intrinsic luminosity of the AGN is highly uncertain.
Here we conservatively adopt the Compton thin L_X value of guainazzi04 ; however, we note that if we use the value of maiolino98 , then NGC 5643 would lie on our derived relationship.

sources.summary: The paper reports new NGC 5643 mid-infrared and near-infrared observations, explains which fluxes enter the SED, and treats it as an undisturbed Seyfert 2 galaxy.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 5643 was observed in May 2012 with the MIR camera/spectrograph T-ReCS Telesco98 on the Gemini-South telescope. The nuclear Q-band flux of NGC 5643, together with a 11.88 flux from Honig10 obtained with VISIR/VLT Lagage04 , are reported in Table mir . The galaxy was also observed with T-ReCS using the narrow Si-2 filter ( _c =8.74 , =0.78 ), but we did not include the Si-2 nuclear flux in the SED because it does not match the SED shape of the > 10 points and the spectrum, as the flux calibration in this filter is uncertain The galaxy was observed following the sequence: 1) PSF star at 8.7 ; 2) PSF star at 18.3 ; 3) NGC 5643 at 8.7 ; 4) NGC 5643 at 18.3 . In the NIR, NGC 5643 was observed with SOFI/NTT in June 1999 in the Ks filter with a seeing of FWHM = 1.6 .

In addition, to see different examples of the use of clumpy model fitting to IR SEDs using BayesClumpy , see Ramos09 , Ramos11 and Alonso11 . table We fit the Sy2 SED considering reprocessed torus emission and no foreground extinction, since NGC 1068, NGC 3081 and NGC 5643 are undisturbed, nearly face-on galaxies without prominent nuclear dust lanes.

sources.summary: NGC 5643 is analyzed as a Seyfert galaxy whose ionized bicone and molecular torus/disc reveal gas clouds around the nucleus and possible AGN fueling.

quotes.quote: The images of the Br and H _2 21218 emission lines from the treated data cube of the nuclear region of NGC 5643 reveal the existence of ionized and molecular-gas clouds around the nucleus, which cannot be seen clearly in the images from the non-treated data cube of this galaxy. The ionized-gas clouds represent the narrow-line region, in the form of a bicone. We observe a good correspondence between the positions of the ionized-gas clouds in the Br image and in an [ O III ] image, obtained with the Hubble Space Telescope , of the nuclear region of this galaxy convolved with an estimate of the point-spread function of the data cube of NGC 5643. The morphologies of the ionized and molecular gas seem to be compatible with the existence of a molecular torus/disc that collimates the active galactic nucleus (AGN) emission. The molecular gas may also flow along this torus/disc, feeding the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5643 as a type 2 Seyfert with nuclear spiral structure, radio features, compact hard X-ray source, Compton-thick absorption, and no spectral variations.

quotes.quote: NGC 5643 This galaxy was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert see an optical spectrum in Appendix multiimages philips1983 , and broad lines were not detected in polarized light gu2002 . The HST image shows a nuclear spiral structure (see Appendix multiimages ). VLA data show a nuclear counterpart alongside fainter features extending to the east and west at radio frequencies morris1985 . The XMM -Newton image shows a compact source at hard X-ray energies. This is a Compton thick object observed with BeppoSAX N_H > 10^ 25 cm^ 2 , comastri2004 .

sources.summary: NGC 5643 is treated as a Seyfert 2 with low-power radio jet and [Fe vii] emission, with coronal-line gas observed nearly 1 kpc east and west of the AGN.

quotes.quote: The sample employed in this work is composed of five local Seyfert 2 AGN ( z < 0.02 , where z is the redshift), widely known for displaying a low-luminosity radio-jet and a narrow line region (NLR) characterized by a bi-conical structure, previously mapped through the [ O iii ] 5007 emission. This is the case of the Circinus Galaxy, NGC 5728, IC 5063, NGC 3393 and NGC 5643. They all display [ Fe vii ] 6087 in the nuclear spectrum and a NLR that extends to at least 2 kpc from the AGN.

Finally, in NGC 5643, [ Fe vii ] is observed up to 0.8 kpc E and 0.9 kpc W to the AGN. To the best of our knowledge, these four Seyfert 2s display the largest CLR already detected in samples of local AGN.

sources.summary: The paper reduces NGC 5643 with a smaller aperture, finds negligible cluster contamination, and argues its continuum and large iron-line equivalent width support a Compton-thick, warm-scattering interpretation.

quotes.quote: NGC 5643 This source is close to a cluster of galaxies. To avoid contamination we reduced the extraction radius to 1 ' . We also checked the level of contamination by extracting the spectrum of two regions located at the same distance from the cluster as NGC5643 and found that the flux from the cluster contributes a negligible fraction of the total flux observed in NGC5643. A simple power law with = 1.7 , and no excess absorption with respect to the Galactic value (N _H=8 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ) fits the continuum significantly better than the cold reflection model. Both the absence of an absorption cutoff, in addition to the Galactic one, and the large EW of the iron line strongly support the idea that the source is Compton thick, and warm scattering dominated. If this were the case, ionized iron lines would be expected. The best fit energy of the gaussian in the rest frame of the galaxy is 6.46 ^ +0.20 _ 0.14 , i.e. the iron is less ionized than Fe xxii .

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 5643 as a radio-quiet AGN whose outflows form double-sided ionization cones and appear to compress clouds, triggering star formation.

quotes.quote: Cresci15 use the Measuring Active Galactic Nuclei Under MUSE Microscope (MAGNUM) survey and present evidence for positive feedback from NGC 5643, a radio-quiet AGN with outflows. They observe double sided ionization cones with high-velocity gas and star formation in clumps exposed to the resulting outflow, and they propose the compression of the clouds from the outflow is triggering the star formation. The clouds are located at 1.2 kpc. The projected velocity of the outflow is 423 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 5643 as a Compton-thick galaxy with a spectacular extended narrow-line region mapped by a two-field mosaic and catalogued HII regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 5643: Since this Compton-thick galaxy was known to have a spectacular ENLR Schmitt94 , we obtained a 2 field mosaic in order to map it completely. Nonetheless with our greater sensitivity, we find the \ emission can be traced right to the edge of the field (over 48 arcsec N-S and 38 arcsec E-W). On the southern side the brightest part of the ENLR is knotty and curved. The \ regions in this galaxy have been catalogued by Tsvetanov95 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5643 as a barred, nearly face-on Seyfert 2 with an eastward ionization cone, bar-aligned dust lane, and MUSE evidence for an outflow wing.

quotes.quote: NGC 5643 (Seyfert 2 - D_ L 17.3 Mpc - 1" 84 pc) is a barred almost face-on Seyfert 2. This galaxy is characterised by a well-known ionisation cone extending eastward of the nucleus and parallel to the bar (e.g. schmitt1994,simpson1997,fischer2013 ), and by a sharp, straight dust lane extended from the sides of the central nucleus out to the end of the bar, roughly parallel to its major axis and clearly visible to the east of the nucleus ho2011 . Our MUSE data of this target has been already published by cresci2015b , who analysed the double-sided ionisation cone, revealed as a blueshifted, asymmetric wing of the emission line, up to a projected velocity of 450 km s ^ 1 , parallel to the low-luminosity radio and X-ray jet, and possibly collimated by a dusty structure surrounding the nucleus. Furthermore, cresci2015b found signs of positive feedback triggered by the outflowing gas in two star-forming clumps, located at the edge of the dust lane of the bar (see silk2013 ), and also characterised by a faint CO(2--1) emission alonso-herrero2018 .

sources.summary: The paper finds bright extended mid-IR emission in NGC 5643, mostly perpendicular to the ALMA torus, increasingly aligned with the cone, and containing polar plus equatorial components.

quotes.quote: The PSF-subtracted images of six out of the twelve galaxies present bright extended mid-IR emission (Fig. fig:midIRimages ). These are NGC 1365, NGC 3227, NGC 5506, NGC 5643, NGC 6300, and NGC 7582. The contours show that the extended emission is detected up to at least a 20 _ bk level. For NGC 4388 we also detected extended mid-IR emission at a lower significance but in a similar orientation as that derived by using a different dataset. The sizes of the extended mid-IR emission range from approximately 50 pc in NGC 6300 to 190 pc in NGC 5506.

In NGC 1365, NGC 3227, and NGC 7582 as well as the inner regions of NGC 5506, the extended mid-IR emission appears to be approximately perpendicular (in projection) to the extended ALMA 870 m continuum emission. The latter for these four galaxies is identified as the torus. In NGC 5643, the orientation of the extended mid-IR emission is mostly along PA_ MIR = 60-70 ^ , which is nearly perpendicular (in projection) to the nearly north-south ALMA 870 m continuum emission. Finally, in NGC 6300 the extended mid- and far-IR emission show similar orientations, and thus the extended mid-IR emission is along the equatorial direction of the torus.

NGC 3227, NGC 4388 and NGC 7582 as well as NGC 1068 show evidence of molecular outflows on physical scales larger than the torus up to a few hundred parsecs, see and GB21 Davies2014, GarciaBurillo2019, DominguezFernandez2020 , which are more likely due to the interaction between the AGN wind and/or a radio jet if present and molecular gas in the disk of the host galaxy. NGC 5643 and NGC 5506 appear to have extended both polar and equatorial mid-IR emission. In NGC 6300 the mid-IR emission might be due to dust in the equatorial direction of the torus as well as in the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 5643's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 5643 is a face-on barred spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 16.9 3.4 Mpc tully_nearby_1988 with a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It features a compact radio core with two-sided, kiloparsec-scale lobes in east-west direction (PA 87 ; morris_velocity_1985, leipski_radio_2006 ) and a cospatial one-sided, kiloparsec-scale NLR cone (PA 84 ; e.g. simpson_one-sided_1997 ).

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 5643 as a face-on barred Sy2 spiral with a compact MIR nucleus embedded in spiral host emission and an arcsecond-scale MIR SED affected by star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 5643 is a face-on barred spiral galaxy with a Sy2 nucleus Veron10 . The IRAC and MIPS images show a compact MIR nucleus embedded within the spiral-like host emission Asmus14 . Moreover, the arcsecond-scale MIR SED is significantly affected by SF e.g. Shi06, Goulding09 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC5643 in a Compton-thick AGN infrared-colour sample, citing a deep Spitzer silicate absorption feature and mid-infrared measurements giving high 9-22 micron colour.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:fig11 we have four CT AGN (NGC6240, NGC7479, NGC6552, NGC5643) that is used to define [ 9 m - 22 m ] > 2.0 colour range.
Shi2006 present Spitzer IRS spectrum of NGC5643, measure a deep silicate absorption feature.
The MIR spectral measurements of NGC6240, NGC7479 and NGC5643 show evidence for relatively faint 9 m and relatively bright 22 m giving higher [ 9 m - 22 m ] colours.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 5643 as barred with well-defined spiral arms and finds a high cutoff mass in its truncated cloud mass distribution, yielding a shallower power-law component.

quotes.quote: Bar - Six of our 10 galaxies have stellar bars (NGC 1637, NGC 2903, NGC 3627, NGC 5068, NGC 5643, NGC 6300).
Spiral Arm - These clouds are located within a spiral arm of the galaxy with well defined arms (NGC 0628, NGC 1637, NGC 3627, NGC 5643).

For NGC 5643 and NGC 6300, the cutoff mass in the truncated mass distribution happens at high mass ( >10^7 M_ ), leading to a slightly shallower slope in the power-law component of the mass distribution.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5643 as an SABc Compton-thick AGN with a water maser and ULX, showing asymmetric azimuthal X-ray dependence and cone-region extended emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 5643 NGC 5643 is an SABc type galaxy at RA=14:32:40.74 (218.170 ), Dec=-44:10:27.86 (-44.174 ), and z=0.00400 (D _ lum 21 Mpc), with a water maser ( Gre03 ) and ultra-luminous X-ray source (e.g., Ann15,Pin16 ).
NGC 5643 NGC 5643 has an azimuthal dependence, as in the other CT AGN discussed thus far, however, this dependence is not symmetric around the central point source (Figure fig:grid:5643 ).
We find extended emission in the cone region for all five CT AGN in our sample, MKN 573, NGC 1386, NGC 3393, NGC 5643, and NGC 7212.

sources.summary: NGC 5643 is discussed as having confirmed molecular outflow evidence in CO and additional support from cold molecular, ionized-gas, and other gas-phase studies.

quotes.quote: NGC 5643 : The presence of an outflow is recently confirmed by high-resolution molecular CO (3--2) gas observations by Garcia-Burillo2021 . Also, evidence for a cold molecular outflow is reported by in S(1)H _ 2 (1--0) Davies2014 and in CO (2--1) Alonso-Herrero2018 and the outflow region is analysed in more detail by GarciaBernadette2020 using CO and ionized gas emission. Several other studies of different gas phases find evidence for an outflow e.g., for ionized gas Cresci2015 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5643 has a roughly 30-arcsecond two-sided diffuse radio jet matching NLR gas, with dust likely hiding the western ionization cone.

quotes.quote: NGC 5643 . This source has a diffuse radio jet on both sides of the nucleus which is nearly 30 ^ long (Fig. mrk266 , right). The whole radio structure matches well with the corresponding distribution of the NLR gas (Simpson et al. ). Although Simpson et al. ( ) detect only a one--sided ionisation cone on the eastern side of the nucleus they state that the western side suffers heavy extinction by a dust lane, thus shadowing the second cone.

gmw_00000239.name: NGC 5813
gmw_00000239.overview: NGC 5813 is a dynamically old, dominant central elliptical galaxy in a nearby subgroup [arXiv:1006.4379, arXiv:2209.09276] that hosts a kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:2001.11277, arXiv:2201.05080] and a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1101.0898, arXiv:1310.2770]. Its active galactic nucleus has produced multiple collinear X-ray cavities and shock fronts from repeated outbursts [arXiv:1101.4583, arXiv:2106.13259], which are co-spatial with extended, filamentary ionized gas [arXiv:2201.05080].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5813 as a dominant group-central E1 galaxy whose AGN produced multiple cavity/shock outbursts, uplifted cool gas, and varied in jet power over long timescales.

quotes.quote: We present results from new Chandra , GMRT , and SOAR observations of NGC 5813, the dominant central galaxy in a nearby galaxy group. The system shows three pairs of collinear cavities at 1 kpc, 8 kpc, and 20 kpc from the central source, from three distinct outbursts of the central AGN, which occurred 3 10^6 , 2 10^7 , and 9 10^7  yr ago. The H and X-ray observations reveal filaments of cool gas that has been uplifted by the X-ray cavities.

In this paper, we report on Chandra observations of NGC 5813 (UGC 09655), a bright ( M_V = -22.01 , Lauer \ 2007) E1 galaxy. It is the central dominant member of a subgroup (which we shall call the NGC 5813 galaxy group) in the NGC 5846 galaxy group (Mahdavi \ 2005).

Detailed Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) observations reveal that NGC 5813 contains a relatively undisturbed dusty circumnuclear disk (Tran \ 2001), suggesting that this galaxy has not recently experienced a major merger. Emsellem \ (2007) classify NGC 5813 as a ``slow rotator'' galaxy which, they argue, represents the extreme evolutionary end point reached in deep potential wells, consistent with this object being a dynamically old galaxy at the center of a galaxy subgroup. NGC 5813 contains a 2.8 10^8 M_ supermassive black hole (SMBH), and an associated AGN that is a known radio source ( , Balmaverde Capetti 2006; Magliocchetti Br u ggen 2007).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5813 as an old early-type galaxy with a LINER/composite nucleus and passive MIR spectrum dominated by circumstellar silicate emission.

quotes.quote: Notwithstanding the unperturbed morphology revealed by both the deep optical Tal09 and our UV imaging, NGC 3258 and NGC 5813 hide a different recent star formation history. The analysis of the optical line-strength indices performed by A07 shows that NGC 3258 has a ``young'' average stellar population (luminosity-weighted age of 4.5 0.8 Gyr). At odds, NGC 5813 is a relatively old ETG (11.7 1.6 Gyr).

Despite the remarkable similarity of the shape of their optical spectra, the nuclear MIR emission of NGC 3258 and NGC 5813, shown in the bottom panel of Figure fig2 , is completely different. The Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons emission (PAH) complexes at 6.2 m, 7.7 m, 11.3 m and 17 m dominate the MIR spectrum of NGC 3258. Their ratios indicate that the galaxy nucleus has undergone a recent starburst see details in Panuzzo10,Vega10 . At odds, the MIR spectrum of NGC 5813 displays a broad emission feature around 10 m attributed to the silicate emission arising from dusty circum-stellar envelopes of O-rich AGB stars, superimposed on the photospheric stellar continuum from red giant stars. This is the typical spectrum of passively evolving ETGs see e.g. Bressan06 .

sources.summary: Chandra observations show NGC 5813 as the central dominant group galaxy with three AGN outbursts, collinear cavities, shock fronts, varying jet power, and shock heating balancing cooling.

quotes.quote: We present results from Chandra observations of NGC 5813, the dominant central galaxy in a nearby galaxy group. We focus on three main results. 1) The diffuse gas shows clear signatures from three distinct outbursts of the central AGN, with three pairs of roughly collinear cavities. The inner two cavity pairs are associated with unambiguous elliptical shock fronts, with Mach numbers M 1.7 and M 1.5 for the inner and outer shocks, respectively.

NGC 5813 (UGC 09655) is the central dominant member of a nearby (redshift z = 0.006578 ) subgroup in the NGC 5846 galaxy group mahdavi05 . It was observed for a total of 150 ks with the Chandra X-ray Observatory . The Chandra image (Fig. ximg ) shows three pairs of collinear cavities. The corresponding temperature map, shown in Fig. tmap , reveals hot gas just inside the bright edges, indicating that these are shock fronts.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5813 to test two-dimensional core-Sersic fitting, finding a bulge-plus-disk light profile and discussing its dust and kinematically decoupled core.

quotes.quote: We demonstrate the software by fitting the images of NGC 5557 and NGC 5813 , which have been previously identified as galaxies by their 1D radial light profiles. These two examples are representative of different dust obscuration conditions, and of bulge/disk decomposition.

Moreover, NGC 5813 also includes a disk component (apart from the bulge component), hence it represented a good test for the robustness of our code with respect to multiple component analysis. In particular, high resolution is critical for the purposes of the current exercise, as previous 1D analysis have shown that depleted cores have linear sizes of few hundreds parsecs at most dullo:2012,dullo:2013b , which at the distances of our sample galaxies NGC 5557 47.9 Mpc; cantiello and NGC 5813 32.2 Mpc; tonry translates to few arcseconds.

NGC 5813 has been classified as elliptical galaxy (E1) in the Third Reference Catalogue RC3; RC3 . However, both their Appendix C trujillo:corser and found that the galaxy may be better described by a + disk component, hence suggesting it may be a S0 galaxy. The results of our 2D analysis ( Fit ) confirm that the galaxian light profile is well fit by the aforementioned components.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5813 as a relaxed bright elliptical/group center where AGN feedback, cool-gas uplift, and mixing shape the intragroup medium.

quotes.quote: We present a robust representation of the chemical and thermal structure in the galaxy group NGC 5813 using archival, deep X-ray observations, and employing a multi-temperature spectral model based on up to date atomic line emission databases. The selection of our target is motivated by the fact that NGC 5813 has a very relaxed morphology, making it a promising candidate for the study of the AGN feedback's influence in the intra-group medium (IGrM). Our results showcase a prominent, extended distribution of cool gas along the group's NE-SW direction, correlating with the direction along which the supermassive black hole in the group's central galaxy is known to interact with the IGrM.

In this paper we focus on the bright elliptical galaxy NGC 5813. This is the central dominant member of a subgroup, hereafter referred to as the NGC 5813 group, with an extensive diffuse X-ray emission. While part of the well-isolated NGC 5846 group z =0.006578; e.g. Mahdavi05,Machacek11 , with a projected separation of 740  kpc , the two show no signs of an interaction between them. NGC 5813 itself has been assumed to be dynamically old Emsellem07 , with no evidence of a recent major merger in its history, as indicated by the lack of any significant disturbances to its dusty circumnuclear disk Tran01 .

sources.summary: NGC 5813 is described as having multiple AGN-outburst X-ray cavities, shocks, aligned inner H filaments, a dusty circumnuclear disk, dust mass, and a steep abundance drop.

quotes.quote: NGC 5813 NGC 5813 has a spectacular well-studied system of X-ray cavities, produced in three separate AGN outbursts, and shocks Randall11 . It also has H filaments in its inner 5 kpc, which lie along the same direction as the X-ray cavities Werner14 . found a dusty circumnuclear disk in NGC 5813, using WFPC2 on board the Hubble Space Telescope . Using the Infrared Space Observatory ( ISO ), calculated a dust mass of 7 10 ^ 5 M _ , for a dust temperature of 20 K.

From the abundance profile of NGC 5813 in Fig. fig:abundprofscertain , there is a steep abundance drop in the innermost spectral bin. With respect to 0.6 Solar metallicity, the missing iron mass was estimated to be 7.2 10 ^ 5 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5813 as the second bright early-type member of the NGC 5846 group, sharing X-ray halo features, central activity signatures, and infrared line detections.

quotes.quote: The group is dominated by two bright ETGs, NGC 5846 and NGC 5813, that do not show clear signatures of interaction in UV and optical images. X-ray studies revealed the presence of an extended (50 -- 100 kpc radius) halo of NGC 5846, and numerous peculiar features, cavities and bubbles in both galaxies Trinchieri02, Mulchaey03, Werner2009, Machacek2011, Randall2011, Werner2014 . These two X-ray halos suggested the presence of sub-structures in the NGC 5846 group and motivated a wide literature about the determination of group members e.g. Tully87, Haynes1991, Nolthenius93, Giuricin00, Ramella02, Mahdavi05, Eige10 . The low optical luminosity galaxy population has been studied by and : nucleated dwarfs reside near the bright members, and only four dwarfs show fine structures or interaction signatures. At odd, signatures of activity are found in the inner part of the two bright members. Werner2014 detected H + [ NII ] at kpc scale and [ CII ] =157 m emission in both galaxies. Rampazzo2013 , using Spitzer, detected mid infrared lines like [ NeII ] =12.81 m, [ NeIII ] =15.55 m and some H _2 0-0 lines in both galaxies.
A high density region approximately centred on NGC 5846, and elongated towards NGC 5813, the second B-band brighter member, appears.

sources.summary: The paper presents detailed turbulence constraints for NGC 5813, reporting a peaked Mach-number distribution, testing prior assumptions, and finding agreement with previous shock-driven velocity estimates.

quotes.quote: In the main text we present detailed results for NGC 4636 and NGC 5813. Green regions in Fig. fig:pdf show the 68 per cent confidence intervals, providing constraints of M=0.44^ +0.21 _ 0.12 ( v_ 1D =111^ +54 _ 31 km s ^ 1 ) in NGC 5813 and M=0.52^ +0.22 _ 0.15 ( v_ 1D =121^ +51 _ 35 km s ^ 1 ) in NGC 4636. Two galaxies, NGC 4636 and NGC 5813, show clearly peaked distributions that allow us to report the most probable value with 68 per cent confidence (lower and upper limit; Fig. fig:pdf ).

This is illustrated in the right side of Fig. fig:prior , where the upper panel shows 68 per cent constraints in a peaked pdf in NGC 5813, and the lower panel shows 95 per cent lower limits in a non-peaked pdf in NGC 5044, for two different maximal Mach numbers: M_ max =1 (our default) and M_ max =2 (red and grey regions respectively). The lower limit in NGC 5813 is least affected by this assumption: the difference between the default case and larger M_ max is only 5 per cent (4 ), while the upper limit increases by 9 per cent (12 ). Our measurements also agree with estimations of shock-driven turbulent velocities in NGC 5813 Randall2015 , as well as with previous upper limits measured via line broadening with RGS data sanders2011,Sanders2013,Pinto2015 .

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 5813 as a deeply observed group-central feedback case with multiple X-ray cavities, repeated AGN outbursts, and shock fronts that heat intragroup gas.

quotes.quote: In particular, Randall:2015 presented the results of a very deep (650 ks) observation of NGC 5813, the longest such observation available to date. In Fig. fig:N5813 , we show the \ image and temperature map of NGC 5813, revealing an impressive number of feedback-induced features. Multiple pairs of X-ray cavities can be observed on both sides of the nucleus, indicating the system has undergone several consecutive AGN outbursts inflating powerful expanding bubbles. Two pairs of concentric shock fronts were discovered perpendicular to the jet axis.

This is again highlighted by the exemplar case of NGC 5813 (see Fig. fig:N5813 ), where each set of three cavities has been associated with an elliptical shock measured at 1 kpc, 10 kpc and 30 kpc, respectively, with Mach numbers M in the range 1.17-1.78. The cumulative heating effect of these successive shock fronts is sufficient to offset cooling within the inner 30 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5813 as a weak radio galaxy with triple central emission, steep outer lobes, and multiple aligned X-ray cavities from repeated activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 5813 is the least powerful in our sample (Table 1). We observed NGC 5813 only at 235 MHz (Fig. fig:ngc5813 ). The galaxy hosts a central, triple component 9 kpc in extent. The 1.4 GHz image from the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-Centimeters (FIRST) resolves this component into a point source and two faint extended structures (inset). At larger distances from the center, the 235 MHz image reveals a pair of lobes located along the same axis of the central component.

sources.summary: The paper reports no Chandra or XMM-Newton spectral improvement from varying parameters for NGC 5813, no short-term variability, and modest UVW1 variation.

quotes.quote: NGC 5813 : For Chandra data we make the simultaneous analysis up to 4 keV because of the low count rate at harder energies. For both Chandra and XMM -Newton data SMF0 is used, with no improvement of the fit when varying the parameters in a period of six and four years, respectively. Note that ^2_r 2 in XMM -Newton data. Unfortunately, none of the proposed models are good enough to improve the final fit. The data from the two instruments are not compared since the annular region contributes with 100 in Chandra data.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 5813 as a core galaxy and KDC whose MUSE kinematics can be modeled with counter-rotating short-axis tube orbits, showing counter-rotation is not limited to discs.

quotes.quote: We also highlight the case of the core galaxy NGC 5813, the first galaxy that was recognised as having a KDC 1980MNRAS.193..931E,1982MNRAS.201..975E . Recent high-quality MUSE data also showed a 2 feature in their velocity dispersion map 2015MNRAS.452....2K . This galaxy is unusual because it apparently does not have two counter rotating discs, but the MUSE data can be reproduced with a dynamical model constructed of two counter-rotating short axis tube orbital families. Strictly speaking, NGC 5813 could be included in Table  t:stat as the only core 2 , but we prefer to keep it as a KDC, reserving the 2 class for galaxies that are made of counter-rotating discs. Nevertheless, NGC 5813 is an important case because it shows that counter-rotation does not need to be solely associated with discs, but likely comes in a spectrum of possible orbital structures.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 5813 as an elliptical with a decoupled core, dust lane, extended filamentary ionized gas resembling an outflow, and H-alpha filaments linked to radio and cool gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 5813 (see Fig. Figure_AB9 ): The most notable feature of this elliptical is that it hosts a kinematically decoupled core Kormendy1984,Krajnovic2015 . reported the presence of a dust lane along the major axis of the galaxy, stating that the dust itself may be suggesting the existence of the two reported cores. Our image of the ionised gas reveals that is very extended emission (see Fig. Figure_largescale ) with filamentary structures that resemble an outflow. In previous H observations with the Imager on the SOAR telescope, reported the presence of filaments co-spatial with the radio emission and with cool gas. They also reported that part of the central H emission is anti-correlated with some inner X-rays cavities, although we do find a rough correlation with the soft X-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5813 as an E1-2 Virgo-Libra galaxy with LINER-like emission, filamentary gas, a kinematically decoupled core, and steep central metallicity gradients.

quotes.quote: NGC 5813 is an E1--2 galaxy in the Virgo-Libra Cloud ( M_B= -21.0 ). It is undetected in HI or CO but has an unresolved, weak central radio continuum source and emission-line ratios typical of LINERS (Birkinshaw Davies 1985; Ho, Filippenko, Sargent 1997). The ionized gas exhibits a complex filamentary structure most likely not (yet) in equilibrium (Caon et al. \ 2000; Paper II). As illustrated in Figure 6, NGC 5813 harbours a kinematically decoupled core (see also Efstathiou, Ellis, Carter 1982). While the body of the galaxy appears purely pressure supported, the center rotates rapidly around an axis tilted by 13^ from the photometric minor axis.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5813 with XMM-Newton RGS spectra and finds lower turbulence than NGC 5044, with modest turbulent pressure support and different intracluster-medium behavior.

quotes.quote: We aim to put constraints on the turbulent velocities and the turbulent pressure in the intra-cluster medium of the giant elliptical galaxies NGC 5044 and NGC 5813 using XMM-Newton Reflection Grating Spectrometer (RGS) observations.

We find that the (I_ 17.05 + I_ 17.10 )/I_ 15.01 line ratio in NGC 5813 is significantly higher than in NGC 5044 . This difference can be explained by a higher level of turbulence in NGC 5044. The best estimates for the turbulent velocities using resonant scattering and upper limits from the line widths, are 320 < V_ turb < 720 km s ^ 1 for NGC 5044 and 140 < V_ turb < 540 km s ^ 1 for NGC 5813 at the 90 confidence limit.
The turbulent pressure support in NGC 5813 is more modest at 15--45 .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 5813 has unusable diffuse X-ray emission for AGN properties, possible Compton-thick indicators, and MIR-X-ray limits that do not resolve the scenario.

quotes.quote: NGC 5813 analysed all archival and data of several epochs for the uncertain LINER AGN in NGC 5813. Bright diffuse X-ray emission renders the data unusable to determine the AGN properties. The data do not show any long-term variation, and a simultaneous fit provides = 39.07 . identify NGC 5813 as Compton-thick candidate based on the low X-ray-- ratio and high upper limit on the equivalent width of an Fe K line. As already mentioned in , the upper limit on the intrinsic X-ray luminosity through the MIR--X-ray correlation of 41.27 does not help to distinguish the different scenarios for NGC 5813.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 5813, the largest galaxy in its subgroup, resembles NGC 5846 in velocity dispersion and entropy, tracking precipitation rather than homogeneous flow.

quotes.quote: NGC 5813 The largest galaxy in its subgroup of the NGC 5846 group, NGC 5813 has a velocity dispersion nearly identical to NGC 5846 and a nearly identical entropy profile, with blue symbols representing Werner+2014MNRAS.439.2291W data and a blue dashed line representing Randall_N5813_2015ApJ...805..112R data. It tracks the precipitation limit from 1 to 10 kpc and is inconsistent with steady homogeneous flow. Beyond 10 kpc, its entropy profile steepens to a slope similar to the K ^ 1.1 slope produced by cosmological structure formation, but its entropy normalization at 20 kpc is greater by a factor of 9 , corresponding to a CGM pressure normalization of 3 of the cosmological expectation.

Kinetic feedback with a time-averaged power of 10^ 41.5 erg s^ 1 is necessary (see sec-TheValve ) and is consistent with the level of central entropy flattening observed in NGC 5846, NGC 5813, and NGC 5044.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 5813's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 5813 is a passive elliptical galaxy at a distance of D= 29.7 1.8 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with evidence for past AGN outbursts randall_shocks_2011 . It possibly still contains a LINER nucleus ho_search_1997-1 . However, indications for a nuclear X-ray compact source are inconclusive gonzalez-martin_x-ray_2009,gonzalez-martin_fitting_2009,randall_shocks_2011 .

sources.summary: The paper uses a multicomponent fit for NGC 5813, links its outer component to elliptical outer isophotes, and finds a mass deficit just below its black hole mass.

quotes.quote: In the end we found that approximately one fourth of our sample galaxies were best fit using multiple components: NGC 1399, NGC 1407, NGC 4552, NGC 5516, NGC 5813, and NGC 6086.

In at least two cases -- NGC 5813 and NGC 6086 -- we can plausibly associate the outer \ component with outer isophotes which are significantly more elliptical than the inner part of the galaxy (where the \ component dominates the light).
In three galaxies (IC 1459, NGC 3842 and NGC 5813), the mass deficit is just below the black hole mass.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 5813's inner ellipticity disagrees with prior luminosity-weighted values and that its deconvolved ellipticity profile declines inward, with the gradient considered real.

quotes.quote: However, for NGC 4382 and NGC 5813, the inner ellipticity at the core-S ersic break radius disagrees with the Lauer et al. \ (2005) average luminosity weighted ellipticities inside the Nuker break radius. These two galaxies have PSF-deconvolved ellipticity profiles which steadily decrease from 0.5 at R_ b to 0.1 at the galaxies' innermost regions ( R 0 .02 ). While this might be due to the PSF, Rest et al. \ (2001) and Lauer et al. \ (1998; 2005) argued that the deconvolution process can robustly remove the effect of the PSF. These gradients are therefore thought to be real.

sources.summary: The paper identifies a well-known kinematically decoupled component in NGC 5813, negligible large-radius rotation, weak minor-axis rotation evidence, and an unusual ring-like dispersion depression.

quotes.quote: A galaxy with a well-known KDC Ef80, Ef82, Kor84, BSG94 evident in the \ velocity map (see Paper II). The galaxy has negligible rotation at larger radii, and shows weak evidence for minor-axis rotation. The dispersion field also shows an unusual ring-like depression at about 5 \ from the center.

gmw_00000240.name: NGC 5866
gmw_00000240.overview: NGC 5866 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1912.07501, arXiv:1310.2770] featuring a narrow dust lane [arXiv:1912.07501, arXiv:1310.2770] and a stellar halo containing accreted light from a dissolving satellite [arXiv:1912.07501]. It hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1908.08421] with a borderline LINER/H II nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] and a compact radio core [arXiv:astro-ph/0401593, arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The paper finds no X-ray nucleus in NGC 5866, detects soft extended emission, and argues any hidden nucleus would need to be strongly obscured.

quotes.quote: Source Detection An X-ray nucleus is seen in all the galaxies except for NGC 4550 and NGC 5866. NGC 5866 has soft extended emission 0.5^ (2 kpc) in diameter and no X-ray nucleus is detected (Table 2).

Only an upper limit to the X-ray flux is obtained for NGC 5866. If an X-ray nucleus is present in this galaxy and its luminosity is only slightly below the upper limit, this source could be an AGN obscured by a column density 10^ 23 or larger. If the apparent X-ray luminosity of the nucleus of NGC 5866 is much lower than the observed upper limit, and the intrinsic X-ray luminosity conforms to the typical / ratio for LLAGN ( / 1-2 ), then the X-ray source must be almost completely obscured.

NGC 5866 (T2) . --- Extended emission of diameter 30 ^ ( 2 kpc) is seen. The spectrum of this emission may be represented by a MEKAL plasma model with kT 1 keV and abundance of 0.15 solar. This component could be identified with a gaseous halo of this S0 galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5866 as an early-type galaxy example whose outskirts show accreted halo light from a dissolving satellite on a nearly radial orbit.

quotes.quote: A ``poster child'' is the spindle galaxy NGC 5866. This galaxy is at 13.4 Mpc distance and has a R_ 25 = 10 kpc. The morphology of the high surface brightness regions of this galaxy (see Fig. fig1 ) is SA0 with a narrow dust lane seen edge-on and forming an angle with the photometric major axis. The kinematics of this galaxy is that of a regular disk rotator ( ).

The deep image of NGC 5866 shows that something different is happening in its outskirts. fig1 at low surface brightness the galaxy has extra light in the halo from a dissolving satellite on an extended nearly-radial orbit, with several pericenter passages. ETGs like NGC 5866 are ``cosmological'' in the sense that they have accreted components that form their halos.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC5866 as methanol-enhanced relative to other tracers, possibly shock-driven, with a barred molecular-gas interpretation and unusually cool methanol rotation temperatures.

quotes.quote: NGC5866 has a higher ratio of methanol emission with respect to all the other tracers studied here, and thus we suggest that shocks could be important in this object. This object has a classical bulge, but molecular gas kinematics reveal it may well be barred 2013MNRAS.429..534D .

In NGC5866 the trend is reversed, with the methanol transitions suggesting a rotation temperature of 2.6 K, half the CO rotation temperatures of 5.5 K (formally rotation temperatures lower than the cosmic microwave background temperature are forbidden, suggesting that the real distribution/extent of the methanol emitting component in NGC5866 is very different from that assumed). Methanol which has been liberated from grains by star-formation is usually present in warm star-forming cores, while methanol released by shocks is generally found in the fast cooling post-shock ISM e.g. 2001A&A...370.1017V . NGC5866 was identified as an object that may have an enhanced methanol abundance due to shocks in Section gasprop . The methanol emission has much cooler methanol rotation temperatures, as expected from the post-shock cooled ISM.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5866 as an S0 whose mid-infrared emission lies in an edge-on dust lane, with [CII], [OI], CO, low [CII]/CO, and low far-infrared-to-blue luminosity.

quotes.quote: In NGC 5866, the 7 and 15 emission is concentrated in the edge-on dust lane. All the galaxies are detected in the [ CII ] (158 ) line, and the S0s NGC 1155 and NGC 5866 are detected in the [ OI ] (63 ) line as well. Previous detections of neutral ISM are sparse: only NGC 1052 had been detected in \ and NGC 5866 in CO.

Three of four early type galaxies, namely NGC 1052, NGC 6958 and NGC 5866, have low ratios of far-infrared to blue luminosity and show a lower , which is explained by postulating a softer radiation field from old stellar populations in early type galaxies, compared to spirals and irregulars, where young stars are present. While optical photons are effective in heating the dust, UV radiation is needed to heat the gas by the grain photoelectric mechanism. The low [ CII ] /CO ratio in NGC 5866 ( rat 570 ) confirms this scenario.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5866 as an edge-on S0 with a classical bulge, separated photometrically and kinematically from its disk, with disk stars appearing younger than the bulge.

quotes.quote: NGC 5866 is an edge-on S0 galaxy that has been studied in some of the most recent surveys of galaxies (Filho, Barthel Ho 2002; McMahon et al. 2002; Terlevich Forbes 2002; Jarrett et al. 2003). NGC 5866 shows a large bulge and an edge-on disk with a prominent dust lane (Fig. Fig:N5866 ). The multi-slit analysis of its stellar kinematics shows a smoothly rising, featureless rotation curve (Fisher 1997), and the ionized-gas behaves like its stellar counterpart (Fisher 1997).

The power of the \ observations to obtain simultaneously 2D spatial and spectroscopic information allows us to show that the bulge and disk in NGC 5866 are separated both photometrically and kinematically. As expected in classical bulges, the disk is a flattened, rapidly rotating structure, while the bulge is a hotter, rounder and slower rotating component. The bulge does not rotate cylindrically, unlike the case of NGC 7332 (see below), but as normal spheroids.

sources.summary: NGC 5866 shows elongated 347-GHz emission linked to its host dust lane and a definitely steep nuclear spectrum, possibly connected to its transition-type nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, in the JCMT measurements at 347 GHz, we found significant elongated features in NGC 4258 (Doi et al. in preparation), NGC 4579, and NGC 5866, and a very diffuse structure in NGC 3147 (Fig. figure2 ). These features are consistent with the morphology of CO emissions that had been imaged with millimetric interferometers (NGC 4258, Helfer_etal.2003 ; NGC 4579, Sofue_etal.2003 ), with dust lane along the major axis of the host galaxy (NGC 5866, e.g., Malhotra_etal.2000 ), or with morphology of a low-frequency radio image (NGC 3147, Hummel_etal.1985 ). It is, therefore, plausible that most of the 347-GHz emission from these sources originates in stellar processes of star-forming regions, probably dust emission, associated with their host galaxies.

NGC 5866 show a definitely steep spectrum, in spite of a low core power. Its nuclear activity type, `transition', might be related to the jet domination; another transition object, NGC 4552, also shows a definitely steep spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5866 has a prominent bulge and dust lane but uncertain diffuse emission, likely affected by continuum-subtraction artifacts rather than clear outflows.

quotes.quote: The R band image of NGC 5866 (Figure f:ngc5866 ) 22 shows a prominent bulge and a dust lane. Unlike other galaxies in our sample, no clumpy H ii regions are seen in . The diffuse emission is partially obscured by the prominent dust lane and is asymmetric with respect to the plane of the disk. The emission is also partially obscured by the dust lane, and does not display any clumpy regions. It is not clear whether the diffuse emission seen on either side of the dust lane in is a real feature and whether this contains evidence for any outflows.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 5866 as one of four lenticular or early-type spiral galaxies with detailed kinematics, finding its bulge-plus-disc dynamics fit without requiring an inner dark halo.

quotes.quote: The model is successfully applied to reproduce the stellar kinematic and photometry of the bulge of the 4 lenticular and early-type spiral galaxies NGC 4565, NGC 7814, NGC 5866 and NGC 4594. For these galaxies detailed stellar kinematical data are available at different positions across the galaxy. The application of our models shows that: 1) For all the galaxies considered in this work, an isotropic model is able to reproduce the whole dynamical data.

2) We do not need a dark mass halo to reproduce these data. This do not mean that a dark halo is not present, but just that its dynamical effects in these inner regions of the galaxies are negligible.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5866 as a comparison S0 galaxy with anomalously red 70/24 micron color, diffuse dust heating, low-level star formation, and PAH features.

quotes.quote: We identify just one galaxy, the S0 galaxy NGC 5866, with the same anomalous red colour as our proposed 70 m-excess population. draine07 identified NGC 5866 as a discrepant galaxy among their sample of 17 SINGS galaxies (mostly spirals) with SCUBA sub-mm photometry, due to its red f_ 70 /f_ 24 colour, and in fitting a dust model to the infrared SED, required all of the dust to be exposed to a single diffuse interstellar radiation field, without any emission from dust heated by star-formation. li do however find evidence for low-level star-formation ( SFR 0.05 M _ yr ^ 1 ) based on the detection of extended Paschen- and H emission, while Spitzer /IRS spectroscopy reveals clear PAH features at 7.7, 11.3 and 12.7 m due to star formation shapiro .

Such a low C value produces dust temperatures and far-infrared SEDs similar to those produced by a radiation field of intensity 1--1 0 the local interstellar radiation field (ISRF), similar to that modelled for NGC 5866 draine07 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5866 as a possible AGN with contradictory nuclear X-ray detections and a Compton-thick classification still compatible with MIR-X-ray limits.

quotes.quote: NGC 5866 While do not detect any nuclear source with in NGC 5866, possibly harbouring an AGN, claim the faint detection of such a source from the same data. Although too faint for spectral analysis, the estimate the observed X-ray luminosity to be = 38.42 . also detect a nuclear source, provide a similar observed luminosity, but classify NGC 5866 as non-AGN from the X-ray point of view. On the other hand, classify it as a Compton-thick AGN candidate based on the low X-ray-- ratio. The MIR--X-ray puts an upper limit on the intrinsic X-ray emission of any AGN in NGC 5866 to = 40.74 , which is compatible with all the above scenarios.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 5866 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: NGC 5866 is an SA0 galaxy belonging to a loose group 2006MNRAS.369..360S . It has a narrow, clear-cut dark lane, running along the disk at an angle with respect to the photometric major axis. It is a regular, disk-like rotator 2011MNRAS.414.2923K with a maximum amplitude of 150-180 2016MNRAS.457..147F at 2 , beyond which the rotation decreases to 110 at 3 . Our velocity map shows that the galaxy keeps rotating at approximately constant speed ( 85 ) at large radii (up to 9 ), with a progressive twist of the kinematic position angle.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5866 among VLBA-detected flat-spectrum radio-core sources, with excess radio emission consistent with a jet model.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, all VLBA detected sample sources (including NGC 4552; Nagar 2002 and NGC 5866; Falcke 2000) show flat spectrum radio cores, although the inverted spectrum in NGC 524 is most probably an effect of resolution mismatch. The origin of the mas-scale radio emission is unlikely to be free-free emission. The detected radio cores show brightness temperatures several orders of magnitude above the thermal limit (10 ^4  K; Condon 1992).
The deviation of the sources NGC 4552, NGC 5846 and NGC 5866 is in the direction of excess radio emission, which is entirely consistent with the jet model.

sources.summary: The paper reports a central hard X-ray source in NGC 5866 with radio loudness, making it a possible LLAGN that dominates the central X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 5866 (T2, S0) has a central, hard X-ray source and one other point source far away from the center and embedded in soft diffuse emission. The spectrum of the central source did not have enough counts to attempt a fit. However its luminosity was estimated to be L_ 0.5-10 keV =3.2 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 with 98 of it emitted in the hard band. This is also the luminosity from the central 2.5'' region indicating that this source is the main contributor to the central X-ray luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 5866 as having especially diverse molecular line profiles, with double-peaked and centrally peaked tracers implying different molecular gas properties in its center and disk.

quotes.quote: Out of all the galaxies, NGC 5866 has the most interesting diversity of line profiles. Despite an asymmetry likely due to pointing in both lines, it is clearly double-peaked in both lines, and , while both lines show a clear single central peak. In this galaxy, the molecular gas must have different properties in the centre and disc.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC5866 has broader lines, unusually high H2 volume density, and a double-peaked line profile that makes predicted CO SLEDs more uncertain.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies (NGC3665, NGC4526 and NGC5866) have broader lines (see Fig. fig:0 ) and for those the backend configuration used two overlapping sub-bands, each 1 GHz wide and covering a total bandwidth of 1.9 GHz with 977 kHz-wide channels.

The best-fit H _ 2 volume densities do not vary by more than a factor of 10 between our sources ( n (H _ 2 ) =10^ 3-4 cm ^ 3 ), except for NGC1222 and NGC5866 that have n (H _ 2 ) =10^ 6.5 and 10^ 7 cm ^ 3 , respectively.

We know that for galaxies such as NGC3032, NGC3607, NGC3665, NGC4459, NGC4526, NGC5866 and NGC6014, a double-peaked line profile is more realistic, so for these sources the SLEDs shown in Figure fig:9 are more uncertain. figure 1 figure 1 figure 1 figure 1 figure 1 Correlations with global parameters In Paper XI, ratios of molecular lines discussed in this paper were shown to correlate with some global physical parameters of the sample galaxies (e.g.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 5866's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 5866 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy at a distance of D= 14.1 2.7 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a borderline LINER/H II nucleus ho_search_1997-1 . The central bulge and nucleus are crossed by a prominent narrow dust lane. The X-ray evidence for an AGN remains inconclusive gonzalez-martin_x-ray_2006,gonzalez-martin_x-ray_2009,flohic_central_2006 , while a compact core was detected at radio wavelengths nagar_radio_2000,falcke_radio_2000 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5866 among four modeled LLAGNs, assigning it a black-hole mass near 10^8 solar masses and a low accretion rate; its cascade gamma-ray flux is predicted too faint for CTA.

quotes.quote: The four LLAGNs differ in M_ BH and m . NGC 3516, NGC 4203, and NGC 5866 have M_ BH close to 10^8 , while NGC 3998 hosts a SMBH of M_ BH 10^9 . m is close to the critical accretion rate for NGC 3516, m 0.1 m_ crit for NGC 4203 and NGC 3998, and m 0.01 m_ crit for NGC 5866.

For a lower m object that has a higher value of _ , such as NGC 5866, the cascade flux is too low to be detected by CTA. Therefore, it would be challenging to detect the cascade gamma rays with current and near-future instruments, except for Sgr A*.

sources.summary: The paper marks NGC 5866 as an outlier in multiple central intensity ratio correlations, suggesting its nearly edge-on morphology may affect CIR measurements.

quotes.quote: The outliers in this correlation are NGC 0404 (marked 1 in Figure fig:figure1 (a)), NGC 3377 (2), NGC 4150 (3), NGC 5128 (4) and NGC 5866 (5).
The nearly complete edge-on inclination of the galaxy NGC 5866 2009ApJ...706..693L might have affected the measurement of central intensity ratio.
In this case also NGC 5866 (marked 5 in Figure fig:figure1 (b)) and NGC 5128 (marked 4 in Figure fig:figure1 (b)) are showing deviation from the main trend.

gmw_00000241.name: NGC 5953
gmw_00000241.overview: NGC 5953 is an interacting lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] classified as a Seyfert 2 or LINER [arXiv:0911.3772, arXiv:1310.2770] that is closely interacting with NGC 5954 [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161, arXiv:1310.2770]. It features a rotating molecular gas disk [arXiv:0911.3772] and a circumnuclear star-forming ring [arXiv:0704.2839] surrounding its active nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 5953 molecular gas, finds a rotating CO disk with an off-nuclear peak, identifies a circumnuclear stellar ring, and argues current torques do not fuel the nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present ^ 12 CO(1--0) and ^ 12 CO(2--1) maps of the interacting Seyfert 2/LINER galaxy \ obtained with the IRAM interferometer at resolutions of 2 1 1 4 and 1 1 0 7, respectively. We also present single-dish IRAM 30 m observations of the central region of \ for the ^ 12 CO(1--0), ^ 12 CO(2--1), and HCN(1--0) transitions at resolutions of 22 , 12 , and 29^ , respectively. The CO emission is distributed over a disk of diameter 16 ( 2.2 kpc), within which are several, randomly distributed peaks. The strongest peak does not coincide with the nucleus, but is instead offset from the center, 2-3 ^ ( 340 pc) toward the west/southwest. The kinematics of the molecular component are quite regular, as is typical of a rotating disk.

Both sets of residuals show a circumnuclear stellar ring with a radius of 5-7 , approximately coincident in size with the ^ 12 CO disk. The presence of the ring in both sets of residuals suggests that it is not the typical ``doughnut'' associated with the diffraction limit and IRAC PRF incompatibilities, but rather is a real feature. Using NIR images we found that gravity torques acting on the gas are predominantly positive in the region 100--400 pc in both ^ 12 CO(1--0) and ^ 12 CO(2--1), indicating that the gas is not fueling the central 100 pc region, down to the effective spatial resolution of our observations.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5953 as a compact Seyfert 2 whose MIR spectrum matches NGC 701, implying an extinguished nuclear continuum and stellar-origin X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: In the case of NGC 5953 , the galaxy is compact (4 pixels FWHM) with an approximately gaussian flux distribution and no well defined point-source. In this case, no nuclear flux can be derived.

n701_n5953 the spectrum of the typical Sf2 spectrum of NGC 5953 on top of the spectrum of the normal SB galaxy NGC 701 (scaled by a factor 0.5). The two spectra are virtually indistinguishable. Moreover, the 7.7 m PAH EW in NGC 701, 5.88 0.05 m as well as the luminosity, L_ 7.7 = 42.048 0.009 erg s^ 1 , are well within the range spanned by Sf2s. This confirms that the PAH emission is not related to the activity in the nucleus. Note also that the continuum in the two objects matches perfectly. This further implies that, at least in NGC 5953, the nuclear continuum is completely extinguished and the faint residual we observe originates from outside the active nucleus.

sources.summary: NGC 5953 is presented as an interacting Seyfert 2 with large-scale rotation, a central kinematically decoupled component, elongated gas toward NGC 5954, and a star-forming ring around an active nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 5953 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy closely interacting with NGC 5954 res93,gp96 . The \ stellar kinematics displays large-scale rotation and a kinematically decoupled component in the central 10 . The \ ionised gas presents an elongated distribution towards NGC 5954 (i.e. NE of NGC 5953 centre), which is associated with radio emission jenkins84 , whereas the \ distribution is symmetric around the galactic centre and it is consistent with a dust ring seen in the unsharp-masked image. The ionised-gas kinematics of the central component follows that of the stars in the inner parts, and it is in good agreement with that of hernandez03 from Fabry-Perot observations of the \ emission-line. The / \ ratio map exhibits very low values in the location of the circumnuclear region and it is enhanced in the very centre of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5953 as an interacting peculiar galaxy with a Seyfert 2 nucleus, surrounding star formation, and aperture-dependent transition spectra.

quotes.quote: NGC 5953 is a peculiar S0 (Rampazzo et al. 1995) or Sa (Delgado Perez 1996) galaxy interacting with NGC 5954 (Arp 1966). It has a Seyfert 2 nucleus (Rafanelli et al. 1990; Delgado Perez) surrounded by a ring of star formation with a radius of 4 \ (Delgado Perez). Rafanelli et al. and Delgado Perez studied this object using a slit width of 2 0 and 1 5 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5953 as an interacting LINER/Seyfert 2 galaxy whose decoupled core, ionized-gas motions, and absorption-line maps reveal a star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 5953 is a Liner Seyfert 2 galaxy (NED) closely interacting with NGC 5954 (Reshetnikov 1993, Gonz alez-Delgado P erez 1996). This interactions manifests itself in a kinematically decoupled stellar core, and ionised gas following the motions of this KDC, but rotating perpendicular to the stars in the outer regions of the SAURON field. The galaxy shows a ring of low / \ (Paper VII, Yoshida et al. 1993). The stellar absorption line maps confirm that this is a star formation ring, with low \ and \ values and high .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 5953's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 5953 is a lenticular galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0066 ( D 31.4 Mpc) which heavily interacts with NGC 5954 to the north-east at a projected distance of D= 43 ( 6 kpc). BOth galaxies form the pair Arp 91 (see casasola_molecular_2010 for a recent detailed study). NGC 5953 hosts an AGN optically classified either as a Sy 2 gonzalez_delgado_circumnuclear_1996,goncalves_agns_1999 , LINER veilleux_optical_1995,kim_optical_1995 , or AGN/starburst composite veron_agns_1997 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5953 as an ongoing-interaction case with fresh gas and inner kinematical decoupling, possibly showing early formation of a decoupled component.

quotes.quote: In Figure 2, we present the case of NGC 5953, which offers a unique opportunity to study the effects that an on-going interaction has on the stellar and gas properties. The kinematical decoupling in the inner parts, together with the presence of large amounts of fresh gas, suggests that we might be witnessing the early stages in the formation of a kinematically decoupled component.
For the remaining three galaxies with rings (NGC 2844, NGC 5953, NGC 7742), the interacting scenario offers an alternative explanation (see Figure 2).

gmw_00000242.name: NGC 5995
gmw_00000242.overview: NGC 5995 is an inclined barred spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0252 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert 1.9 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] that has been detected as a compact radio source [arXiv:1310.2770] and a weak mid-infrared source [arXiv:1206.6620, arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 5995 as the highest-redshift sample galaxy, where VISIR filter placement makes PAH detection difficult; nearly equal filter fluxes imply a non-detection and upper-limit PAH measurements.

quotes.quote: Finally we discuss the case of the NGC 5995, the galaxy with the highest redshift in our sample. This galaxy is well beyond the threshold where the reference filter has more of the PAH band than the original PAH filter, which in the case of VISIR happens at z = 0.019 . A positive detection would therefore be characterised by f_ PAH f_ REF 0.015 Jy. However the photometric measurements show practically no difference between the fluxes in both filters, thus placing NGC 5995 in a region which would imply a negative PAH flux, or a more likely misrepresentation of the underlying continuum. We classify such cases as non-detections and report PAH fluxes and derived quantities as upper limits, based on the photometric uncertainty.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5995 as a weak MIDI AGN example, fitting its data with unresolved-plus-Gaussian visibility components and finding scatter consistent with the derived errors.

quotes.quote: This treatment of errors gives consistent results for sources as weak as 150 mJy, e.g. \ for the MIDI AGN Large Programme source NGC 5995 (Fig. fig:radial ). For this source we fitted the data to a simple one-dimensional visibility model of an unresolved point source and a resolved source of a Gaussian intensity profile (similar to a one-component power-law intensity profile). The spread of the data about this model is compatible with the error bars derived using the error calculation presented above, i.e. \ the ^2 is approximately equal to the number of degrees of freedom.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 5995's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 5995 is an inclined barred spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0252 ( D 117 Mpc) with a Sy 1.9 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , which was detected as a compact radio source thean_high-resolution_2000 . After its detection in , this object was followed up with Palomar 5 m/MIRLIN gorjian_10_2004 and ESO 3.6 m/TIMMI2 galliano_mid-infrared_2005 . An unresolved nucleus at arcsecond resolution was detected in both data sets.

gmw_00000243.name: NGC 6221
gmw_00000243.overview: NGC 6221 is a barred spiral galaxy located within a small galaxy group [arXiv:astro-ph/0211453, arXiv:astro-ph/0012035]. It exhibits a peculiar morphology and asymmetric arms due to interactions with its neighbor NGC 6215 [arXiv:astro-ph/0501355] and hosts a composite active nucleus featuring both a weak Seyfert 2 engine and intense starburst activity [arXiv:1501.02022, arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The paper portrays NGC 6221 as a barred Sbc Seyfert in a group with circumnuclear star formation, extracted nuclear/off-nuclear spectra, velocity offsets, and fairly homogeneous bulge spectra.

quotes.quote: NGC 6221 is located within a small galaxy group. The host galaxy is a barred Sbc with a weak Seyfert nucleus. The radio morphology and spectral index of the nucleus and bar are indicative of the presence of supernovae remnants (Forbes Norris, 1998). This galaxy is known to exhibit clear signs of intense circumnuclear star formation. The optical spectrum of the central 6 arcsec shown in Phillips (1979), exhibits signatures of emission originating from HII regions together with a weak Seyfert nucleus, whereas the underlying continuum is apparently dominated by late type stars.

In addition to the HII region mentioned above, three 1-D spectra were extracted: the nucleus and two regions at 1.3 and 3 arcsec south respectively from the centre (Fig. 6). The stellar features from the regions are blueshifted by 34 3 km s ^ 1 and 68 4 km s ^ 1 with respect to the nucleus, in agreement with the kinematics of the stellar component as published by Vega-Beltr an et al. (1998). From EWs in Table 4 we infer up to 30 of dilution.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6221 as a peculiar barred spiral interacting with NGC 6215, with offset star formation, Mg absorption changes, and AGN-related nuclear dilution.

quotes.quote: NGC 6221 - SB(s)bc pec This galaxy exhibits peculiar morphology and asymmetric arms due to interaction with its neighbour NGC 6215 and several smaller dwarf galaxies KD04 . A thick bar is evident and most of the current star formation is offset from the nucleus. Cid Fernandes et al. (1998) measured the Mg I+Mg H EW out to a radius of 12 arcsec in NGC 6221.
