Their EW peaked at about 5 arcsec before becoming diluted by about 50 toward the nucleus (see their fig. 43). Our profile peaks at a similar radius, but shows only moderate diminishment towards the nucleus.

The cause of the dilution of this absorption feature is likely to be the presence of an active galactic nucleus (AGN). NGC 6221, NGC 6814 and NGC 7213 are all classified as Seyfert galaxies and therefore produce non-thermal continuum radiation and broad emission lines in the nucleus. The superposition of an AGN continuum on top of the stellar spectrum can act to decrease the measured strength of the absorption features in the galaxy's core.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6221 as a barred spiral interacting system, paired with NGC 6215, whose optical X-shooter spectrum requires intermediate and BLR H components.

quotes.quote: NGC 6221 is a nearby, spiral galaxy classified as SBc(s) by . The bar is clearly visible in the optical and in the infrared and lies at a PA of 118 ^ with a length of 6 kpc. NGC 6221 forms an apparent physical pair with the spiral galaxy NGC 6215, which is 110 kpc distant, and is also possibly interacting with two nearby galaxies koribalski04 .

The optical X-shooter spectrum of NGC 6221 is quite complex. Beside the NLR components both in the H and H regions, three intermediate components having a blueshift in the range 20--380 \ have been fitted (see Figure fig:NGC6221 ). It was also necessary to add a BLR H component having FWHM = 1630 . The F test gives a probability of 1 10 ^ 256 that the improvement of the fit is due to statistical fluctuations.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 6221 as a starburst-obscured AGN whose optical starburst hides Seyfert-like X-ray variability, Fe K properties, and broad-line-region signatures.

quotes.quote: To account for the optical and X-ray observations of NGC 6221, we suggest a scenario in which the gas and dust associated with the starburst is the material that obscures the AGN. The extent of the starburst-like optical emission line ratios indicates that the physical scale of the starburst is a few hundred parsecs. This size is sufficient to block not only the broad-line region, but also the narrow-line region of the AGN. This is unlike the putative obscuring ``torus'' that distinguishes type 1 and type 2 Seyfert galaxies because the latter hides only the broad-line region (Figure fig:cartoon ).

The total obscuring column density to the AGN measured at X-ray energies is only 10^ 22 . The Seyfert 1-like characteristics of NGC 6221, such as variability and Fe K line properties, are therefore not surprising. Without the obscuring starburst, we would have a direct view of the AGN (Figure fig:cartoon , viewpoint A).

sources.summary: NGC 6221 contains a weak Seyfert 2 nucleus embedded in a bright starburst, with X-ray-loud composite behavior and many H II complexes.

quotes.quote: NGC 6221: This galaxy, imaged in H by Ryder93 and in the optical and IR at high resolution by Levenson01 and also studied spectroscopically by Gu06 contains a very weak Seyfert 2 nucleus (detected by the broader \ line) embedded in a bright starburst nucleus. This galaxy is an X-ray-loud composite galaxy, with very weak AGN activity seen at optical or IR wavelengths , but displaying strong X-ray emission. Obscuration by a column of N_ H = 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 is sufficient to explain these properties Levenson01 . Many \ region complexes are scattered across the WiFeS field of view.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 6221's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 6221 is a barred late-type spiral galaxy at a low Galactic latitude at a distance of D= 10.7 4.3 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an active nucleus containing a clumpy starburst and a Sy 2 nucleus (see levenson_obscuring_2001 for a detailed study). This results in a AGN/starburst composite classification (e.g. veron_agns_1997 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6221 as an unusual barred group galaxy whose X-ray properties resemble a Seyfert 1, with low hard-component absorption and secure X-ray identification.

quotes.quote: NGC 6221 is unusual in that it contains a broad line with EW 400 eV.
The absorption of the hard component in NGC 6221 is only 10^ 22 cm^ 2 , which is much lower than in any other members of the sample.
NGC 6221 NGC 6221 contains a bar, is in a group, and its peculiar morphology may be due to interaction with NGC 6215 Pen84 .

sources.summary: NGC 6221 is highlighted as the atypical composite: only slightly absorbed, Seyfert 1-like in X-rays, and unusual among heavily absorbed AGN in the sample.

quotes.quote: The central source of NGC 6221 is only slightly absorbed.
NGC 6221 is unique in that it is the only galaxy that has a truly Seyfert 1-like X-ray spectrum, with a broadened Fe K line that suggests origin in an accretion disk Nan97 .
In contrast, the Sy2/SBs tend to have higher directly measured or inferred column densities, with N_H < 10^ 23 only in the unusual case of NGC 6221.

gmw_00000244.name: NGC 6240
gmw_00000244.overview: NGC 6240 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0607468, arXiv:1508.05065] classified optically as a LINER [arXiv:0909.4082, arXiv:1101.0682] and characterized by intense starburst activity [arXiv:1108.1747, arXiv:1303.2980]. The system features a complex multi-nucleus structure [arXiv:1910.12813] hosting heavily obscured active galactic nuclei [arXiv:1508.05065, arXiv:2001.06293] and driving massive molecular outflows [arXiv:1812.04316, arXiv:2402.17851].

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 6240's pan-spectral starburst SED, deriving a high star-formation rate while noting its obscured AGN, older stellar contribution, and post-merger status.

quotes.quote: With these assumptions, we show in figure fig:NGC6240 the fit we obtain for the galaxy NGC 6240. As can be seen, with five free parameters we obtain an excellent fit to the observations over nearly 3.5 decades of frequency. In this figure, the data have been scaled to a star formation rate of 1.0 \ in order to make this SED comparable to the others in this paper.

NGC 6240 has long been implicated with an active nucleus, and shows both a strong non-thermal radio excess, extended radio emission Gallimore04 , and X-ray emission associated with a highly dust-obscured AGN eg Ikebe00,Risaliti00, Kewley00 . Recently Armus06 have directly detected the active nucleus via the [ Ne 5 ] 14.3 \ line using the IRS on the Spitzer Space Telescope . From this, they estimate that the AGN has a flux of 3-5 of the bolometric luminosity. At such low levels, it would account for the slight excess in the flux in the vicinity of the 6-14 \ PAH features seen in the observations when compared to our model, but is not sufficient to compromise the rest of the fit. NGC 6240 has an unusually strong contribution from older stars, suggesting either that the starburst is rather old in this object, or that the current starburst is the second episode in this galaxy, or that there is an important contribution of stars older than 10^8 yrs.

sources.summary: The paper uses Herschel SPIRE spectroscopy of NGC 6240 to argue that shocks, rather than UV or X-ray irradiation, dominate excitation of its CO-emitting gas.

quotes.quote: We present Herschel SPIRE FTS spectroscopy of the nearby luminous infrared galaxy NGC 6240. In total 20 lines are detected, including CO J=4-3 through J=13-12 , 6 H _2 O rotational lines, and [ ] and [ ] fine-structure lines. The CO to continuum luminosity ratio is 10 times higher in NGC 6240 than Mrk 231. Although the CO ladders of NGC 6240 and Mrk 231 are very similar, UV and/or X-ray irradiation are unlikely to be responsible for the excitation of the gas in NGC 6240.

The CO ladder is best reproduced by a model with shock velocity v_s=10  km s ^ 1 and a pre-shock density n_ H =5 10^4  cm ^ 3 . We find that the solution best fitting the H _2 lines is degenerate: The shock velocities and number densities range between v_s = 17 - 47  km s ^ 1 and n_ H =10^7 - 5 10^ 4  cm ^ 3 , respectively. The H _2 lines thus need a much more powerful shock than the CO lines. We deduce that most of the gas is currently moderately stirred up by slow (10 km s ^ 1 ) shocks while only a small fraction ( 1 percent) of the ISM is exposed to the high velocity shocks.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6240 as an ultraluminous infrared merger and strong double-AGN case, with both nuclei heavily obscured and the southern nucleus dominating hard X-rays.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 NGC 6240 is an ultra luminous infrared merger system wtih the best case of a double AGN found so far. The latter was discovered in X-rays by observations komossa_discovery_2003 . Both nuclei are heavily obscured and surrounded by star formation, which makes precise luminosity estimates difficult. However, Puccetti et al. (in prep.) utilized the new to analyse the hardest part of the X-ray emission. While can not resolve both nuclei, most of the emission should be associated with the more powerful southern nucleus, NGC 6240S.

The MIR--X-ray correlation predicts = 43.27 0.37 and only mild Compton-thickness for NGC 6240S ( = 23.76 0.44 ). The northern nucleus, NGC 6240N, appears to be fainter in X-rays komossa_discovery_2003 but is presumably highly obscured as well, as indicated by the low X-ray spectral index and infrared spectra risaliti_double_2006 . However, no intrinsic luminosity estimate is available and we simply use the observed luminosity from the unobscured fit of , = 42.07 .

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 6240's inner kiloparsecs, describing a merging infrared galaxy with two AGN, strong H2 emission, complex gas kinematics, shocks, and X-ray-heated molecular gas.

quotes.quote: In this work, we use K-band integral field spectroscopy to map the emission line flux distributions and kinematics and investigate the origin of the molecular and ionized gas line emission from inner 1.4 2.4 kpc ^ 2 of the LIRG NGC 6240, known to be the galaxy with strongest H _2 line emission. The emission lines show complex profiles at locations between both nuclei and surrounding the northern nucleus, while at locations near the southern nucleus and at 1 ^ west of the northern nucleus, they can be reproduced by a single gaussian component. We found that the H _2 emission is originated mainly by thermal processes, possible being dominated by heating of the gas by X-rays from the AGN at locations near both nuclei.

One prototype of merging galaxies is the object NGC 6240, being one of the most studied merger system in all wavelengths ranges e.g. fosbury79,wright84,joseph84,max05,max07,engel10,meijerink13,feruglio13,mori14,hagiwara15,tunnard15 . NGC 6240 is located at a distance of 97 Mpc, has two nuclei separated by 1 5 (corresponding to 700 pc) and each of them hosts an AGN as detected in X-ray wavelengths komossa03 . NGC 6240 presents an infrared luminosity of L _ IR =10^ 11.8 L _ and lies at the limit between LIRGs and ULIRGs sanders96 . It presents the strongest H _2 line emission in the near-IR observd for this kind of objects, with a luminosity L_ H_2 10^9 L_ e.g. joseph84 . Then, NGC 6240 is an ideal object to study the origin of the molecular hydrogen emission in merger systems with AGNs, as the H _2 line emission can be originated both by Starburts and AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6240 as a LIRG merger with unusually strong shocked molecular hydrogen, starburst-like dense-gas ratios, and two known AGNs.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240: NGC 6240 (IRAS 16504+0228, UGC 10592, VV 617, IC 4625) is unusual among the class of LIRGs in having exceptionally strong emission in the near and mid-IR lines of molecular hydrogen. The consensus of the infrared observers is that the strong H _ 2 lines are due to shocked gas, not star formation Rieke1985,Depoy1986,Lester1988,Herbst1990,Elston1990,vanderWerf1993,Mori2014 . Since the cooling time of this shocked gas is short ( 10 ^ 7 yr), we may be seeing NGC 6240 at a privileged moment during the merger process e.g. Sugai1997 . Recent work of Meijerink2013 showed that the \ spectral line energy distribution (SLED), obtained using the Herschel FTS, is consistent with either an X-ray dominated region (XDR) or with shocked molecular gas; however, the lack of the ionic species OH ^ + and H _ 2 O ^ + , normally found in high abundance in gas clouds near elevated X-ray or cosmic ray fluxes, ruled out the XDR models and once again, shocks are the most likely scenario to explain the observations.

We find that the line ratios are significantly different between the two sources. Arp 220 is consistent with AGN-like line ratios while NGC 6240 is consistent with a starburst-like ratio, similar to NGC 4039 and the Antennae Overlap region Schirm2016 . This result is quite surprising considering the fact that NGC 6240 has two well known AGNs e.g. Komossa2003 while the presence of an AGN in Arp 220 is still debatable. The low line ratio found for NGC 6240 may reflect the different area filling factors of the two species as our observations are slightly unresolved with our beam; however, this is not the case for Arp 220 as our observations do resolve the emission and therefore, line ratios within one beam element would be filled with emission from both species.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6240 as a mostly Compton-thick galaxy merger with two nuclei, obscured active material, shock-heated gas, a soft X-ray halo, and CO emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240: Mostly Compton thick galaxy merger. 2016A&A...585A.157P studied the source with Nustar data, and concluded that this source could be an early merger stage galaxy with two nuclei separated, and an intrinsic source luminosity of =7 10^ 43 . Both active and obscured Compton thick material present. 2014ApJ...781...55W detected fast shock heated gas within 5 kpc of the central region. 2013ApJ...765..141N and 2013A&A...549A..51F have studied the Chandra data and detected a soft X-ray halo, and also CO emission lines.

2010ApJ...725.1848T and 2011A&A...529A.106I have studied the source in a sample. 2005ApJ...629..739N have studied observation of this source and found that starburst emission dominates the soft X-ray 0.5-3 energy range. In our study the X-ray spectra required a broad Fe K emission line, along with a soft X-ray emission line at 0.89 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6240 as a nearby ULIRG in an active merger with two Compton-thick nuclei, irregular morphology, X-ray AGN activity, and spectral central-mass estimates used for SMBH coalescence simulations.

quotes.quote: One of the most prominent dual AGN candidates is nearby ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) NGC 6240 ( z=0.0243 , D_ L =111.2 Mpc https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ ) that contains two heavily obscured Compton-thick nuclei separated by 1 8 Gerssen2004 . Multiple multiwavelength observations unfold complex morphological structure and confirm that it is in an active merging state Pasquali2003 . Clearly visible by Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) irregular elongated morphology of this galaxy is often referred as `butterfly' or `lobster-shaped' Muller2018 . NGC 6240 is observed as the AGN in X-ray Komossa2003,Puccetti2016,Fabbiano2020 .

The spectra of the Northern and Southern nuclei were fitted by spectral models from Sherpa and both presented the narrow component of the Fe K emission line. It enabled us to apply the spectral model to these lines and to find relative offset 0.02 keV. The enclosed dynamical mass of the central region of NGC 6240 with radius 1 kpc was estimated 2.04 10^ 11 M_ . These data allowed us to carry on the high resolution direct N-body simulations with Newtonian and post-Newtonian (up to 2.5 PN correction) dynamics for this particular double SMBH system.

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra and HST observations of NGC6240, describing its luminous infrared nature, complex two-nucleus morphology, tidal tails, starburst dominance, extended X-ray emission, and likely obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: We present high spatial resolution X-ray Chandra HRC and HST WFPC2 H observations of the prototypical infrared luminous galaxy NGC6240. The central region of this system shows a remarkably complex morphology, with filaments and loops observed in the optical and X-rays. The total X-ray luminosity is dominated by the extended emission. Both nuclei are clearly detected in the HRC image and both appear to be extended. The energetics of the nuclei imply that the southern nucleus is the more plausible counterpart to the obscured AGN.

NGC6240 is a prototypical luminous infrared galaxy. Its high IR luminosity ( L_ IR 10^ 12 L_ ) and proximity ( d = 147 Mpc, for z = 0.0245 , H _ =50 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ) have made it the subject of numerous studies at all wavelengths. Its central region exhibits an extremely complex optical morphology with two distinctive nuclei, whilst at larger radii a distorted disc-like system with extended tidal tails is observed.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6240 as a prototype ULIRG with disturbed interacting nuclei, starburst-linked infrared emission, highly absorbed AGN character, and XMM-Newton Fe-line/plasma structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 (IRAS 16504+0228) is perhaps one of the most interesting and best studied objects in this class. Although it is slightly less luminous ( L_ FIR 6 10^ 11 L_ for H_0 = 75 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 ; Genzel et al. 1998) than the typical IR luminosity used to define a ULIRG, it exhibits all of the ULIRG characteristics, and it is thus studied as a prototype of this class of sources. Optical/IR observations show a complex system with a disturbed morphology consisting of two gravitationally interacting nuclei (Fosbury Wall 1979; Fried Schulz 1983).

XMM-Newton observations of the ULIRG NGC 6240 reveal: (1) For the first time, the presence of three distinct Fe K lines located at (6.41 0.02) keV, (6.68 0.02) keV and (7.01 0.04) keV (in the rest frame of the source); (2) That the broad-band spectrum is successfully described by the emission from three different plasma components with temperatures of (0.66 0.03) keV, (1.4 0.2) keV, and (5.5 1.5) keV. The ionized lines can be explained by emission from the highest temperature plasma components resulting in emission from Fe XXV and Fe XXVI.

Early X-ray observations of NGC 6240 with ROSAT (Schulz et al. 1998) revealed an exceptionally high X-ray luminosity ( L_ 0.1-2.0 keV 10^ 10 L_ ), which further confirmed the importance of NGC 6240 in understanding the AGN-starburst connection. Therefore, considerable effort has been made to analyze the spectral and spatial properties using different X-ray satellites. Moreover, NGC 6240 can be considered as the prototype objects of highly absorbed AGN in the local universe (Hasinger 2003).

sources.summary: NGC 6240 is described as a double-nucleus merging LINER with a starburst superwind, obscured AGN evidence, strong silicate/PAH features, and warm molecular hydrogen emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 ( z=0.0245 ), is a double-nucleus, merging galaxy (Fosbury Wall 1979), with an 8-1000 m luminosity of 7 10^ 11 L_ . The optical nuclear spectrum of NGC 6240 is classified as a LINER (Armus, Heckman Miley 1987), and the extended optical nebula reveals the presence of a starburst-driven superwind (Heckman, Armus Miley 1987). X-ray observations with ASCA (Turner et al., 1998), Beppo-Sax (Vignati et al., 1999), Chandra (Komossa et al., 2003; Ptak et al., 2003), XMM-Newton (Netzer et al., 2005) provide clear evidence for the presence of one (or two) AGN behind significant columns of absorbing material ( N_ H = 1-2 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ). Relatively strong [ OIV ] 25.89 m line emission in the ISO SWS spectrum of NGC 6240 led Lutz et al. (2003) to suggest that up to 50 of the infrared energy emitted by NGC 6240 could be powered by a buried AGN.

The spectra of UGC 5101 and NGC 6240, on the other hand, are dominated by strong silicate absorption at 9.7 m and 18 m, and PAH emission at 6.2, 7.7, 11.3, and 12.7 m (Armus et al. 2004, 2005). The extinctions toward the nuclei in UGC 5101 and NGC 6240, as estimated from the depths of the silicate absorption, are at least A _ V = 15-35 and A _ V = 60 mag, respectively.

NGC 6240 has little or no water ice absorption, but very strong emission lines from warm ( T 300-400 K) H _ 2 . The mass of this warm gas is estimated to be approximately 1.4 10^ 8 M_ - about 1 of the cold molecular gas mass derived from single-dish millimeter CO line measurements (Solomon et al. 1997), but up to 3-7 of the cold molecular gas within the central 1 kpc measured by Tacconi et al. (1999).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 6240 as a merging, double-nucleus LINER/ULIRG where both nuclei host AGN, with starburst-like mid-infrared continuum and a modest bolometric AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 NGC 6240 is an interacting system in an initial state of merging showing two distinct nuclei with gaseous and stellar tails. It is optically classified as a LINER (Veilleux et al. 1995). In the hard X-rays (E >10 keV) the high observed luminosity is interpreted as direct AGN emission absorbed by a column density N _H 2 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 (Vignati et al. 1999); in the 2-10 keV range the presence of the AGN is indicated by a Fe K line with large EW. Recently, observations obtained with Chandra revealed that both nuclei host an AGN (Komossa et al. 2003).

This has been confirmed by our L-band observations (Risaliti et al. 2006B), which will be summarized in the next Section. The mid-IR continuum of NGC 6240 is typical of a starburst, with an indication of the presence of an AGN coming from high ionization emission lines, such as [ OIV ] 25.9 m (Lutz et al. 2003). A recent study based on observations performed with Spitzer confirms this result (Armus et al. 2006) and estimates an AGN contribution to the bolometric luminosity of 20-24 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6240 as an advanced merger with buried AGN evidence, including dual hidden black holes and a 4.5-11.5 percent bolometric AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 is the only source that does not strictly comply with the ULIRG classification, because of its slightly lower IR luminosity. It is anyway an advanced merger showing all the morphological and physical properties of ULIRGs, and is usually included in this class.

As mentioned, many X-ray observations suggest the possible presence in NGC 6240 of a pair of supermassive black holes hidden behind large column densities of screening material. A detailed IR study pivoted on the Spitzer IRS spectrum and integrated with photometric data from near- to far-IR is presented by Armus et al. (2006). As a result of the SED modelling a hot dust component at 700 K is found, accounting for 3.5 per cent of the bolometric luminosity and pointing to the presence of a buried AGN. The AGN contribution is again estimated as 3--5 per cent through high-ionization lines, but can raise up to 20--24 per cent if correcting the [ Ne v ] / [ Ne ii ] ratio for the extinction derived from the hard X-ray data. Our estimate of _ bol (4.5--11.5 per cent) is then fully reliable, in spite of the great simplicity of our method as opposed to a multicomponent fit of the whole IR SED, which is anyway necessary to fully characterize an individual source.

sources.summary: NGC 6240 is described as an infrared-luminous merger with two AGN/LINER nuclei; its molecular abundance ratios allow PDR/XDR models but favor an XDR contribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 is an infrared luminous merger of two massive spiral galaxies. Two AGN/LINER nuclei are separated by 1 \ and the bulk of the molecular gas has piled up between the two nuclei iono07,tacconi99 . Luminous \ 4--3 emission is also emerging from in between the two nuclei where the H _2 emission is also located. The mid-IR sources (24.5 m) are associated with the two X-ray nuclei - the brightest by far being the southern nucleus ( T =55-60 K) egami06 .

For NGC 6240 the / \ ratio is consistent with both XDR and PDR models while the \ relative abundance favours an XDR. It is interesting to note that even if the molecular gas of NGC 6240 is collected in-between the two nuclei, their radiation could still impact the gas. The two AGNs are separated by 1 .5 which means that the molecular gas is irradiated by intense X-ray emission from two directions on only 370 pc distance.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6240 as a luminous galaxy merger with dual active nuclei, intense star formation, and spatially resolved shock-heated hard X-ray gas in its central region.

quotes.quote: We have obtained a deep, sub-arcsecond resolution X-ray image of the nuclear region of the luminous galaxy merger NGC 6240 with Chandra , which resolves the X-ray emission from the pair of active nuclei and the diffuse hot gas in great detail. We detect extended hard X-ray emission from kT 6 keV ( 70 million K) hot gas over a spatial scale of 5 kpc, indicating the presence of fast shocks with velocity of 2200 km s ^ 1 . For the first time we obtain the spatial distribution of this highly ionized gas emitting Fe 25 , which shows a remarkable correspondence to the large scale morphology of H _2 (1-0) S(1) line emission and H filaments.

In the local universe, NGC 6240 z = 0.02448 0.00003 ; Downes93 is a unique galaxy, in the throes of a violent merging event and on its way to becoming an elliptical galaxy Tacconi99,Tecza00,Bush08,Engel10, Medling11 . It is experiencing intense star formation (e.g., 61 30 M_ yr ^ 1 in Yun Carilli 2002; 25 M_ yr ^ 1 in Engel et al. \ 2010). With L_ FIR 10^ 11.8 L_ just below 10^ 12 L_ , the luminosity threshold for the ultraluminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs, Sanders Mirabel 1996; Genzel et al. \ 1998), NGC 6240 is expected to become an ULIRG when the galaxies coalesce and a final starburst is triggered Engel10 . The Chandra X-ray images Lira02,Komossa03 of the central region of NGC 6240 revealed two gravitationally interacting active supermassive black holes (SMBHs) separated by 1.5 ( 0.7 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 6240 as a LIRG/LINER major merger with two active nuclei, shock-heated molecular gas, dense cold clouds, and large molecular gas masses.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: centred on the phase centre RA =16^ h 52^ m 58^ s .890 , Dec =+02^ 24^ 03^ .90

quotes.quote: We present the first observations of H ^ 13 CN (1-0) , H ^ 13 CO ^+(1-0) and SiO (2-1) in NGC 6240, obtained with the IRAM PdBI. Combining a Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) code with Large Velocity Gradient (LVG) modelling, and with additional data from the literature, we simultaneously fit three gas phases and six molecular species to constrain the physical condition of the molecular gas, including mass luminosity conversion factors. We find 10^ 10 M_ of dense molecular gas in cold, dense clouds ( T_ k 10 K, n_ H _2 10^6 cm ^ 3 ) with a volume filling factor <0.002 , embedded in a shock heated molecular medium ( T_ k 2000 K, n_ H _2 10^ 3.6 cm ^ 3 ), both surrounded by an extended diffuse phase ( T_ k 200 K, n_ H _2 10^ 2.5 cm ^ 3 ). We derive a global _ CO =1.5^ 7.1 _ 1.1 with gas masses _ 10 M / [ M_ ] =10.1_ 10.0 ^ 10.8 , dominated by the dense gas.

NGC 6240 is an early stage major merger galaxy in the constellation of Ophiuchus. Hosting two active supermassive black holes and possessing an infra-red luminosity L_ 8-1000 m =10^ 11.73 L_ it is a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) and a LINER galaxy (low-ionization nuclear emission-line region) Komossa2003,Lutz2003,Sanders2003,Veron2006 . Studies by Tacconi1999 and Tecza2000 have shown that while star formation is concentrated in two nuclei, the molecular gas is predominantly in a turbulent, thick inter-nuclear disk.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution MUSE spectroscopy of the merging system NGC 6240, discovering three nuclei within 1 kpc and resolving the southern component into two nuclei separated by 198 pc.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 is a well-studied nearby galaxy system in the process of merging. Based on optical, X-ray, and radio observations, it is thought to harbor two active nuclei. We carried out a detailed optical 3D spectroscopic study to investigate the inner region of this system in connection with existing MERLIN and VLBA data.

We observed NGC 6240 with very high spatial resolution using the MUSE instrument in the Narrow-Field Mode with the four-laser GALACSI adaptive optics system on the ESO VLT under seeing conditions of 0 49 . Our 3D spectra cover the wavelength range from 4725 to 9350 at a spatial resolution of 75 mas. We report the discovery of three nuclei in the final state of merging within a region of only 1 kpc in the NGC 6240 system. Thanks to MUSE we are able to show that the formerly unresolved southern component actually consists of two distinct nuclei separated by only 198 pc.

sources.summary: The paper models ALMA CO observations of NGC 6240, describing it as a luminous infrared major merger whose central molecular gas is better explained by a tidal bridge than a disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 is a luminous infrared galaxy in the local universe in the midst of a major merger. We analyze high-resolution interferometric observations of warm molecular gas using CO J = 3-2 and 6-5 in the central few kpc of NGC 6240 taken by the Atacama Large Millimeter Array. Using these CO line observations, we model the density distribution and kinematics of the molecular gas between the nuclei of the galaxies. Our models suggest that a disk model represents the data poorly. Instead, we argue that the observations are consistent with a tidal bridge between the two nuclei.

NGC 6240 ( ; ) is a unique galaxy in the local universe ( z = 0.02448) in the midst of a major merger event Fried1983 that is triggering high star formation rates ( ; ) and active galactic nuclei (AGN) activity in its two progenitor nuclei Vignatti1999 , separated with a projected distance of around 1 kpc. This activity results in a far-infrared (FIR) luminosity L _ FIR 10 ^ 11.8 L _ ( ; ; ) that classifies it as a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG), just below the threshold that would classify it as an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG).

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 6240 as a late-stage merger with Chandra-resolved nuclear sources, a long-known double active nucleus, and a southern component that is itself a binary SMBH system.

quotes.quote: Next, the X-ray image of NGC 6240 obtained with Chandra (shown in blue in bottom right box), superimposed on the optical image of the galaxy (shown in red in top left panel, Figure fig:example_1 a), clearly shows two sources at a distance of about 900 pc (Kollatschny et al., 2020) between them (projected onto the sky plane). Interestingly, the presence of a double active nucleus -- two black holes -- at the center of NGC 6240 was known back in 1983. Since then, numerous studies of the galaxy have been carried out. But because the black holes are so close to each other, their exact locations have remained elusive. Later, thanks to observations with extremely high spatial resolution, it was established (Kollatschny et al., 2020) that one of them, the southern one, is in fact itself a binary system of supermassive black holes (Figure fig:example_4 ).

Each of the supermassive black holes has a mass exceeding 9 10^7 M _ (see Table tab1 ). Moreover, these three objects are at such a distance that we can say that the system is at a fairly late stage of its merger, which will take more than a billion years.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6240 as a merging ULIRG with a double nucleus, young massive nuclear condensations, heavy obscuration, and nuclear star formation that dominates its infrared output.

quotes.quote: We have analysed HST data available for three discrete sources identified in the double nucleus of NGC 6240. We have fitted the observed colours to STARBURST99 models to estimate their ages, masses and intrinsic reddenings.

The nuclear condensations in NGC 6240 have four independent colours available, (B-V), (V-I), (V-J) and (V-K), which allow us to determine a unique combination of age, reddening and SFR with relatively small uncertainties. The probability distributions for the nuclear sources in NGC 6240 have been derived from the fits with _ tot ^2 1.5 times the _ min ^2 value of the fitting procedure. Under the assumption that these sources are clusters, they are dated as young as 4 Myr and are heavily obscured, with E(B-V) = 1 - 2.3 or A _V = 4 - 9 in agreement with spectroscopic measurements. Their mass estimates, however, are extremely high, from 3 10 ^7 to 10 ^9 M _ .

Comparison with previously determined SN rates and galactic wind estimates based on NGC 6240's L _ IR indicates that the nuclear sources dominate the galaxy's total IR luminosity and star formation activity.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6240 as a high-R starburst merger with LINER-like excitation but star-formation-dominated mid-infrared spectra, whose emission is consistent with fast shocks.

quotes.quote: The high- R starburst mergers are NGC 1614, NGC 3256, NGC 4194, NGC 6240, Arp 220, Arp 236 (ESO 541--IG 23), Arp 299, and IRAS 18293 3413.

The three high- R mergers (NGC 1614, NGC 6240, and Arp 220) show LINER-like excitation. NGC 4194 and Arp 299 are also close to the domain of the LINER. The recent mid-infrared spectroscopy by Genzel et al. (1998) suggests that NGC 6240 and Arp 220 are star-formation dominated objects. Therefore, it is unlikely that their optical emission-line nebulae are powered by the putative central engine of active galactic nuclei in them. Thus their LINER-like excitation conditions can be attributed to shock heating (see also Veilleux et al. 1995; Kim et al. 1998; Taniguchi et al. 1998).

The excitation properties of both Arp 220 and NGC 6240 can be explained by fast shock models with the shock velocity of 300 -- 400 km s ^ 1 . Since the H line widths of these galaxies are 600 km s ^ 1 and 900 km s ^ 1 , respectively (Armus et al. 1989), the shock interpretation seems reasonable.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6240 as a merging ULIRG with tidal structure, a compact molecular core, and X-ray/FeK evidence for a luminous obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: With a redshift z=0.024 and a far-infrared luminosity of 10^ 12 L_ (cf. ), NGC 6240 is one of the nearest members of the class of ULIRGs (although due to changes of the methods to integrate over the wide IRAS bands and the adopted value of H_0 , most authors now attribute an IR luminosity <10^ 12 L_ to NGC 6240 rendering it a LIRG instead of a ULIRG; favoring a small H_0 and considering it more typical for the `upper class' we continue to call it an ULIRG). This object has remarkable properties: its infrared H _2 2.121 m and [ FeII ] 1.644 m line luminosities and the ratio of H _2 to bolometric luminosities are the largest currently known ; its apparent tidal tails and loops and a double nucleus complemented by its large stellar velocity dispersion of 360 km/s (among the highest values ever found in the center of a galaxy, ) suggest that it is a merging system on its way to become an elliptical. Like other ULIRGs, the object contains a compact, only 10^2 pc large, luminous CO(1-0) emitting core of molecular gas .

The first analysis of the spectrum of NGC 6240 already revealed a conspicuous FeK line complex with a high equivalent width of 2 keV typical for an AGN with some sort of scattering geometry. The spectrum resembles that of NGC 1068, the well studied `prototype' of a hidden AGN . Adopting the same scattering geometry as in NGC 1068 the intrinsic X-ray luminosity should be about 10^2 times larger than that of the observed scattered radiation, yielding at least 10^ 44 erg/s for the hard component which translates into L_ bol ( AGN ) 10^ 45 erg/s. This means that the AGN contributes an appreciable part of L_ FIR 4 10^ 45 erg/s.

sources.summary: The paper presents K-band and CO observations of NGC 6240, finding dispersion-dominated dual nuclei, limited recent-starburst contribution, and nuclear stellar populations consistent with remnants of the progenitor galaxies' bulges.

quotes.quote: We present spatially resolved integral field spectroscopic K-band data at a resolution of 0.13 \ (60 pc) and interferometric CO(2-1) line observations of the prototypical merging system NGC 6240 . Despite the clear rotational signature, the stellar kinematics in the two nuclei are dominated by dispersion. We use Jeans modelling to derive the masses and the mass-to-light ratios of the nuclei. Combining the luminosities with the spatially resolved Br equivalent width shows that only 1/3 of the K-band continuum from the nuclei is associated with the most recent star forming episode; and that less than 30 of the system's bolometric luminosity and only 9 of its stellar mass is due to this starburst.

The star formation properties, calculated from typical merger star formation histories, demonstrate the impact of different assumptions about the star formation history. The properties of the nuclei, and the existence of a prominent old stellar population, indicate that the nuclei are remnants of the progenitor galaxies' bulges.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6240 as a ULIRG merger of two Seyfert hosts, with weak observed lines, only one detected line, and anomalously high HCO+/HCN.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 and NGC 6240 did not show strong lines, so we spent additional time on these sources to lower the noise to better than 0.5 mK. Both NGC 6240 and Arp 220 are ultra luminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGS). NGC 1068, NGC 4258 and NGC 6240 all have Seyfert 2 nuclei; however, the emission we observe in NGC 1068 is likely dominated by the surrounding nuclear starburst ring sch00 . NGC 6240 is the merger of two Seyfert 2 host galaxies where the two AGNs are separated by less than 2 \ and the molecular emission is concentrated in a small region centered on the Seyfert nuclei ion07 .

In NGC 6240 the only line detectable is , and in two other galaxies, NGC 3690 and NGC 4258, we detect \ but not HCN. NGC 6240 is not plotted in Figure 2 because we did not detect either HCN or , but the ratio of HCO ^+ /HCN is greater than 2.3 (3 sigma limit).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6240 as a mid-stage merger outlier, with enhanced H2/PAH and PAH ratios, two close nuclei, central shock-excited H2, silicate absorption, and a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: Despite the small number of galaxies in our sample we find two galaxies that are outliers in one or more properties: NGC 6240 does not only show a significantly larger H _2 /PAH flux ratio (see subsec:PDR ), but also a larger PAH 11.3/17 m flux ratio as well as a larger ratio of the radii H _2 /IR cont, H _2 /PAH and PAH 11.3/7.7 m than most of the other galaxies in our sample. Previous observations of the center of this galaxy have revealed two nuclei with a separation of 1.8 Fri83 and that the H _2 emission peaks between the two nuclei and is thermally excited via slow shocks triggered by the collision of the two nuclei Wer93,Sug97,Tec00,Arm06 . This may explain the intense H _2 emission in the very center of NGC 6240 unresolved with Spitzer IRS, see Arm06 , but is likely not responsible for the correlation seen in the outer disk between H _2 and PAH.
Only for NGC 6240 and NGC 520 we find a three times smaller H _2 flux with no-extinction than for the screen model, which is due to the large silicate absorption in these galaxies ( _ 9.7 1.3 ).

However, for three galaxies (NGC 4676 North, NGC 6240, and NGC 2623) we find the opposite behavior, namely, a decreasing PAH 7.7/11.3 m ratio towards the center, which has been also found in the center of weak AGN host galaxies e.g., Smi07, Odo09, Dia10, Sal10 and suggested to be attributed to the selective destruction of the 7.7 m PAH molecule with the increasing hardness of the radiation field. This picture is supported by our observations since two of the galaxies mentioned above (NGC 6240 and NGC 2623) are characterized as low luminosity AGN hosts (see Table tab_sample ), wheras none of the rest of the sample seems to host an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6240 as a disturbed infrared galaxy merger with dual nuclei, tidal structures, molecular gas, and a large soft X-ray halo probed by Chandra.

quotes.quote: We report on a recent 150-ks long Chandra observation of the ultraluminous infrared galaxy merger NGC 6240, which allows a detailed investigation of the diffuse galactic halo. Extended soft X-ray emission is detected at the 3 confidence level over a diamond-shaped region with projected physical size of 110 80 kpc, and a single-component thermal model provides a reasonably good fit to the observed X-ray spectrum. The hot gas has a temperature of 7.5 million K, an estimated density of 2.5 10 ^ 3 cm ^ 3 , and a total mass of 10 ^ 10 M_ , resulting in an intrinsic 0.4--2.5 keV luminosity of 4 10 ^ 41 erg s ^ 1 .

The optical morphology of NGC 6240 is highly disturbed, with long tidal tails and a distorted disk obstructed by broad dust lanes (Fosbury Wall 1979; Gerssen et al. 2004). The central kpc harbors two nuclei, whose position and relative separation are wavelength-dependent owing to the significant obscuration (Schulz et al. 1993; Max et al. 2007). A huge amount (several 10 ^9 M_ ) of molecular gas is concentrated in between (Tacconi et al. 1999; Iono et al. 2007), giving rise to exceptional, shock-excited H _2 line emission in the near-IR (Joseph et al. 1984; Ohyama et al. 2003).

sources.summary: NGC 6240 is treated as a strong outlier with enhanced mid-J CO relative to infrared luminosity, likely requiring powerful shocks beyond ordinary star-formation-driven superwinds.

quotes.quote: The relatively small sample scatter in Fig. 4 for the SF-dominated LIRGs (except for NGC 6240) suggests convincingly that the current SF is also the main heating source for the mid- J CO line emission. If we exclude NGC 6240 on one side of the main locus and the two AGNs from our sample on the other side, the average and sample standard deviation of R_ midCO from the remaining detections in our own sample are 4.13 ( R^ SF _ midCO ; i.e., the dashed line in Fig. 4) and 0.10 ( _ SF ; marked by the two dotted lines in Fig. 4), respectively. NGC 6240 has R_ midCO /R^ SF _ midCO 6.3 , which is 8 _ SF above R^ SF _ midCO . The starburst superwinds in NGC 6240 are believed to power the large-scale diffuse ionized gas (e.g., Heckman et al. 1987) and shock-excited H _ 2 line emission (e.g., Max et al. 2005).

The fact that no galaxies are seen at intermediate R_ midCO values argues against the hypothesis that NGC 6240 represents an optimal wind-gas coupling efficiency. Therefore, we can not rule out the possibility that some forms of category-B shocks might also be at work in the nuclear region of NGC 6240, where the gas is highly turbulent (Tacconi et al. 1999) and the heating source of the warm H _2 emission is still controversial, with arguments for (e.g., Ohyama et al. 2000) and against (e.g., Feruglio et al. 2013) a superwind case.

sources.summary: The paper measures massive black holes in NGC 6240 nuclei, notes dust prevents one JAM model, compares its scaling-relation placement, and finds its gas may sustain growth.

quotes.quote: In medling11 , we place limits on the mass of the black hole in the south nucleus of NGC 6240 using 2-D stellar kinematic maps and two methods. We calculate the lower limit to the mass by assuming the stars lie in a thin Keplerian disk, and fit a density profile including a black hole and a smoothly-varying spheroidal mass component: () = M_ BH + _ 0 ^ .

We use both methods here for all galaxies with measured stellar kinematics, except for the north black hole in NGC 6240. This nucleus contains patchy dust that prevents a consistent light profile from being produced by the JAM models; in order to use this measurement technique on that black hole, equivalently high resolution imaging at a longer wavelength ( L band or beyond) is required.

For example, IRASF17207-0014 falls closer to the M_ BH _ * relation than to the M_ BH L_ H,bulge relation; for NGC 6240S, the reverse is true. Taking these uncertainties into account, the gas masses could be sufficient in UGC5101 and NGC 6240, but perhaps not in NGC 2623 or IRASF17207-0014.

sources.summary: The paper critiques very cold dense-gas interpretations for NGC 6240, arguing ALMA HCN and dust temperatures rule out chemical fractionation-dominated conditions.

quotes.quote: The effects of isotope fractionation have recently been invoked to explain the isotopologue ^ 13 CO, ^ 12 CO and H ^ 13 CN, H ^ 12 CN line ratios observed in the (U)LIRG, NGC 6240 2015ApJ...815..114T . However, this work did not consider the minimum T_ kin set by the CR heating that is expected to be ubiquitous in the environments of starbursts, and argued that dense ( n=10^6 cm ^ 3 ) and very cold ( T_ kin = 10 K ) gas comprises the most massive phase of H_2 in NGC 6240. At such low temperatures, isotopic fractionation can indeed operate e.g. 2013A&A...550A..56R , but these conditions can only be found in dense gas cores, deep inside giant molecular clouds in the relatively quiescent Milky Way, with its low levels of average CR energy density.

For NGC 6240, ALMA observations (Project: 2012.00077.S, PI: N. Scoville) give T_ b HCN (J=4-3) 3 K at 70-pc spatial resolution; thus for its observed HCN( 4-3/1-0 ) global brightness ratio of 0.18 it would be: T_ b HCN (J=1-0) 17 K as the corresponding lower limit for the temperature of the dense gas. This is well above the temperature range where chemical fractionation can operate. On the other hand, the T_ dust 40 K found in NGC 6240 2000MNRAS.312..433L , and for a normal gas-to-dust mass ratio, there is no room for a significant mass of colder dust.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6240 as an interacting ULIRG/LINER with two hard X-ray nuclei associated with black holes, then models its central SMBH binary and estimates merger completion within 55 Myr.

quotes.quote: Today one of the strongest SMBHB candidate is a ULIRG galaxy NGC 6240 which was X-ray spatially and spectroscopically resolved by Chandra.

As mentioned before first spatially resolved SMBHB was a system at interacting galaxy NGC 6240 (z=0.0243 Solomon1997 ) which optically classified as low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) Veron2006 . Chandra observations confirmed the presence of two luminous hard X-ray emission sources at south (S) and north (N) cores with a strong neutral Fe K _ line at each Komossa2003 associated to BHs. Using the adaptive optics at Keck II telescope Max2007,Medling2011 separation was found between the components 1.5 which corresponds to previous radio Beswick2001,Gallimore2004 and X-ray observations Komossa2003 .

The estimated time for a merging in NGC 6240 galaxy with the different physical parameters is less than 55 Myr. But this concrete number are valid only for our set of combination of initial mass ratios.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes central Chandra data for merger galaxy NGC 6240, finding extended X-ray structures from photoionized and shock-heated gas, a confined inner hot ISM/free outer wind, and star-formation-driven enrichment.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed spectral and imaging analysis of the central 15'' radius ( 7.5 kpc ) region of the merger galaxy NGC 6240 that makes use of all the available Chandra ACIS data ( 0.3 - 3 keV effective exposure of 190 ks ). This region shows extended X-ray structures with lower energy counterparts imaged in CO, [ O III ] and H line emission. We find both photo-ionized phases of possible nuclear excitation and thermal shock-excited emission in the different large-scale components: the north-west ``loop" detected in H , the region surrounding the two nuclei , the large outflow region to the north-east detected in [ O III ] , and the southern X-ray extensions. The latter could be the ionization cone of the northern nucleus , with the N counterpart being obscured by the galaxy disk . The radial distribution of the X-ray surface brightness suggests a confined hot interstellar medium at < 2.5 kpc , with a free-flowing wind at larger radii; if the confinement is magnetic, we estimate B-field values of 100 G , similar to those measured in the halo of M82.

The element to Fe abundance ratios of the thermal components in the different regions of the extended X-ray emission are generally compatible with SNe II yields, confirming the importance of the active star formation in NGC 6240.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6240 as a dense-gas outlier and final-stage starburst merger whose shocks and extreme conditions may enhance HCN and HCO+ emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 and NGC 3627 are two outliers deviated from the relationship of IR and dense gas tracer luminosity. This makes their dense-gas star formation efficiencies, which are traced by infrared to dense gas luminosity ratio, about three times lower than the average value of other galaxies. This may indicate that star formation efficiency of these dense gas is suppressed, or the dense gas tracer emission is enhanced by the extra heating mechanisms for the same star-forming activity.

NGC 6240 is a starburst galaxy in final merger stage Tacconi99,Iono07,Papadopoulos14,Kollatschny2020 . The enormous shock condition across NGC 6240, shown both in the enhanced high- J CO lines and in large scale shocked gas distribution Meijerink2013,Cicone18, Lu15 , could contribute mechanical heating to the dense gas, along with cosmic-ray and far-ultraviolet (FUV) radiation from photon-dominated regions Papadopoulos14 . These extreme conditions in NGC 6240 may also enhance the \ and \ emission.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 6240 among emission-dominated galaxies, notes its unusual low-excitation H2O SLED ordering, and finds dense collisional excitation affects its submillimeter H2O emission.

quotes.quote: Our extragalactic sample includes a variety of well-known local (U)LIRGs: a QSO (Mrk 231), AGNs such as IRAS 05189-2524, NGC 1068, NGC 7469, NGC 4945, IRAS 08572+3915, and NGC 6240 the latter with strong CO emission associated with shocks, see mei13 , (U)LIRGs with a compact obscured nucleus (NGC 4418, Arp 299a, Zw 049.057, ESO 320-G030, Mrk 273, Arp 220, IRAS 17208-0014), and starburst galaxies (NGC 253 and IRAS 13120-5453). As delineated by the equivalent width of the OH 65 m doublet fis14,gon15 , 6 galaxies (NGC 1068, NGC 7469, NGC 253, NGC 6240, IRAS 13120-5453, and NGC 4945) have overall weak far-IR molecular absorption features but usually strong emission in the atomic/ionic fine-structure lines ( EQW(OH 65 m) <20 km s ^ 1 ), and will be referred to as ``emission-dominated galaxies'' (EDGs), while the rest (with EQW(OH 65 m) >20 km s ^ 1 ) have stronger far-IR molecular absorption features and will be referred to as ``absorption-dominated galaxies'' (ADGs). The extragalactic submm SLEDs, with the H _2 O line fluxes normalized to that of the 3_ 21 3_ 12 1163 GHz line in Fig. sleds b, show a rather common U shaped pattern for the low-excitation ( E_ up 300 K) lines: the 1163 GHz line is usually the strongest line followed by the 988 GHz line, except in NGC 1068, NGC 6240, and probably NGC 7469 where this sequence is interchanged.

We also show that in some EDGs (NGC 6240 and NGC 253) collisional excitation of the base levels is required to make the radiative pumping operational, and finally discuss in Appendix app_outl whether the cases of NGC 7469 and NGC 1068 represent sources where H _2 O is shock-excited, or whether geometrical effects can account for the lack of H _2 O 75 m absorption.

Only in the case of NGC 6240, the highest density was favored for the component that dominates the H _2 O submm emission. While in the 4 ULIRGs results remain the same, meaning that collisional excitation has little effect on the H _2 O excitation, the quenching of collisions has a strong effect in some sub-ULIRGs, specifically in NGC 253 and NGC 6240.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 6240 as a dual AGN with a lopsided, partly detached outflow whose apparent energy-driving consistency may mask a weak fossil outflow.

quotes.quote: Finally, NGC 6240 is a dual AGN, with the outflow located in between the two nuclei, with the blue wing very close to the southern nucleus and the red wing approximately in the middle between them Fig. 1, panels d and e in Cicone2018ApJ . This lopsidedness (and, in the case of the red wing, detachment) suggests the outflow may be a fossil. On the other hand, the p and E values of the outflow agree with expectations of energy driving, calling the fossil interpretation into question.

However, the current luminosity of the AGN, L_ AGN 1.1 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 Cicone2018ApJ , may be only a small fraction of the Eddington luminosity. The SMBH mass in the southern nucleus is estimated to be M_ BH 7-8 10^8 Medling2011ApJ, Kollatschny2020AA , which corresponds to an Eddington luminosity L_ Edd 0.9-1 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 , i.e. L_ AGN /L_ Edd 0.01 . In that case, the outflow is in fact very weak and the agreement with the energy-driving expectation based on the current AGN luminosity is merely a coincidence.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 6240 as a nearby ULIRG merger with two optical nuclei and spatially resolved active black holes detected by Chandra.

quotes.quote: Recent observations of the Chandra X-ray observatory let to the discovery of a pair of active black holes Actually, what is observed is the characteristic signs of AGN activity. The step that this is equivalent to SMBH detection is based on the widespread belief, that AGN are indeed powered by SMBHs. This statement basically holds for all other BBH evidence: it is always the activity associated with the black hole(s) which is detected. in the center of the (ultra)luminous infrared galaxy NGC 6240 (Komossa et al. 2003). The BBH in NGC 6240 is special in that it is presently the only BH pair I am aware of at the center of one galaxy, and spatially resolved such that both (active) BHs can be separately identified.

The galaxy NGC 6240 belongs to the class of (ultra)luminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs) which are characterized by an IR luminosity exceeding 10 ^ 12 L _ (see Sanders Mirabel 1996 for a review). NGC 6240 is one the nearest (U)LIRGs and is considered a key representative of its class. NGC 6240 is the result of the merger of two galaxies, expected to form an elliptical galaxy in the future. It harbors two optical nuclei (Fried Schulz 1983).

sources.summary: The paper presents ISO infrared spectroscopy of merging luminous infrared galaxy NGC 6240, finding starburst and obscured AGN signatures, powerful molecular hydrogen emission, and shock cooling tied to turbulent gas and a superwind.

quotes.quote: We present Infrared Space Observatory mid- and far-infrared spectroscopy of the merging galaxy NGC 6240 , an object presenting many aspects of importance for the role of star formation and AGN activity in [ ultra ] luminous infrared galaxies. The mid-infrared spectrum shows starburst indicators in the form of low excitation fine-structure line emission and aromatic `PAH' features. A strong high excitation [ O IV ] line is observed which most likely originates in the Narrow Line Region of an optically obscured AGN.

NGC 6240 shows extremely powerful emission in the pure rotational lines of molecular hydrogen. We argue that this emission is mainly due to shocks in its turbulent central gas component and its starburst superwind. The total shock cooling in infrared emission lines accounts for 0.6 of the bolometric luminosity, mainly through rotational H _2 emission and the [ O I ] 63 m line.

sources.summary: The paper presents HST morphology and kinematics of NGC 6240, describing its distorted ULIRG merger, double nucleus, obscured AGNs, LINER emission, and non-equilibrium gas motions.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 at a distance of 104 Mpc (assuming H_0 = 70 Mpc ^ 1 , both here and throughout this paper) has L_ IR = 10^ 11.8 , just below the canonical ULIRG luminosity boundary of 10^ 12 . Nonetheless, it is generally regarded both as a bona fide ULIRG, and as one of the nearest and best studied examples of the class (Genzel 1998). The large-scale optical morphology of NGC 6240 is highly distorted and shows the tidal tails indicative of an ongoing merger (Keel 1990; see also Figure f:DSS ).

The optical emission of NGC 6240 is characterized by a LINER-type spectrum and the width of the H + [ NII ] emission lines increases strongly towards the double nucleus (Keel 1990). The presence of an AGN component in NGC 6240 was confirmed by the detection of hard X-rays with ASCA (Iwasawa Comastri 1998) and BEPPO-SAX (Vignati 1999). Interestingly, subsequent Chandra observations showed that there is actually an AGN associated with each of the two nuclei of NGC 6240 (Komossa 2003).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6240 as unresolved in ISOCAM, adopts a Br-map size and line fluxes, and says MIR diagnostics are starburst-dominated.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 - The MIR emission of NGC 6240 is unresolved in the ISOCAM data. We adopt a size of 3 \ based on the Br map of Tec00 . As a comparison, we derive from brightness profile fitting a HPBW of the order of 2.7 , which is ill-constrained since it is smaller than the cleaned PSF HPBW of 3 . We adopted the Br flux of Rieke85 measured in a 8.7 \ aperture, and the Pa flux of Sim96 measured in a slit of width 1.5 \ oriented along the major axis of the source. In view of the Br map of Tec00 , these apertures should include the total fluxes.
MIR diagnostics indicate that starburst activity dominates the dust emission and the low-excitation fine-structure line emission at these wavelengths ( Gen98, Rig99, Lau00 ; but see also Lutz03 ).

sources.summary: The ULIRG survey identifies NGC 6240 as unusually bright in molecular-hydrogen transitions, with S(0) and S(7) detections and multi-component excitation affecting warm gas estimates.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 is the only source in our sample where the emission is bright enough to be detected in these transitions (Armus et al. 2006a). The resulting S(3), S(2), and S(1) line profiles, averaged over the two slit-nod positions, are shown in the first, second, and third columns of Figure 1, respectively. The S(0) detections, which indicates a somewhat cooler component, are shown in Figure 2, while the S(7) detections, which arise from a hotter component, are shown in Figure 3.

Besides NGC 6240, we detect the S(0) line in only two other sources, IRAS 12112+0305 and IRAS 13342+3932. Likewise, the S(7) line is detected in only six sources, NGC 6240, Arp 220, IRAS 06301-7954, IRAS 12032+1707, IRAS 12112+0305, and IRAS 17068+4027 with S/N > 3.
The resulting gas mass is an order of magnitude larger than the masses calculated for NGC 6240 and IRAS 12112+0305.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC6240 as a merging ULIRG, requiring an added stellar component and discussing prior interpretations of its luminosity and obscured AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: NGC6240 NGC6240 is a merging ULIRG. Such objects are one to two orders of magnitude brighter than starbursts. Our fit in Fig. ulirg.ps is acceptable despite a 30 deficiency near 40--50 m. To better match the NIR photometry, we added to the starburst SED a 4000K black body with L = 10^ 8.8 .

Lutz et al. (2003) suggest that stars account for most ( 75 ) of the total luminosity and that the rest is due to an optically obscured AGN. When they subtract from the SED of NGC6240 a scaled--up M82 template, a faint (0.07Jy) residue remains which they attribute to the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper reviews NGC 6240 as a double-nucleus ULIRG merger with luminous extended X-rays, thermal and hard AGN-like components, and obscured AGN emission contributing to FIR output.

quotes.quote: Greiner X-ray emission of the ULIRG NGC 6240 We briefly review and extend our discussion of the detection of the extraordinarily luminous ( >10^ 42 erg/s) partly extended ( > 30 kpc diameter) X-ray emission from the double-nucleus ultraluminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) NGC 6240. The spectrum can be well fit by emission from two components in roughly equal proportions: a thermal optically thin plasma with kT 0.6 keV and a hard component that can be represented by a canonical AGN powerlaw.

Hence, owing to its low redshift of z =0.024, NGC 6240 is one of the nearest members of the class of ultraluminous infrared galaxies (hereafter ULIRGs). Its optical morphology (e.g., Fosbury Wall 1979, Fried Schulz 1983) and its large stellar velocity dispersion of 360 km/s (among the highest values ever found in the center of a galaxy: Lester Gaffney 1994) suggest that it is a merging system of disk galaxies near to forming an elliptical galaxy.

NGC 6240, a merger on its way to become an elliptical galaxy, is found to harbour an exceptionally luminous extended X-ray source. A spectral decomposition of the X-ray flux leads to a hard component most likely interpreted by scattered radiation from an otherwise obscured AGN that significantly contributes to the FIR of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The review treats NGC 6240 as a local gas-rich ultraluminous merger with dual accreting black holes, starburst emission, and rotating stellar disks around the active nuclei.

quotes.quote: In our local universe, one outstanding example is the case of the ultra--luminous infrared galaxy NGC 6240, an ongoing merger between two gas--rich galaxies (Komossa 2003; for a review on binary black holes see also Komossa 2006). Chandra images have revealed the occurrence of two nuclear X-ray sources, 1.4 kpc apart, whose spectral distribution is consistent with being two accreting massive BHs embedded in the diluted emission of a starburst.

The double AGN activity in NGC 6240 occurs just on a similar scale, as the X--ray nuclei are 1 kpc apart in projection on the sky. It is remarkable that high--resolution near--infrared images at the Keck II telescope, combined with radio and X-ray positions, have revealed the habitat of the two active BHs in this ultra--luminous system. Each active BH appears to be at the center of a rotating stellar disc surrounded by a cloud of young star clusters lying in the plane of each disc (Max, Canalizo de Vries 2007).

sources.summary: The paper explains NGC 6240 as a ULIRG that should be an OH megamaser by FIR color, but likely lacks one because its pumping-radiation to dense-gas ratio is too low.

quotes.quote: lo05 pointed out that, based on its FIR color and \ the ULIRG NGC 6240 should be an OHM see also figure 2 of baan89 . A possible reason why it is not is its low ratio of \ to dense molecular gas. This gas is traced by HCN and HCO ^+ molecules, and Table 1 in carpio shows that the ratios / \ and / + \ are both less than half in NGC 6240 than in every OHM, seven in all, included in their sample. Thus the FIR pump rate per molecule in NGC 6240 is much lower than in the typical OHM. Therefore, a minimum ratio of pumping radiation to dense molecular gas may also be a prerequisite for producing OHM.

sources.summary: The survey describes NGC 6240 as a LINER merger with two hard-X-ray AGN, hot dust, compact radio Seyfert-like nuclei, and extended near-IR emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 (IC 4625, UGC 10592, VV 617) v95 classify the optical spectrum as LINER. However, hard X-ray imaging has revealed two AGNs separated by 1^ k03 . a07 detect continuum emission from dust hotter than 300 K, which also indicates the presence of an AGN. High-resolution radio images from 2.3 to 8.4 GHz reveal two compact radio sources with properties similar to Seyfert nuclei gb04 . Near-infrared images also show two nuclei as well as bright extended emission sco00 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6240 as a confirmed Compton-thick LINER, citing its hard continuum, flat-spectrum diagnostic, low X-ray/[OIII] ratio, and measured FeK equivalent width.

quotes.quote: Based on BeppoSAX data, sensitive above 10 keV up to 300 keV, two of the LINERs in the sample, NGC 3690B, and NGC 6240, show intrinsic continuum above 10 keV, typical of Compton-thick sources ptak_chandra_2003,ceca_enshrouded_2002, risaliti_distribution_1999,vignali_probing_1999 . Hereinafter we consider UGC 05101, NGC 3690B, and NGC 6240 objects as Compton-thick sources according to published literature results (hereinafter we refer to them as 'Confirmed Compton-thick '). Concerning the indirect Compton-thick diagnostics used above, two of the confirmed Compton-thick show flat spectra (UGC 05101 and NGC 6240) whereas NGC 3690B seems to have a somewhat steeper spectrum.

Moreover, the three confirmed Compton-thick show log(F_ X (2-10 keV)/F( [ OIII ] ))<0 , consistent with being Compton-thick object. Also, we have been able to determine the EW(FeK ) of the three confirmed Compton-thick in our sample, finding EW(FeK )=280 180 keV , EW(FeK )=230 110 keV and EW(FeK )=380 60 keV , for UGC 05101, NGC 3690B and NGC 6240 respectively (shown as black stars in Figs. cha3:fig:EWNH2 and cha3:fig:LHOIII_fek ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6240 as a local LIRG with extreme starburst activity and a binary AGN, reporting marginal H13CN and broad sharp HCO+ and HCN line profiles.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 is a local LIRG with extreme starburst activity and the first Binary Active Galactic Nucleus komossa03 . The detection of H ^ 13 CN line was only marginal and its spectrum indicates complicated velocity components, probably a reflection of the dynamical structure of NGC 6240 (Figure. spec-hcn ). Comparing to other galaxies, the HCO ^+ and HCN lines of NGC 6240 show very sharp single-peak profile and much broader line width ( > 1000 ). greve09 reported an extensive molecular line data set of NGC 6240, including multi-transition CO, HCN, HCO ^+ , and CS. Our observation of C _2 H(1-0) lines of NGC 6240 is a complement to their work, because C _2 H(1-0) has an intermediate critical density lying between HCN and CO and has its own distinctive chemical property.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 6240 as a LIRG and treats it as a merger case where radio supernova rates and [FeII] excess lie above the sample relation.

quotes.quote: The far-infrared luminosities range from 1.4 10^ 10 L_ to 3.2 10^ 12 L_ , where Arp 220 is considered an Ultra Luminous Infrared Galaxy (ULIRG), with L>10^ 12 L_ , and NGC 6240, NGC 1614 and NGC 7552 are classified as Luminous Infrared Galaxies (LIRGs), with L>10^ 11 L_ ,.

Arp 220 and NGC 6240 have radio SNrates even higher than the SB99 instantaneous SNrates. Also, in Figure fig:pixpix , the points for these galaxies lie above the best fit line, demonstrating a [ FeII ] excess. This excess is caused by merger-related shocks that are exciting the [ FeII ] in addition to the SNRs. These powerful merger (U)LIRGs may demonstrate a limit to the validity of the direct relationship between SNrate and [ FeII ] luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper reports GBT H2CO absorption toward NGC 6240 that conflicts with earlier emission claims and compares the detection with CO and HCO+ imaging sizes.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240: Our GBT detection of the 1_ 10 1_ 11 transition in absorption (Figure fig:ExgalFormSingles-OnePage ; 5.2 in a single smoothed 20 km s ^ 1 channel) is not consistent with the emission reported by Baan1993 . As noted by Mangum2008 , our 1_ 10 1_ 11 spectrum peaks near 7300 km s ^ 1 , slightly blueshifted relative to the systemic velocity of 7359 km s ^ 1 Greve2009 . CO J= 1-0 images Bryant1999 indicate a molecular gas source size of 10^ . NGC 6240 has also been imaged in CO J= 3-2 and HCO ^+ J= 4-3 by Wilson2008 . Based on the Wilson2008 measurements Iono2009 derive a deconvolved CO J= 3-2 source size of 0.9^ 0.6^ .

sources.summary: The paper maps CO(1-0) in NGC6240, describing it as a luminous infrared, early-stage merger hosting two AGN with complex gas kinematics and a blueshifted molecular outflow from the southern nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present high spatial resolution CO(1-0) mapping of the luminous infrared galaxy NGC6240 obtained with the IRAM - Plateau de Bure Interferometer. This source is a well-known early-stage merging system hosting two AGN. We find a broad CO(1-0) line profile, with maximum velocity 800 km s^ 1 and FWZI=1400 km s^ 1 , and displaying several kinematic components, revealing the complexity of the gas dynamics in this system. We detect a blueshifted CO emission with velocity in the range -200 and -500 km s^ 1 , peaked around the southern AGN, at the same position where the H_2 outflow is located, and with a mass loss rate of 500 M_ yr^ 1 . We interpret this blueshifted component as a outflow, originating from the southern nucleus.

sources.summary: The HIFI survey models NGC 6240 as an early-stage two-nucleus merger whose warm and low-excitation gas components support a shock-heated, outflow-rich interpretation.

quotes.quote: NGC6240 NGC 6240 is an early-stage merger hosting two nuclei that are separated by 1.5 beswick2001 . CO observations reveal that most of molecular gas in NGC 6240 is located in the overlap region of the two nuclei e.g., iono2007, engel2010, feruglio2013b, feruglio2013a . We utilized a warm component to model medium-excitation H_ 2 O emissions (which is likely associated with the gas concentration between the two nuclei), and an extended low-excitation component (i.e., ER) to account for ground-state H_ 2 O emissions.

The high line-to-continuum ratios of CO lines has been investigated in several recent publications and have been attributed to galaxy-wide shocks due to the advance merger state of NGC 6240 e.g., meijerink2013, papadopoulos2014 . This picture also explains the strong H_2 ( 1-0 ) S(1) emission at 2.12 m goldader1995 , the spectacular butterfly-shaped emission-line nebula seen in HST H images gerssen2004 and Chandra X-ray image feruglio2013a , and the massive molecular outflow detected by the high-resolution interferometry CO maps feruglio2013b and HCN/CS maps scoville2015 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6240 as a merging system with two active nuclei and reports two compact mm-wave components probably associated with them.

quotes.quote: 80: NGC 6240 This is a well-studied merging system, and two active nuclei have been identified at various wavelengths . According to a recent study by , there may be another in-active nucleus to the south of the active nucleus in the south. Our image at a 0 03 resolution shows two mm-wave compact components: one at the center ( a ) and the other in the north ( b ), probably associated with the first and second active nuclei . On the other hand, insignificant emission is found around the third nucleus ( ), as already confirmed by , who analyzed the same ALMA data. suggested that within a radius of 0 077 around each of the two active nuclei, dust thermal emission is dominant at 242 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6240 as a ULIRG with starburst and AGN activity, extreme high-J CO emission, massive molecular outflows, attenuated broad wings, and shock-driven CO excitation.

quotes.quote: The CO ladders of our sources are more in agreement with those measured in more extreme SBs, such as M 82 the archetypal SB galaxy, see Mashian+15 , and NGC 6240 a known ULIRG to host both SB and AGN, see Papadopoulos+14 . Nevertheless, while these two galaxies may show similar gas excitation up to J_ up =7 , they differ strongly for higher rotational lines, as it was evidenced in the study performed by , analyzing Herschel CO data in the range J_ up = 14-30 . These authors found that the CO ladders of M 82 and NGC 6240, albeit similar up to J_ up 8 , differ strongly at higher- J , with the former peaking around J_ up 8 and then quickly declining with increasing J_ up , as opposed to NGC 6240 that shows strong emission lines even up to J_ up =28 .

An important take-away point from is that the broad wings are highly attenuated in the galaxy-integrated CO spectrum of NGC 6240, where the components at velocities above 500 show fluxes that are <5 of the peak of the line, despite this source hosting one of the most massive and extended molecular outflows known. Indeed, the spatially resolved analysis of the CO data of NGC 6240, reported in the same paper, demonstrates that (i) along the east-west direction of expansion of the outflow, several kpc away from the known nuclear molecular disc(s) in NGC 6240 Medling+19 , the broad CO components can reach velocities beyond 1000 , and at the same time still show a significant flux at low-to-zero line-of-sight velocity, and that (ii) as much as 60 20 of the total H _2 reservoir in the central 6 kpc 3 kpc region of NGC 6240 is embedded in the outflow.
Previous studies by Meijerink+13 and Papadopoulos+14 focused on the galaxy NGC 6240 argue that the high CO excitation is arising from galaxy-wide shocks.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6240 as a local ULIRG analogue whose obscured AGN, far-infrared dust peak, starburst-like emission, and hard X-ray absorption resemble high-redshift absorbed ROSAT sources.

quotes.quote: Here it is shown that a number of high-redshift absorbed X-ray sources selected in the ROSAT Deep Survey of the Lockman Hole have broad-band spectral energy distributions very similar to the local ULIRG NGC6240, lending additional support to the background models for the obscured universe.

Figure SED shows the spectral energy distribution (SED) of NGC 6240 in a F_ representation from the radio to the hard X-ray band. The SED peaks in the far-infrared dust band and shows the mid-infrared policyclic aromatic hydrocarbonic emission bands as well as the synchrotron radio emission typical for starburst galaxies. The optical starlight peak is lower than the dust emission peak - similar to the overall extragalactic energy distribution. In the hard X-ray range the nonthermal AGN continuum emission is severely absorbed below 15 keV and strong reprocessed iron line is seen.

sources.summary: The paper presents 6240 as a nearby luminous merger whose exceptionally strong vibrational emission peaks between two remnant nuclei, indicating shock-powered gas infall capable of fueling its nuclear starburst.

quotes.quote: The most extreme vibrational emission is found in the nearby luminous merger 6240 : 7 10^7 is emitted in the v=1 0 S(1) line alone (for H_0=75 Mpc 1 and with no correction for extinction). This line contains 0.012 of the bolometric luminosity of 6240 , which is considerably higher than any other galaxy . Together the vibrational lines may account for 0.1 of the total bolometric luminosity. Imaging of the v=1 0 S(1) emission from 6240 has shown that the emission peaks between the two remnant nuclei of the merging system .
This analysis shows that the central regions of 6240 are being fueled at a rate sufficient to maintain starburst activity at the level required to account for the FIR luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra imaging spectroscopy of NGC 6240, identifying two absorbed hard X-ray nuclei and extended soft X-ray emission tied to starburst-driven superwind activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 is one of the nearest ULIRGs and is considered a key representative of its class. Here, we report the first high-resolution imaging spectroscopy of NGC 6240 in X-rays. The observation, performed with the ACIS-S detector aboard the Chandra X-ray observatory, led to the discovery of two hard nuclei, coincident with the optical-IR nuclei of NGC 6240. The AGN character of both nuclei is revealed by the detection of absorbed hard, luminous X-ray emission and two strong neutral Fe K lines.

The close correlation of the extended emission with the optical H emission of NGC 6240, in combination with the softness of its spectrum, clearly indicates its relation to starburst-driven superwind activity.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6240 as an outlier in the mid-infrared to hard-X-ray relation, with high X-ray emission, low mid-infrared continuum, and likely 6 micron obscuration.

quotes.quote: In the discussion of individual objects in the Appendix, we also mention NGC 6240 and NGC 4945 where obscuration of the mid-infrared AGN continuum is likely significant.

The two outlying objects Mrk 463 and NGC 6240 are briefly discussed in the Appendix. The spread observed in our sample is significantly larger than in the imaging study of Krabbe et al. ( ), where with the exception of the outlier NGC 6240 the ratio of intrinsic hard-X and mid-IR emission varies by just a factor of 3-4.

NGC 6240 is one of the sources deviating most from the correlation, in the sense of high X-ray and low mid-IR. This could include a contribution of adopting a high estimate for the intrinsic X-ray emission (cf. also the lower estimate of Ikebe et al. ), and the likely noticeable obscuration of the 6 m continuum in this object.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6240 as a merging ULIRG with starburst-dominated soft X-rays, zoned hot gas, Seyfert-like nuclear photoionization, and supersolar abundance ratios.

quotes.quote: We present new \ observation of the Ultra Luminous Infrared Galaxy (ULIRG) NGC 6240. We analyze the reflecting grating spectrometer (RGS) data, and data from the other instruments, and find a starburst dominated 0.5--3 keV spectrum with global properties resembling those observed in M82 but with a much higher luminosity. We show that the starburst region can be divided into an outer zone, beyond a radius of about 2.1 kpc, with a gas temperature of about 10^7 K and a central region with temperatures in the range (2--6) 10^7  K.

We also identify a small inner part, very close to the active nuclei, with typical Seyfert 2 properties including a large amount of photoionized gas producing a strong Fe \ 6.4 keV line. The combined abundance, temperature and emission measure analysis indicates super solar Ne/O, Mg/O, Si/O, S/O and possibly also Fe/O.

sources.summary: The paper presents sub-arcsecond Keck mid-infrared observations of NGC 6240, finding compact nuclear emission dominated by the southern nucleus while AGN and starburst power remain difficult to disentangle.

quotes.quote: In order to examine the relative importance of powerful starbursts and Compton-thick AGNs in NGC 6240, we have obtained mid-infrared images and low-resolution spectra of the galaxy with sub-arcsecond spatial resolution using the Keck Telescopes. Despite the high spatial resolution ( 200 pc) of our data, no signature of the hidden AGNs has been detected in the mid-infrared. The southern nucleus, which we show provides 80--90 of the total 8--25 m luminosity of the system, has a mid-infrared spectrum and a mid-/far-infrared spectral energy distribution consistent with starbursts. At the same time, however, it is also possible to attribute up to 60 of the bolometric luminosity to an AGN, consistent with X-ray observations, if the AGN is heavily obscured and emits mostly in the far-infrared. This ambiguity arises because the intrinsic variation of properties among a given galaxy population (e.g., starbursts) introduces at least a factor of a few uncertainty even into the most robust AGN-starburst diagnostics.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3-5 micron spectra of NGC 6240, identifying two active nuclei and showing that AGN emission dominates both nuclei in this band while contributing little bolometric luminosity.

quotes.quote: We present 3-5 m spectroscopy of the interacting system NGC 6240, showing the presence of two active galactic nuclei.
