The brightest (southern) nucleus shows up with a starburst-like emission, with a prominent 3.3 m emission feature. However, the presence of an AGN is revealed by the detection of a broad Br emission line, with a width of 1,800 km s ^ 1 . The spectrum of the faintest (northern) nucleus shows typical AGN features, such as a steep continuum and broad absorption features in the M-band. We discuss the physical properties of the dusty absorbers/emitters, and show that in both nuclei the AGN is dominant in the 3-5 m band, but its contribution to the total luminosity is small (a few percent of the starburst emission).

sources.summary: The paper presents VLA H I and OH absorption observations of merging galaxy NGC 6240, linking gas disks, central gas accumulation, the southern nucleus, and large-scale absorption to merger dynamics and superwind activity.

quotes.quote: VLA observations of large-scale HI and OH absorption in the merging galaxy of NGC 6240 are presented with 1 arcsec resolution. HI absorption is found across large areas of the extended radio continuum structure with a strong concentration towards the double-nucleus. The OH absorption is confined to the nuclear region. The HI and OH observations identify fractions of the gas disks of the two galaxies and confirm the presence of central gas accumulation between the nuclei. The data clearly identify the nucleus of the southern galaxy as the origin of the symmetric superwind outflow and also reveal blue-shifted components resulting from a nuclear starburst.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC6240 as an interacting near-ULIRG with a hidden AGN, high extinction, starburst-dominated near-IR emission, and unusually low inferred metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC6240 This peculiar object is an interacting system with a bolometric luminosity close to the limit of IRAS ultraluminous galaxies. A deeply hidden AGN is revealed by X--ray spectra showing a prominent FeK 6.4 KeV line and powerful continuum emission at 100 keV (Iwasawa Comastri , Iwasawa , Matt ). The active nucleus is much less evident at longer wavelengths but its contribution to the bolometric luminosity is likely to be important because the L (100 keV)/ L(FIR) ratio is similar to that measured in the Circinus galaxy.
The mass to light ratio derived here is unequivocally in the starburst domain and demonstrates that the near IR emission from this massive object is still dominated by a young stellar population.
Since (1.62) is much more sensitive to metallicity than (2.29), this indicates that NGC6240 is a relatively metal poor system as well.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC6240 in a dense-gas HCN SED comparison, describing its lower excitation than NGC253 and citing work where HCO+ emission traces lower-density gas than HCN.

quotes.quote: We have used our new HCN observations as well as data from the literature to compare the excitation of the dense gas via the HCN SEDs in NGC253, M82, NGC4945, NGC6240, Mrk231 and Arp220 (references are given in the caption of Fig. fig:normseds ). We restrict ourselves here to HCN because it provides the most complete data set among tracers of the dense gas for these active galaxies. All galaxies except Mrk231 have been measured in HCN(1 0), HCN(3 2), and HCN(4 3). For Mrk231, only the HCN(1 0) and HCN(4 3) have been published in the literature.
Interestingly, NGC6240 and Mrk231 show a lower excitation of the dense gas phase compared to NGC253 (Fig. fig:normseds ) despite their much higher FIR luminosities (Fig. fig:hcnhco_vs_lir ).
From an analysis of the dense gas properties in Arp220 and NGC6240, greve06 recently concluded, that the HCO ^+ emission is dominantly emitted from regions with 10 times lower density than HCN.

sources.summary: NGC 6240 is treated as a very complex, peculiar double-nucleus AGN system that was excluded from analysis but may satisfy Compton-thick criteria.

quotes.quote: In this discussion, we have not considered the complicated spectra of Cen A, NGC 1275, and the double nucleus NGC 6240, who may also have consistent properties with the various Compton thick definitions.
Additionally, we did not include an analysis of the very complex source NGC 6240, which may also be classified as Compton thick.
NGC 6240: This peculiar source hosts two active nuclei, as found by Chandra 2003ApJ...582L..15K . Therefore, we do not include an analysis of this source.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6240 as a prototypical ULIRG with broad molecular emission and elevated dense-gas fraction inferred from dense molecular tracers.

quotes.quote: We adopt the GC08 measurements for two non-detections in B08 (Mrk 231 IRAS 17208-0014), as well as for NGC 6240, which had a discrepant measurement. This galaxy has a very broad \ emission line, so in this case we prefer to use the data from GC08 because of their significantly wider bandwidth.

Our findings are also in agreement with the Arp 220 and NGC 6240 case studies of 2009ApJ...692.1432G , who report an increased dense gas fraction in these two prototypical ULIRGs. Using measurements of a large number of dense molecular tracers, these authors infer that, for these two ULIRGs, most of the molecular gas is in a dense phase and that the GMCs mass-size power-law is steeper than in normal star-forming galaxies, indicating that HCN emission traces denser and more compact GMCs compared to HCO+ transitions between the same J levels.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6240 as a nearby major merger where integral-field spectroscopy and simulations constrain stellar populations, mass-to-light ratios, and progenitor-bulge nuclei.

quotes.quote: There are therefore only a few nearby examples -- of which NGC 6240 is one -- from which we can learn how the merging process proceeds in detail. Building on the existing qualitative picture of this merger , we have recently used integral field spectroscopy (see Fig. fig:science2 ) and hydrodynamical simulations to perform a quantitative analysis of the mass, luminosity, age, and origin of its young and old stellar populations . The mass-to-light ratios indicate the bulk of the stellar mass is older than 1 Gyr. Coupled with other properties (size, dispersion, etc) of the nuclei, this strongly suggests they are the progenitor bulges.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 6240 as the first discovered supermassive black-hole binary system, an ultraluminous infrared galaxy with two Chandra-resolved AGNs separated by about 1 kpc.

quotes.quote: The first discovered SMB system was, an ultra-luminous infrared galaxy, NGC 6240. The object was observed with the Chandra X-ray telescope that resolved a pair of X-ray emitting AGNs k03 . Two nuclei in NGC 6240 were revealed by the detection of absorbed, hard, luminous X-ray emission as well as two strong low-ionization Fe K lines. The SMBHs in NGC 6240 have a projected separation of 1 kpc at a distance of 103 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6240 as nearly ULIRG-class, with optical tidal tails, a double nucleus seen at optical/infrared/radio wavelengths, and infrared power from starburst plus AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 just falls short of being a traditional ULIRG with a luminosity of L_ IR =10^ 12 L_ but otherwise has all the characteristics of the class. The extended tidal tails seen in the optical indicate a merger. NGC 6240 hosts a double nucleus separated by 1.5'' 0.1'' as seen in ground based observations in the optical 1993A&A...277..416S and near infrared 1994ApJ...437L..23D , yet a separation of 2'' 0.15'' as measured in [ FeII ] _ 1.64 m 1993ApJ...405..522V and radio observations 1990ApJ...362..434C,1990ApJ...365..478E,2001MNRAS.325..151B . The high infrared luminosity is partially due to the powerful nuclear starburst and partially to an AGN continuum 1998ApJ...498..579G .

sources.summary: The paper confirms NGC 6240 as the one previously X-ray-selected dual AGN whose two nuclei are both red in infrared K-L' colors.

quotes.quote: Infrared properties of X-ray dual AGNs Among the 29 observed infrared luminous merging systems, signatures of dual AGNs with separations of > 0 5 were previously reported from X-ray observations for the following five sources: Mrk 463 bia08 , Mrk 739 kos11 , NGC 3393 fab11 , NGC 6240 kom03 , and IRAS 20210 + 1121 pic10 . Using our methods, we detect double nuclear emission in Mrk 463, Mrk 739, NGC 6240, and IRAS 20210 + 1121, but not in NGC 3393. However, only NGC 6240 displays the double red ( K-L' 1.0 mag) nuclei that are indicative of a dual AGN.

Fraction of infrared dual AGNs Among the 29 observed merging (U)LIRGs, only four systems (Mrk 273, Arp 220, IRAS 16474 + 3430, and NGC 6240) show red ( K-L' 1.0 mag) colors in both merging nuclei, indicative of dual AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6240 as a local ULIRG merger of two AGN host galaxies with extreme starburst activity, detected HC3N 29-28, and HC3N-poor chemistry.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 is a local ULIRG with extreme starburst activity and prominent AGN 2003ApJ...582L..15K at the distance of 98 Mpc 2012A&A...538A.152Z , as merger of two AGN host galaxies. Observations showed that molecular emissions are concentrated in a small region between the two Seyfert nuclei 2008ApJS..178..189W,2007ApJ...659..283I . Dense gas tracers including lines of HCN, HCO ^+ , HNC and other molecular lines of CO, C _2 H, HNCO, C ^ 18 O and CN 1-0 had been detected, as well as tentatively detection of H ^ 13 CN, SiO, H ^ 13 CN and CH _3 OH lines and HC _3 N 10-9 and 12-11 were reported in .

With the detection of HC _3 N 29-28, which requires the excitation conditions with high density (around 10^6 cm ^ 3 ) and high temperature ( E_u 190 K) 2015A&A...579A.101A , at least part of molecular gas in NGC 6240 should have similar physical properties to that in NGC 4418.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 6240 as the first X-ray-identified spatially resolved SMBH pair, with two heavily absorbed, iron-line-emitting X-ray galaxy cores.

quotes.quote: Indeed, it was X-ray imaging spectroscopy which led to the identification of the first pair of accreting SMBHs in the galaxy NGC 6240 (see the next section).

Discovery and follow-up observations of dual AGN and candidates t The first spatially resolved SMBH pair was identified in X-rays, and was based on a dedicated \ observation which targeted the nearby ultraluminous IR galaxy (ULIRG) NGC 6240. \ imaging spectroscopy has revealed that both galaxy cores emit luminous point-like X-rays (Fig. fig: n6240_komossa ), and show similar X-ray spectra which are flat, heavily absorbed, both exhibiting a strong neutral iron line Komossa2003, Nardini2017 . These are the tell-tale signs of heavily obscured AGN.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC6240 as a Compton-thick AGN colour-calibration object, citing Spitzer IRS spectra, strong silicate absorption, a steep mid-infrared slope, and high 9-22 micron colour.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:fig11 we have four CT AGN (NGC6240, NGC7479, NGC6552, NGC5643) that is used to define [ 9 m - 22 m ] > 2.0 colour range.

Stierwalt2013 represent Spitzer Infrared Spectrograph IRS, Houck2004 spectra covering 5-38 m of NGC6240. According to their silicate depth and MIR slope measurements, NGC6240 shows a strong silicate absorption with a steep MIR slope.
The MIR spectral measurements of NGC6240, NGC7479 and NGC5643 show evidence for relatively faint 9 m and relatively bright 22 m giving higher [ 9 m - 22 m ] colours.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6240S as one nucleus of a stage-D LIRG merger whose dual nuclei both contain Compton-thick AGNs with reflected-dominated spectra.

quotes.quote: IRAS F16504+0228 S/N (NGC 6240S and NGC 6240N): This LIRG is a stage-D merger composed of two nuclei, NGC 6240S (south) and NGC 6240N (north), with a separation of 1 7 (0.8 kpc). This target has been well studied with Chandra Ptak2003,Grimes2005,Iwasawa2011 , and it is revealed that both nuclei contain CT AGNs showing reflected-dominated spectra Komossa2003 . Puccetti2016 carry out a detailed spectral analysis of the NuSTAR, Chandra, XMM-Newton, and BeppoSAX data, and confirm the presence of the dual CT AGNs (see also Nardini2017 ). They estimate the column densities ( N_ H = 1.47^ +0.21 _ 0.17 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 and N_ H = 1.55^ +0.72 _ 0.23 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) and the absorption-corrected X-ray luminosities (log L_ 2-10 = 43.72 ^ +0.20 _ 0.19 and log L_ 2-10 = 43.30 ^ +0.24 _ 0.20 ) for NGC 6240S and NGC 6240N, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 6240 as a near-ULIRG and models its strongly detected inner X-ray emission with a 6.4 keV Gaussian component.

quotes.quote: Following ptak03 we have additionally included NGC 6240, whose far-IR luminosity is just below the ULIRG range.

In ptak03 emission was strongly detected in NGC 6240 and MKN 273. For the inner region in NGC 6240 we have added a gaussian component centered at 6.4 keV. This provides an excellent fit for the spectrum of NGC 6240.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6240 as optically LINER-like but unambiguously AGN-powered in X-rays, with Beppo-SAX evidence for a heavily obscured hard component.

quotes.quote: A prime example is NGC 6240, which was identified as an AGN from its X-ray luminosity, which exceeds any X-ray luminosity observed from pure star-forming galaxies schultz98 , but does not appear as an AGN in the optical.

Furthermore, NGC 4388 and MCG 03-58-007 are Sy2/LINERs from their optical line ratios, NGC 6240 is a pure LINER and NGC 6552 has no line ratios available for classifications, but all of these sources are unambiguously powered by an AGN due to their X-ray luminosity.

This includes NGC 6240, well known for its LINER classification in the optical, but unambiguously powered by a heavily obscured AGN in the X-rays, as Beppo-SAX showed with the detection of a hard component mitsuda95 .

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 6240 with the z 2 star-forming composite, finding similar mid-infrared shape but a shorter-wavelength far-infrared peak indicating warmer dust.

quotes.quote: The bottom panel of Fig. sb_local_temp shows our z 2 composite SF SED compared to two local prototypical (U)LIRGs, Arp 220 ( L_ IR = 1.4 10^ 12 L _ ) and NGC 6240 ( L_ IR = 6.3 10^ 11 L _ ), both normalized to our composite SED at 7.0 m. While the mid-IR spectrum of NGC 6240 is very close to that of our high redshift composite, Arp 220 shows substantially more silicate absorption.
Neither of these local (U)LIRGs match our z 2 SED in the near or far-IR; specifically, Arp 220 and NGC 6240 peak at shorter wavelengths in the far-IR, indicating much more warm dust emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6240 as the sole elevated-[Fe II]/[O IV] LIRG lying on shock-model grids, with resolved neon lines and diagnostic evidence for shock excitation and fast gas motions.

quotes.quote: Only one of these source, NGC 6240 , which is known to exhibit strong shock gas emission due to the interaction of two counter-rotationg disks and references therein Armu06 , falls on the shock model ionization grids discussed earlier (Figure fig:SIV_NeII_shock ).
Among the 10 sources lying on the shock model grids, five of them have resolved neon emission lines, including NGC 6240 .
There appear to be five starburst LIRGs (including NGC 6240 ) that show evidence in at least one of these diagnostics for shock excitation and fast gas motions.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 6240 as a well-studied starburst with high diffuse X-ray luminosity and a diffuse fraction near or above unity, depending on SFR.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240, however, has been recently observed with a high diffuse luminosity 5.3 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 at 0.5-8 keV ( Wang13 ), which indicates a high value of f_ d 1.3 for a total SFR of 100 M_ yr ^ 1 ( Heckman00 ). A higher estimated SFR of 200 M_ yr ^ 1 gives f_ d 0.7 .
However, for some galaxies, such as NGC 6240, f_ d 1 is also possible.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 6240's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 is a very infrared-luminous, late-stage merger system consisting of two gas-rich massive spirals at a redshift of z= 0.025 ( D 114 Mpc) with a massive starburst and a binary AGN. Owing to this unique nature (discovered by ), NGC 6240 has been extensively studied at all wavelengths over the last decades. It belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample.

sources.summary: NGC 6240 is described as an early-stage merger with separated nuclei; its CO and [C II] line profiles are broad and model-dependent.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 is an early stage merger in which the two nuclei are well separated by 1.8'' in the I and -band Fried-1983 .
NGC 6240 has been observed in CO(1-0) by Feruglio-2013 , who find a line profile extending from −600 km s ^ 1 to +800 km s ^ 1 (compared to 1000 km s ^ 1 to +1500 km s ^ 1 in [ CII ] ).
The NGC 6240 line profile can be fitted with two or three components, which results in different values for the broad component FWHM.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6240 as the sample outlier in [CI]/CO behavior, likely because shock-dominated gas heating gives it the highest excitation temperature.

quotes.quote: The galaxy with the largest deviation in Figure figure1 b is NGC 6240, in which the gas heating is likely dominated by shocks (Meijerink et al. 2012). As shown in Figure 2a, NGC 6240 has the highest excitation temperature in the sample. Therefore, the temperature influence on the level populations of C i in NGC 6240 is likely the highest in this sample.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 6240 has ALMA-resolved molecular outflows and episodic UFO detections, and computes a time-averaged UFO velocity for the galaxy.

quotes.quote: The molecular outflows in NGC 1068 and NGC 6240 are spatially resolved by Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA), and their published results gar14,sai18 are also listed.
Most UFOs are episodic; for example, in NGC 6240, UFO absorption lines are detected in XMM2 (exposure time is 4763 s) and XMM4 (10678 s) observations, whereas not in XMM1 (10119 s) or XMM3 (3050 s).
In NGC 6240 case, the ``time-averaged'' v_ UFO is calculated to be (4.3 10^4 km s^ 1 4763 s +3.2 10^4 km s^ 1 10678 s )/(4763 s +10678 s )=3.5 10^4 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 6240 with Makani, noting its 40 kpc ionized outflow, smaller outflow-to-stellar-size ratio, tidal-feature overlap, and lower nebular luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240 ( z=0.04 ) has an ionized outflow that reaches a 40-kpc radius 2016ApJ...820...48Y . However, the outflow size relative to the stellar half-light radius (inside which half of the galaxy's starlight resides) is only /r_ *,1/2 4 in NGC 6240 (ref. 2013ApJ...768..102K ), versus /r_ *,1/2 >20 in Makani, and much of the NGC 6240 nebula coincides with stellar tidal features, unlike Makani. Furthermore, the \ luminosity of the NGC 6240 nebula is four times smaller than in Makani if the core \ emission follows the oxygen emission at a constant / \ line ratio.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 6240 among obscured or embedded AGN systems, uses existing images/calibrated data, and notes it is heavily obscured with two separated AGNs.

quotes.quote: Similarly, we already had calibrated data for IRAS 13120-5453 Ledger2021 , NGC 1068 Saito2022a, Saito2022b , and NGC 6240 (O.
Six galaxies in our sample have obscured or embedded AGN: IRAS 13120, Arp 220, NGC 6240, the southern nucleus of NGC 3256, NGC 4418, and NGC 1365.
NGC 6240 is heavily obscured Iwasawa2011 and has two separated AGNs Saito2018b .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC6240 as a composite LINER, citing ROSAT-based evidence that AGN and starburst power contribute comparably and noting its FIR-radio position lies near the model border.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, we investigate two cases of LINERs that are considered to have a composite nature, NGC6240 and M81. For NGC6240, Schulz et al. (1999) claimed, based on ROSAT data, that both AGN and starburst contribute in roughly equal proportion to the energetics of this galaxy. In our Fig. 2a, both NGC6240 and M81 are approximately located on the border of our model prediction.

sources.summary: The paper calls NGC 6240 atypical because, despite evidence for an active nucleus, its MIR features and FIR/MIR ratio suggest that star formation supplies most IR luminosity.

quotes.quote: In this sense NGC 6240 is quite atypical, since it falls perfectly on the correlation followed by star forming galaxies. The presence of an active nucleus in NGC 6240 was revealed by its strong X-ray luminosity and Ikebe et al. (2000) suggested that a large fraction of the IR luminosity of this galaxy could originate from its AGN. However, the presence of strong MIR features (Genzel et al. 1998), the absence of a strong continuum emission below 6 m (Laurent et al. 2000), together with a FIR over MIR ratio typical of star forming galaxies, suggest that the bulk of its IR luminosity may still be due to star formation.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6240 as a local mildly Compton-thick AGN prototype, comparing its observed optical and X-ray properties with IRAS 09104+4109 through redshift-track modeling.

quotes.quote: There are several examples of both mildly and heavily Compton thick sources in the local Universe discovered thanks to BeppoSAX. The prototype of the former is NGC 6240 at z=0.0245 (Vignati et al. 1999) while for the latter an handful of objects are reported by Maiolino et al. (1998).

The redshift tracks in the optical magnitude versus X--ray flux plane (Fig. 3) have been normalized to the observed X--ray flux and R magnitude of NGC 6240 and IRAS 09104 + 4109. The two objects are characterized by a similar SED but different X--ray luminosities (about 3 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 and 10 ^ 46 erg s ^ 1 respectively).

sources.summary: The paper reports new CI J=1-0 observations of NGC 6240 and uses them to estimate molecular gas mass, finding agreement with CO-based methods.

quotes.quote: We present new sensitive wide-band measurements of the fine structure line ^3 P _1 ^3 P _0 (J=1--0, 492 GHz) of neutral atomic carbon (CI) in the two typical Ultra Luminous Infrared Galaxies NGC 6240 and Arp 220. We then use them along with several other CI measurements in similar objects found in the literature to estimate their global molecular gas content under the assumption of a full CI-H _2 concomitance. We find excellent agreement between the H _2 gas mass estimated with this method and the standard methods using ^ 12 CO.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6240 as a close-binary ULIRG with two nuclei, disturbed velocity structure, and a velocity-dispersion estimate based on both nuclei and the internuclear peak.

quotes.quote: NGC 6240: The two nuclei of this source are separated by 1.4 kpc and still show some discrete velocity patterns. The velocity dispersion of the system peaks between them; at the position of this peak the stellar kinematics may reflect localized motions of self-gravitating gas ( tecza ; genzel01 ). For the velocity dispersion of this source we use the average of the values at two nuclei and at the internuclear peak (229 ), which is close to the luminosity-averaged value, 225 \ ( tecza ).

gmw_00000245.name: NGC 6251
gmw_00000245.overview: NGC 6251 is a nearby giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0801.2104] hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0801.2104] and a warped dust disk [arXiv:1211.0859]. It is classified as an intermediate Fanaroff-Riley type I/II radio galaxy [arXiv:1202.0868, arXiv:1310.2770] featuring a 2-megaparsec radio jet [arXiv:1810.12350, arXiv:2208.02130] and extended gamma-ray emission from its lobes [arXiv:2402.04591].

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 6251 in X-rays, describing it as a nearby FR I giant elliptical/type-2 AGN with debated accretion origin, high-flux variability, and possible jet-dominated output.

quotes.quote: Sambruna NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, Code 661, Greenbelt, MD 20771 We present the first X-ray monitoring observations of the X-ray bright FR I radio galaxy NGC 6251 observed with \ for 1 year. The primary goal of this study is to shed light on the origin of the X-rays, by investigating the spectral variability with model-independent methods coupled with time-resolved and flux-selected spectroscopy. The main results can be summarized as follows: 1) Throughout the monitoring campaign, NGC 6251 was in relatively high-flux state with an average 2--10 keV absorbed flux of the order of 4.5 10^ 12 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 and a corresponding intrinsic luminosity of 6 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 .

Here, we concentrate on the X-ray nuclear properties of the nearby radio galaxy NGC 6251 ( z =0.024), which is a giant elliptical galaxy hosting a supermassive black hole with mass M_ BH 4-8 10^8 M_ (Ferrarese Ford 1999). Based on its radio power at 178 MHz, NGC 6251 is classified as an FR I (e.g., Owen Laing 1989), whereas in the optical, it is classified as a type-2 AGNs (e.g., Shuder Osterbrock 1981). Despite the intensive study of this source at all wavelengths, the nature of the accretion process in NGC 6251 is still a matter of debate. Based on the radio-to-X-ray spectral energy distribution, Ho (1999) suggested that an Advection-Dominated Accretion Flow (ADAF) is present in the nucleus of NGC 6251.

At first sight, the possibility that the jet may dominate the radiative output of a radio galaxy may be surprising, and even more so for NGC6251, which has a Mpc radio jet (and hence appears forming a large viewing angle) and Seyfert-like emission during the low flux state.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6251 as a FRI radio galaxy and Fermi-detected MAGN, modeling its nuclear SED as jet-dominated and comparing one-zone SSC and spine-layer jet scenarios.

quotes.quote: NGC 6251 is classified as a FRI radio galaxy 1983MNRAS.204..151L based on of its morphology and monochromatic radio luminosity Throughout the paper, we adopt the cosmological parameter values H_0=71 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _m = 0.27 , and _ = 0.73 . of 0.94 10^ 31 erg s ^ 1 sr ^ 1 at 178 MHz 1977MNRAS.181..465W According to the classification proposed by 1974MNRAS.167P..31F , the flux density at the FRI/FRII boundary, corrected for the adopted cosmological values, is 1 10^ 32 erg s ^ 1 sr ^ 1 . . Nevertheless, it exhibits an unusual radio morphology, with an 1 extension of bright radio emission to the northwest 1977MNRAS.181..465W , and characteristics typical of both FRI and FRII radio galaxies see the radio maps at 327 and 608 MHz in 1997A&A...324..870M . At a distance of 104 Mpc, this corresponds to a linear extension of 1.8 Mpc (1 0.5 kpc). High resolution 1.48 and 4.9 GHz VLA studies reveal a radio core and a complex jet, that is bright and structured within 4.4 of the core ( 113 kpc), and then faint and curved at larger size scales 1984ApJS...54..291P . On the basis of radio images, 2000AJ....120..697S showed that the angle between the jet axis and the line of sight increases by 10 , going from 33 at 50 from the core to 45 at 200 .

We have presented a study of the broad-band nuclear SED of the radio galaxy NGC 6251, one of the MAGNs detected by Fermi LAT. In agreement with previous studies, the nuclear SED is dominated by non-thermal emission related to the sub-pc jet. A nuclear origin appears to be the most likely hypothesis for the ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6251, a nearby giant FR I/II radio galaxy, finding diffuse nonthermal X-ray emission from the NW lobe and modeling possible gamma-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Orla 171, Krak ow 30-244, Poland 4 National Research Council Research Associate, National Academy of Sciences, Washington, DC 20001, resident at Naval Research Laboratory, Washington, DC 20375, USA 5 Scuola Internazionale Superiore di Studi Avanzati (SISSA), 34014 Trieste, Italy We report the results of a X-ray imaging study of NGC 6251, a nearby giant radio galaxy with intermediate FR I/II radio properties. Our pointing direction was centered on the ray emission peak recently discovered with around the position of the north-west radio lobe 15 arcmin offset from the nucleus. After subtracting two ``off-source'' pointings adjacent to the radio lobe, and removing possible contaminants in the XIS field of view, we found significant residual X-ray emission most likely diffuse in nature. The spectrum of the excess X-ray emission is well fit by a power law with photon index = 1.90 0.15 and a 0.5-8 keV flux of 4 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . We interpret this diffuse X-ray emission component as being due to inverse-Compton up-scattering of the cosmic microwave background photons by ultrarelativistic electrons within the lobe, with only a minor contribution from the beamed emission of the large-scale jet.

We have presented a X-ray observation of the NW lobe in a nearby radio galaxy NGC 6251. Through this observation, we found for the first time non-thermal diffuse X-ray emission associated with the NW lobe. Since the error circle of the ray source 2FGL J1629.4+8236 contains both the NW lobe and outer jet region, we discussed the possible origin of ray emission by detailed modeling of the spectral energy distributions assuming a synchrotron plus inverse-Compton emission on CMB background photons.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6251 as a giant radio galaxy with disputed H I absorption, reporting a new non-detection and explaining it through a warped dust disk geometry.

quotes.quote: The giant radio galaxy NGC 6251 is listed as a non-detection by JvG89. However, evans05 reported an unpublished column density of 4.5 10^ 20 . To confirm this, we obtained a 12 hour WSRT service observation, with 1024 channels and a bandwidth of 20 MHz. The observation was processed similar to the VLA observations. We found no detection and set an upper limit of N_ HI <0.3 10^ 20 \ for this source.

The non-detection of NGC 6251 can be explained by the existence of a strong warp in the inner regions. While the outer parts of the disk are nearly edge-on at 76 ^ inclination, the inner regions are at 35 ^ inclination ferrarese99 . If neutral gas is present in the dust disk, this would significantly suppress the column density of \ in the center.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6251 as the nearest target, noting its loose-group environment, mixed FR-I/FR-II radio structure, old LOFAR lobes, dust lane, and misaligned inner and outer jets.

quotes.quote: R2-4: NGC 6251 NGC 6251 is our nearest target, and it has been the object of many observations since its identification Waggett1977,Laing1983 . studied the neighbouring 17 galaxies, which may form a loose group, but with very low background X-ray gas, explaining the large 1.6 Mpc radio jets. The main jet and lobes are centre-brightened like FR-I, but there are also hot spots near the end of the north and south lobes that suggest a FR-II type of morphology. The radio power is in between the two FR types.

Recent LOFAR observations at 150 MHz have revealed low surface brightness extensions and backflows of the lobes Cantwell2020 , with ages older than 200 Myr. A dust lane has been discovered by , roughly parallel to the radio jet, with a size of about 5 kpc. The inner orientation of the jets is still unclear; it appears at least that the inner jet and outer lobes are misaligned Jones2002 .

sources.summary: The paper reports Fermi-LAT detection of extended gamma-ray emission from NGC 6251's radio lobes and models its core and lobe spectral energy distributions.

quotes.quote: We report on the detection of extended ray emission from lobes in the radio galaxy NGC 6251 using observation data from Fermi Large Area Telescope (Fermi-LAT). The maximum likelihood analysis results show that a radio morphology template provides a better fit than a point-like source description for the observational data at a confidence level of 8.1 , and the contribution of lobes constitutes more than 50 of the total ray flux. Furthermore, the ray energy spectra show a significant disparity in shape between the core and lobe regions, with a curved log-parabola shape observed in core region and a power-law form observed in lobes. Neither the core region nor the northwest lobe displays significant flux variations in the long-term ray light curves.

NGC 6251 is a nearby (z=0.0247, 2003AJ....126.2268W ) giant RG with a radio angular scale extending to about 1^ .2 . Its radio morphology mainly consists of a radio core, a narrow straight jet with roughly uniform cone angle, and two large-scale radio lobes 1977MNRAS.181..465W,1981MNRAS.197..287S . The jet starts within 1 pc of the nucleus, extends towards the northwest (NW) for approximately 4^ .5 , and subsequently forms the NW lobe about 0^ .3 away from the radio core 1981MNRAS.197..287S,1984ApJS...54..291P .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6251 as a nearby FRI radio galaxy with a Seyfert-2 nucleus, dust disk, large radio jet, X-ray jet emission, thermal gas, and lobe-related X-ray cavity.

quotes.quote: NGC 6251 is a nearby ( z = 0.0244) Fanaroff Riley type I (FRI) radio galaxy with a supermassive black hole of mass (4--8) 10 ^ 8 M _ ( fer99 ). It exhibits complex morphology on a range of angular scales, and shows a rich variety of structure across the electromagnetic spectrum. Optically, NGC 6251 hosts a well-defined dust disk of diameter 730 pc for (H _0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ) ( fer99 ) and, based on velocity widths of allowed and forbidden lines, has been classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy ( shu81 ). NGC 6251 is the parent galaxy of a spectacular radio jet, which extends 4.5 arcmin (130 kpc) north-west (NW) of the nucleus and has an opening angle of 7.4 ^ ( wag77,per84 ).

We resolve X-ray jet emission in three distinct regions, and argue in favour of a synchrotron origin for all three; inverse-Compton emission models are possible but require extreme parameters. We detect thermal emission on both galaxy and group scales, and demonstrate that hot gas can confine the jet, particularly if relativistic beaming is important. We show evidence that the radio lobe has evacuated a cavity in the X-ray-emitting gas, and suggest that the lobe is close to the plane of the sky, with the jet entering the lobe close to the surface nearest to the observer.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6251 as a typical FRI misaligned AGN, describing its GeV detection, archival X-ray spectra, weak absorption, and absent Fe K line.

quotes.quote: A typical FRI: NGC 6251 NGC 6251 is a luminous FRI, the fifth -ray brightest object among the MAGNs (see Table tab2 ), located at z=0.0247. This radio galaxy, already associated with the EGRET source 3EG J1621+8203 , was detected by the Fermi -LAT in the first year of survey , , . All available archival data, from XMM--Newton (2002 March) and Swift (2007 April -- 2009 June) satellites were re--analyzed.

The XMM--Newton best--fit spectrum (0.3--10 keV) is typical of FRI radio sources . It is modeled by a power law ( =1.89 0.04) absorbed by Galactic column density (N _ H, Gal =5.4 10 ^ 20 cm ^ 2 , ) and intrinsic column density (a few 10 ^ 20 cm ^ 2 ), plus a thermal component (kT 0.6 keV). There is no significant evidence for a Fe K emission line, in agreement with .

sources.summary: The paper compares its NGC 6251 jet-shape transition measurement with earlier work, finding a slightly smaller break radius and attributing differences to width-fitting and core-offset treatment.

quotes.quote: We have compared our results for the radio galaxy NGC 6251 with those obtained earlier for this source by . We have found that the jet shape transition region in this source is at (1.6 0.2) 10^5 r_ g , assuming viewing angle of 18 and black hole mass of 6 10^8 M_ (see t:sourprop , t:break ). This is slightly smaller compared to (1-2) 10^5 Schwarzschild radius estimated by , who assumed the same black hole mass and a viewing angle of 19 .

The small difference might be caused by different techniques used to derive it. First, we measured transverse jet widths from the stacked image of the source, using 14 epochs at 15 GHz from the MOJAVE program and archival VLBA data. Second, we have taken into account the synchrotron opacity of the jet base by introducing the parameter r_0 that reflects an offset of the apparent 15 GHz core from the true jet apex.

sources.summary: The paper argues that NGC 6251 jet acceleration is not thermal, modeling its observed sub-parsec speed increase with magnetic driving in a radio-galaxy jet.

quotes.quote: One example is provided by the radio galaxy NGC 6251, in which S00 inferred a bulk acceleration from V 0.13 to V 0.42 on sub-parsec scales. This behavior was attributed by MLF02 to a thermal acceleration of a proton-electron plasma that is heated to a temperature T 10^ 12 K in a region of radius 0.03 pc . We note, however, that a thermally driven, purely hydrodynamic flow typically undergoes the bulk of its acceleration over a distance that is of the order of the size of the mass distribution that initially confines it by its gravity, which in this case is much smaller than the radius of the apparent acceleration zone (see thermalaccel ).

The sub--parsec-scale kinematics of the NGC 6251 jet was deduced by S00 after they discovered the counterjet in this powerful radio galaxy. By applying a relativistic beaming model to the measured variation in the jet--counterjet intensity ratio, they inferred that the outflow is accelerated from 0.13 at 0.53 \ pc to 0.42 at 1.0 \ pc .
The dimensional parameter values were determined on the basis of the inferred magnitudes of the NGC 6251 black-hole mass and mass outflow rate.

sources.summary: NGC6251 is cited as a radio galaxy whose jet shows repeated oblique or conical shock structures, motivating the paper's multiple-shock acceleration model.

quotes.quote: The observed standing shock features in AGN jets (e.g. Sanders 1983, Gawthorne 2006, Marscher et al. 2008), and the system of repeated shocks observed in jet structure at vastly discrepant spatial resolutions, like in the radio galaxy NGC6251 (Bridle and Perley, 1984) motivated us for this work.

Obviously, only 3D simulations can hope to reproduce the obliquity of the features in some jets observed with very high angular resolution, e.g. NGC6251, 3C66B, M87, etc During the course of this work, we will assume a sequence of oblique shocks along the jet axis (as depicted in figure 4), following the reconfinement mechanism (Sanders, 1983). Sanders showed by the method of characteristics, that the reconfinement process is a typical property of jets observed transversely. The internal pressure in a free jet decreases rapidly with distance from the AGN nucleus, and therefore the jet would be expected to come into pressure equilibrium with the ambient medium. The reconfinement shocks allow the jet flow to adjust to the outside pressure staying supersonic. These shocks shocks are almost periodically repeated, contacting the jet circumference and they are mostly subluminal as long as the flow stays supersonic. The reconfinement mechanism and the solutions of a hydrodynamic wind (e.g. Fukue Okada 1990) show that a jet flow goes through multiple critical points, passing from subsonic to supersonic and vice versa several times, with formation of shock structures. This mechanism is actually an attempt to recover the strength of a weak jet flow, which would mean that one would have repeatedly formations of superluminal to subluminal shocks and so forth, until the critical point were the outside pressure totally overpowers the pressure of the jet in the deceleration phase. Observations of polarized synchrotron radiation in AGN jets (e.g. Agudo et al., 2001), indicate stationary conical components in the observer frame in the parsec-scale jets, which could be identified as recollimation shocks. We note that systems of repeated oblique (conical) shocks are observed in jet structures at vastly discrepant spatial resolutions, like in the radio galaxy NGC6251 (Bridle Perley 1984), interpreting this as a quasi-self-similar pattern, with the local scales all proportional to radial distance along the jet.
Specifically we assume that particle acceleration can take place in such internal shocks as theorized or observed in e.g. PKS 1510-089, NGC6251, etc.

sources.summary: The merged 1.4 GHz map of NGC 6251 resembles lower-frequency WSRT structure and supports a possible double-double radio galaxy interpretation.

quotes.quote: GRG J1632+8232 The overall morphology of NGC 6251 at 1.4 GHz (lower panel of Fig. 14) resembles that observed by Mack et al. (1997) with the WSRT at much lower frequency of 325 MHz. The NVSS data alone suggest that we see a double-double structure produced by this radio galaxy. A more extended diffuse radio lobe in the eastern side of the merged map and coaxiality of both the outer and inner structures could support such a scenario. Especially, that the outer structures seem to have steeper spectral indices, as visible in the multifrequency maps shown by Mack et al. (1997). An inspection of the NVSS data showed that 51 of the flux has been lost due to interferometric observations when compared with the merged map.

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 6251 significantly, finds its spectrum extends to 60-70 GeV, and argues a log-parabolic model better explains its particle acceleration.

quotes.quote: NGC 6251: Detected with 32.0 , its spectrum extends up to (60-70) GeV, but the PL model fails to explain the data above 1 GeV. The results of fitting with alternative models, LP (blue) and PLEC ( =1 , red), are shown in Fig. sed_all (). The data are better explained by LP with =2.29 0.05 and =0.1 0.03 . Among the considered radio galaxies, only the spectrum of NGC 6251 is described by LP, indicating that perhaps a different process is responsible for the particle acceleration. As shown by massaro , the spectra well described by the LP are formed when the jet electrons are accelerated stochastically.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 6251 as a 2 Mpc giant radio galaxy with a prominent blowtorch jet, counterjet, VLBI-studied collimation, misalignment, and relativistic beaming.

quotes.quote: NGC 6251 is a GRG with a size of 2 Mpc growing in a relatively dense environment, having a prominent 200 kpc jet Waggett77 , also referred to as `blowtorch jet'. Its counterjet of 50 kpc was detected using the VLA Perley84 . The jet has been studied in detail from radio to x-ray wavelengths e.g. Perley84,mack97,Evans05 . DaytonJones86 carried out detailed study of NGC 6251 using VLBI techniques, and observed that the nuclear jet is well collimated and like GRQ 4C34.74, NGC 6251 also shows a misalignment of 5 between its 4 mas inner jet and kpc-scale jet. The large jet to counterjet brightness ratio in NGC 6251 at smaller as well as longer scales can be explained by relativistic beaming.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 6251 as a nearby FRI radio galaxy associated with an EGRET source, using an XMM-Newton pointing and favoring an absorbed broken power-law model.

quotes.quote: This EGRET source has been associated by Mukherjee et al. (2002) with the nearby FRI radio galaxy NGC 6251 ( z=0.02471 ). Later studies supported this conclusion, e.g. Sowards-Emmerd et al. (2003), Chiaberge et al. (2003), Guainazzi et al. (2003), Foschini et al. (2005). There is one XMM-Newton pointing available which lasted 50 ks, but heavy contamination with soft-proton flares strongly reduced the effective exposure on the three detectors (see Table tab:log ). The best fit model is an absorbed broken power law model. The addition of a thermal plasma to the single power law does not provide an improvement with respect to the broken power law model.

sources.summary: The FRI radio galaxy NGC 6251 has compact and extended X-ray emission, including a weak HRI component coincident with a bright knot at the jet base.

quotes.quote: NGC 6251 This FRI radio galaxy shows both extended and compact emission in the PSPC observation (Birkinshaw Worrall 1993; Worrall Birkinshaw 1994). The HRI observation detects mainly the compact core, but there is also a weak but significant detection of a component 25 arcsec to the NW of the core, coincident with a bright knot in the base of the jet (Perley, Bridle Willis 1984). [ This component is not to be confused with the possible jet-related X-ray emission claimed by Mack, Kerp Klein (1997), which is at much larger distances from the nucleus and is not visible on the HRI image. ] The knot contributes only 10 5 counts to the total.

sources.summary: NGC 6251 has Mpc-scale radio lobes exceeding the ACIS array, detected jet emission, a corrected piled-up core spectrum, and AGN power-law plus thermal fits.

quotes.quote: NGC 6251 ( z=0.024 ) : The very large (Mpc-scale) radio lobes of this radio galaxy dwarf the entire ACIS array, and the 1/8-size S3 subarray contains emission only from the central core and along the jet evans05 . We detect some emission associated with the jet to the northwest of the galaxy along the chip. A bright readout streak has been removed, and we correct the spectrum of the central PSF for pileup. The spectrum of the AGN is fit well by either a power law with < 0.5 and a thermal model or a slightly absorbed power law with = 1.5 and a thermal model.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC6251, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC6251 (Fig. fig:NGC6251 ) is a luminous early-type galaxy, although its core cannot be resolved in our data. The slight ``knee" in the profile at 50 may indicate an outer disk, and a fit that includes a disk indeed produces a decrease in the ^2 . Yet, neither the ellipticity nor the isophotal shape hint at the presence of a disk, and the residual likewise not suggest significant mismatch at intermediate or large radii.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6251 as a comparison radio galaxy whose parabolic jet profile matches the force-free model and resembles NGC 315 and M87, though with a different break location.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:jet_width_comparison , we compare the jet radius of NGC 315 as a function of distance with the nearby FR I radio galaxies M87 AN2012, Doeleman2012, NA2013, Hada2013, Hada2016, Akiyama2015, Nakamura2018 and NGC 6251 Tseng2016 . We also show the steady axisymmetric force-free electrodynamic (FFE) solution Narayan2007, Tchekhovskoy2008 for the outermost poloidal field line anchored to the black hole event horizon on the equatorial plane with =0.75 is related to the radial power-law index in the poloidal flux function of the approximate FFE solution, which describes the asymptotic shape of the field line (see Narayan2007, Tchekhovskoy2008, Nakamura2018 for more details). , which describes the observed parabolic jet collimation profiles of M87 and NGC 6251 well Nakamura2018 . The jet collimation profiles of NGC 315, NGC 6251, and M87 in the parabolic regions (before the jet collimation breaks) are remarkably similar, although the locations of the breaks are different.

The break locations appear to be smaller than the Bondi radii (or ) in NGC 6251, NGC 4261, NGC 1052 Tseng2016, Nakahara2018, Nakahara2020 , while it is possibly much larger in Cygnus A No transition from a parabolic to conical geometry was observed at distances 10^9 \ R_g . Nakahara2019 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 6251's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 6251 is an elliptical galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0247 ( D 112 Mpc) hosting one of the largest radio sources in the sky waggett_ngc_1977 and classified as FR I/II. It consists of giant double lobes with an S-shaped geometry (see migliori_implications_2011 for a recent SED study). Its nucleus is active but with uncertain classification.

sources.summary: NGC 6251 is discussed as an intermediate FR I/FR II LERG lacking clear Fe-K emission, with hard X-rays attributed to synchrotron self-Compton emission.

quotes.quote: Often, the jet origin of the unresolved core emission is claimed based solely on the modeling of broad-band spectral energy distributions (SEDs; see, e.g., chi03 and fos05 for the case of NGC 6251), and therefore the alternative possibilities, such as a disk/corona emission, cannot be ruled out.

All these radio galaxies are of the FR I type, with the exception of 3C 111 and NGC 6251 which display classical FR II and intermediate FR I/FR II large-scale radio morphologies, respectively e.g., gli04,sam04,gra12 .

The X-ray spectra for Cen A abd10a , M 87 abd09b , NGC 1275 abd09a , and NGC 6251 mig11 are hard ( _ X <2 ), indicating they originate from the SSC component, although M 87 likely has some synchrotron contribution as well abd09b .

sources.summary: The catalog reports no convincing extended-emission case for NGC 6251, notes its earlier EGRET detection, and says its Fermi position shifted toward the western radio lobe.

quotes.quote: There is no convincing case of extended emission in other radio galaxies with relatively large radio extensions, such as Cen B kat13 , NGC 6251 tak12 , and Fornax A.
NGC 6251 (one square degree in solid angle) was also detected by EGRET muk02 . Its location shifted between 1LAC and 2LAC towards the western radio lobe.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6251 as a radio galaxy similar to Centaurus A and Fornax A, with very large lobes and plausible UHECR magnetic deflection geometry.

quotes.quote: The radio galaxy NGC 6251 is intriguingly similar to Centaurus A and Fornax A in that it has extremely large lobes (linear extent 2 Mpc) with large total energy contents waggett_ngc_1977 and is thought to be an extended ray source takeuchi_suzaku_2012 . Similarly, the restarted radio galaxy B2 0258 + 35 hosted by NGC 1167 shulevski_recurrent_2012,brienza_duty_2018 has giant lobes 240 kpc across, and shows evidence for past/ongoing merger activity emonts_large-scale_2010,struve_cold_2010 , which may have triggered a powerful past jet episode shulevski_recurrent_2012 . The offsets from the TA hotspots for NGC 6251 and B2 0258 + 35 are 48.7^ and 73.8^ , respectively. Thus, the required magnetic deflections are large, but 48.7^ is possible for an R 10 EV CR from a source at NGC 6251's position and distance in either the Galactic magnetic field see their figure 2 farrar_galactic_2016 or an extragalactic field of 1 nG in accordance with results from bray_upper_2018 .

sources.summary: NGC 6251 is compared with NGC 4261 as a similar radio galaxy with a dusty disk, symmetric jets, inverted radio core, stronger X-ray and H emission, and comparable black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: It is also interesting to note that NGC 6251 is quite similar to NGC 4261 in that both of them have a hundred-pc scale dusty disk, large scale symmetric radio jets and an inverted radio spectrum at the core (Melia, Liu Fatuzzo 2002). Compared with NGC 4261, NGC 6251 is about 3 times further away from us, its X-ray luminosity is 30 times stronger, and its broad H line flux is about 100 times bigger. HST observations suggest that there is a supermassive black hole of 6 10^8 , which is very close to the black hole mass in NGC 4261.

gmw_00000246.name: NGC 6300
gmw_00000246.overview: NGC 6300 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1501.02022, arXiv:1811.11386] hosting an obscured Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1501.02022] that exhibits changing-look X-ray behavior [arXiv:1501.02022, arXiv:1505.01166]. It features an extended atomic gas disk with a southwest tail [arXiv:1811.11386], an inner ring [arXiv:2304.00515], and an ionized gas outflow [arXiv:1902.02373, arXiv:2107.07635].

sources.summary: The paper studies the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 6300 in the near-infrared, finding an off-center nuclear continuum, a black-hole mass estimate, ionized outflow, and hot circumnuclear dust.

quotes.quote: We present a near-infrared study of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 6300, based on subarcsecond images and long slit spectroscopy obtained with Flamingos-2 at Gemini South. We have found that the peak of the nuclear continuum emission in the K_s band and the surrounding nuclear disk are 25 pc off-center with respect to the center of symmetry of the larger scale circumnuclear disk, suggesting that this black hole is still not fixed in the galaxy potential well. The molecular gas radial velocity curve yields a central black hole upper mass estimation of M_ SMBH ^ upper =(6 2) 10^ 7 . The Pa emission line has a strongly asymmetric profile with a blueshifted broad component that we associate with a nuclear ionized gas outflow.

We have found in the K_s band spectra that the slope of the continuum becomes steeper with increasing radii, which can be explained as the presence of large amounts of hot dust not only in the nucleus but also in the circumnuclear region up to =27 pc. In fact, the nuclear red excess obtained after subtracting the stellar contribution resembles to that of a blackbody with temperatures around 1200 K. This evidence supports the idea that absorbing material located around the nucleus, but not close enough to be the torus of the unified model, could be responsible for at least part of the nuclear obscuration in this Seyfert 2 nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 6300 among the INTEGRAL Seyfert 2 targets, extracts ISGRI spectra, and finds no high-energy cutoff over the observed range.

quotes.quote: We present results on approximately one year of INTEGRAL observations of six AGN detected during the regular scans of the Galactic Plane. The sample is composed by five Seyfert 2 objects ( MCG --05--23--16 , NGC 4945 , the Circinus galaxy, NGC 6300 , ESO 103--G35 ) and the radio galaxy Centaurus A. The continuum emission of each of these sources is well represented by a highly absorbed ( N_ H >10^ 22 cm^ 2 ) power law, with average spectral index = 1.9 0.3 .

Because of the faint nature of the sources in the sample, we chose to extract the ISGRI spectra for NGC 4945 , the Circinus galaxy, NGC 6300 and ESO 103--G35 from the count rate and variance mosaic images at the position of the source, which in all cases corresponds to the brightest pixel in the 30--50 keV band. MCG --05--23--16 was never in the field of view of JEM-X and NGC 4945 , NGC 6300 and ESO 103--G35 were not detected. INTEGRAL confirms the presence of a cut-off at 50 keV for the Circinus galaxy, a lower limit of 130 keV for NGC 4945 and the lack of this feature for NGC 6300, in agreement with what has been found in PDS spectra.

sources.summary: NGC 6300 is a barred ring spiral with an obscured Seyfert 2 nucleus, changing X-ray obscuration, a water maser, and an ENLR traceable beyond its core.

quotes.quote: NGC 6300: This barred ring spiral galaxy has a heavily obscured Seyfert 2 nucleus with deep NaD absorption. Long slit spectroscopy obtained by Fraquelli03 . This galaxy changes from Compton-thick to Compton-thin at X-ray wavelengths over timescales of a few years Matt03 , and contains an H _2 O maser Greenhill03 . The ENLR is approximately 7 5 arcsec, or 0.5 0.3 kpc in diameter in \ but can be traced further out in the NE-SW axis in . A number of \ region complexes are scattered across the WiFeS field of view.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6300 as a barred spiral type 2 Seyfert with dust lanes, radio nucleus, changing-look behavior, and soft and hard X-ray normalization changes.

quotes.quote: NGC 6300 NGC 6300 is a barred spiral galaxy, whose type 2 Seyfert classification at optical frequencies was derived from the data reported in . The HST image shows dust lanes (see Appendix multiimages ). A nuclear counterpart was found at radio frequencies, without any jet structure ryder1996 . NGC 6300 was classified as a changing-look AGN, observed in the Compton thick state with RXTE in 1997 and in the Compton thin state with BeppoSAX in 1999 guainazzi2002a . The galaxy was observed once with XMM -Newton in 2001, and five times with Chandra during 2009.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 6300 has an extended HI disc, barred inner ring, ordered but warped HI rotation, and an HI ring at the OLR with SFR and HI peaks.

quotes.quote: NGC 6300: The bottom left panels in Fig. fig:mosaic4 show the DSS optical composite image and the 1 velocity map of NCG 6300. Comparing the optical image to the 1 distribution, it is clear that this galaxy has a very extended 1 disc. From the optical image it is clear that this galaxy has a bar which is surrounded by an inner ring. The 1 gas is observed to trace the inner ring, where the 1 gas density also peaks. The velocity maps shows ordered rotational motion of the 1 gas throughout the disc, with some warping observed towards the extreme ends of the 1 disc. While the 1 gas distribution in NGC 6300 is very extended, we do observe an 1 ring coinciding with the predicted location of the OLR radius.

sources.summary: The paper finds an asymmetric iron K line in NGC 6300, with a red wing fitted by a strongly relativistic DISKLINE profile at small radius and 30-degree inclination.

quotes.quote: NGC 6300: In Fig. fig:n6300 we plot the residuals of the best fit of NGC 6300, obtained with model C, but without the Gaussian component that fits the iron K line. The asymmetry of the line is apparent: a clear ``red wing'' extends down to 4 keV, while a broad blue wing is absent. We fitted this excess with a relativistic line model. We used the DISKLINE model in XSPEC, leaving all the parameters free. The best fit is obtained with a strongly relativistic profile: the best fit inner radius of the emitting region is 6.5 Shwarzchild radii; the inclination is 30^o .

sources.summary: NGC 6300 is discussed as a changing-look AGN with transitions between Compton-thick and Compton-thin states and strong luminosity variability across X-ray measurements.

quotes.quote: NGC 6300 This is one of a sample of changing-look \ AGN whose spectra exhibit transitions between Compton-thick and Compton-thin obscuration on timescales of years matt03_changinglook . It was found to be reflection-dominated in 1997, but has since been in a Compton-thin state \ and \ analysis; guainazzi02 . Assuming a Compton-thin column of = 2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 (from the \ analysis of matsumoto04 ) obscuring a PL with =2.42 ( beckmann06 ) and normalizing to JEM-X and ISGRI 2--100 keV fluxes of the latter publication, the implied intrinsic log L_ 2-10 =42.76 . But, in addition to its changing-look nature, the source also exhibits large flux variability awaki06 and a much lower luminosity of log L_ 2-10 =41.8 was derived by matsumoto04 from their \ analysis.

sources.summary: The paper detects polarized broad H-alpha for NGC 6300, notes its broad-line fit has relatively low BIC support, and contrasts the result with earlier lower-S/N spectropolarimetry.

quotes.quote: We detect broad H and H components in polarized light for 10 targets, and just broad H for NGC 5793 and NGC 6300, with line widths ranging between 2100 and 9600 km s ^ 1 .
The majority of the detections are very strong, although for NGC 4941, NGC 5793, and NGC 6300 the BICs are considerably lower than for the rest of the galaxies.

The broadest components that we measured are those of NGC 2110, NGC 3393, NGC 4388, and NGC 6300 (FWHMs 4200--9600 km s ^ 1 ). These broad lines were already reported by Moran07 and Tran10 for NGC 2110 and by Young96 for NGC 4388. A polarized spectrum of NGC 6300 was presented by Lumsden04 , but they did not find any evidence for broad H , although they could not rule out the presence of a HBLR in this galaxy because of the low S/N of the data.

sources.summary: The paper reports a significant disk-reflection fit improvement for NGC 6300, presents its unfolded spectrum, and suggests an ionized-disk reflection component there for the first time.

quotes.quote: Adding the disk-reflection component to the best-fit models obtained in Section sec:base_model , we find that fits are significantly improved at > 99 confidence level in four sources (2MASX J1200+0648, Fairall 49, NGC 526A, and NGC 6300). Their unfolded spectra that give a smaller ^2 value between the assumptions of =10 or 100 are shown in Figure fig:disk . Except for NGC 6300, the disk-reflection component better improves the fit at energies below 10 keV than above 10 keV.
Possible presence of an ionized-disk reflection component in 2MASX J1200+648 and NGC 6300 is suggested here for the first time, while it was reported for Fairall 49 by , consistent with our result.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6300 as a Seyfert barred spiral with an extended southwest atomic-gas tail, interpreted as possible ongoing gas accretion from the intergalactic medium.

quotes.quote: NGC 6300 is classified as SB(rs)b and is known to be a Seyfert type 2 galaxy. Ryder et al. ( ryder96 ) studied the 1 distribution in NGC 6300 using the ATCA and find an extended tail like feature in the South-west corner of the galaxy. We also notice this feature as shown in Fig. fig:galaxy-maps . However, since there are no nearby neighbours that are likely to have caused this tidal tail like feature, Ryder et al. conclude that this could be a sign that NGC 6300 is still accreting 1 gas from the IGM.

sources.summary: The paper finds bright extended mid-IR emission in NGC 6300, but as the exception it is along the equatorial torus and host-galaxy dust rather than the polar cone.

quotes.quote: The PSF-subtracted images of six out of the twelve galaxies present bright extended mid-IR emission (Fig. fig:midIRimages ). These are NGC 1365, NGC 3227, NGC 5506, NGC 5643, NGC 6300, and NGC 7582. The contours show that the extended emission is detected up to at least a 20 _ bk level. For NGC 4388 we also detected extended mid-IR emission at a lower significance but in a similar orientation as that derived by using a different dataset. The sizes of the extended mid-IR emission range from approximately 50 pc in NGC 6300 to 190 pc in NGC 5506.

In NGC 1365, NGC 3227, and NGC 7582 as well as the inner regions of NGC 5506, the extended mid-IR emission appears to be approximately perpendicular (in projection) to the extended ALMA 870 m continuum emission. The latter for these four galaxies is identified as the torus. In NGC 5643, the orientation of the extended mid-IR emission is mostly along PA_ MIR = 60-70 ^ , which is nearly perpendicular (in projection) to the nearly north-south ALMA 870 m continuum emission. Finally, in NGC 6300 the extended mid- and far-IR emission show similar orientations, and thus the extended mid-IR emission is along the equatorial direction of the torus.

NGC 3227, NGC 4388 and NGC 7582 as well as NGC 1068 show evidence of molecular outflows on physical scales larger than the torus up to a few hundred parsecs, see and GB21 Davies2014, GarciaBurillo2019, DominguezFernandez2020 , which are more likely due to the interaction between the AGN wind and/or a radio jet if present and molecular gas in the disk of the host galaxy. NGC 5643 and NGC 5506 appear to have extended both polar and equatorial mid-IR emission. In NGC 6300 the mid-IR emission might be due to dust in the equatorial direction of the torus as well as in the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 6300's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: This Sy 2 shows the and SER more extended to the NE, approximately perpendicularly to the PMA, where blueshifts are observed in both emission lines. Blueshifts are observed also at the nucleus, while to the SW, both [ N ii ] and [ O iii ] show redshifts. The highest values of and gas density are observed to the SW. As these structures are observed perpendicularly to the PMA, we interpret them as due to outflows , although the value remain below the 600 threshold.

sources.summary: NGC 6300 has reported molecular or ionized outflow signatures in the literature, but this paper obtains a low score and notes no molecular-hydrogen outflow signature.

quotes.quote: Davies2014 find molecular outflow signatures ( S(1)H _ 2 (1--0) ) in NGC 6300 and NGC 7743 . Our s are rather low with 1.56 for NGC 6300 and 0.33 for NGC 7743. For NGC 6300, NGC6300Gaspar2019 interpret a broad component in ionized gas as a central outflow, but they find no outflow signatures in molecular hydrogen observations. Stone2016 analysed molecular OH emission of several PHANGS galaxies ( NGC 1068 , NGC 1365 , NGC 1566 , NGC 4579 , NGC 4941 , NGC 6300 , NGC 7465 ). They find no evidence for inflows in these galaxies.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 6300's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 6300 is a low-inclined barred spiral galaxy at a low Galactic latitude and a distance of D= 14.3 3.3 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) hosting a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It features a slightly resolved radio core at arcsecond resolution with extension in southern direction (PA 190 ; morganti_radio_1999 ) and water maser emission greenhill_discovery_2003 . After , NGC 6300 was observed with bendo_infrared_2002 and /IRAC, IRS and MIPS.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6300 as discrepant relative to previous joint modeling, arguing the high lower limit is likely spurious and lower fitted cutoffs imply high optical depth.

quotes.quote: NGC 6300 -- The only apparent discrepancy in constraints for this target is with respect to joint modeling of \ and \ spectra by molina+2013 . The high lower limit ( > 520 keV) is based on their modeling with a free \ parameter and is likely spurious. From fits with \ fixed at 0, 1, and 2, they derive \ in the range of 40--90 keV, all of which correspond to _e > 3 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 6300 among barred systems, reports regions of high cloud virial parameter, and finds a high cutoff mass that shallows its truncated mass distribution.

quotes.quote: Bar - Six of our 10 galaxies have stellar bars (NGC 1637, NGC 2903, NGC 3627, NGC 5068, NGC 5643, NGC 6300).
NGC 4826 represents a unique starburst case and shows high _ vir , NGC 2903 and to a lesser extent NGC 3621, NGC 3627, NGC 6300 also show regions with high _ vir .

For NGC 5643 and NGC 6300, the cutoff mass in the truncated mass distribution happens at high mass ( >10^7 M_ ), leading to a slightly shallower slope in the power-law component of the mass distribution.

gmw_00000247.name: NGC 6810
gmw_00000247.overview: NGC 6810 is an edge-on, unbarred spiral galaxy located approximately 28.6 Mpc away [arXiv:astro-ph/0312268, arXiv:1310.2770]. While historically classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy, its nuclear emission is also characterized as an H II region or an active galactic nucleus transition zone driven by star formation [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:2301.02252].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6810 as an unbarred spiral with a zero central major-axis gas gradient, a plateau-like gas rotation curve, and non-zero minor-axis velocities attributed to decoupled off-plane gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 949, NGC 2460, NGC 2541, and NGC 6810 are unbarred spiral galaxies which belong to a sample of 16 bright ( B_T 13.5 ) and nearby ( V_ 3500 ) spirals studied by Coccato et al. (2004) to search for a circumnuclear Keplerian disk (Bertola et al. 1998; Funes et al. 2002). The main properties of the sample galaxies are summarized in Table tab:sample . The major-axis rotation curve of their gaseous component is characterized by a remarkable zero-velocity plateau.

The central velocity gradient along the major axis is zero (NGC 949, NGC 2541, NGC 6810) or at least less steep (NGC 2460) than that we measured for radii larger than 3'' . Further out the gas velocity increases to reach a maximum and then remains constant out to the last observed radius. Non-zero gas velocities are measured along the minor axis of all the sample galaxies, in spite of which would be expected if the gas traced the circular velocity in the disk plane.
We attribute the minor-axis velocity gradients to the presence of kinematically-decoupled component which is not rotating in the plane of the stellar disk.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 6810's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 6810 is an edge-on spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 28.6 5.2 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a poorly studied active nucleus. Initially, it was classified optically as a Sy 2 kirhakos_x-ray_1990 , while it turned out later to be either an H II or H II/AGN transition or composite nucleus strickland_new_2007,brightman_nature_2008,brightman_xmm-newton_2011-1,yuan_role_2010 . In particular, find no evidence for an AGN in X-rays and based on other diagnostics.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6810 as a non-Seyfert source with weak fluxes, mixed diagnostic classifications, and emission that AGN grids explain poorly compared with star formation.

quotes.quote: We note that these targets agree well with the relation, with NGC 6810 showing the weakest fluxes.

Two of the non-Sy, Mrk 897 and NGC 6810, have log_ 10 / of 1.38 and 1.2 , respectively and they fall in the AGN-dominated area of the first two diagrams (i.e., / \ versus / , / \ versus / ) while in the composite (AGN and star formation) region in the / \ versus / [ Ar ii ] 6.9 \ diagram.

Our AGN grid only agrees well for targets in the / \ versus the mid-IR ratios planes, with the exceptions of two non-Sy (Mrk 897 and NGC 6810), whose emission is explained well by star formation (e.g., bottom-right panel of Fig. fig:mir_opt_appendix ).

gmw_00000248.name: NGC 6814
gmw_00000248.overview: NGC 6814 is a low-inclination, grand-design barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2308.15543, arXiv:1612.02810] classified as a Seyfert 1.5 [arXiv:2308.15543, arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a highly variable active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0606488, arXiv:0902.2777] that exhibits strong X-ray, ultraviolet, and optical flux variations [arXiv:2308.15543, arXiv:astro-ph/0606488].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the 2016 XMM-Newton eclipse of NGC 6814, finding absorber column and covering changes inconsistent with one homogeneous cloud and suggestive of multiple clouds.

quotes.quote: Here we study the eclipse data from the 2016 XMM-Newton observation of NGC 6814 using a colour-colour analysis. Colours (i.e. hardness ratios) can provide the advantage of better time-resolution over spectral analysis alone. Colour-colour grids are constructed to examine the effects of different parameters on the observed spectral variability during the eclipse. Consistent with previous spectral analysis, the variations are dominated by changes in the column density and covering fraction of the absorber. However, during maximum eclipse the behaviour of the absorber changes.

Just after ingress, the eclipse is described by changes in column density and covering fraction, but prior to egress, the variations are dominated by changes in column density alone. Simulations are carried out to consider possible absorber geometries that might produce this behaviour. The behaviour is inconsistent with a single, homogeneous cloud, but simulations suggest that multiple clouds, perhaps embedded in a highly ionised halo, could reproduce the results.

sources.summary: The paper revisits NGC 6814’s 2016 X-ray occultation, arguing its HR-CR behavior requires clumpy Compton-thin and Compton-thick absorbers near the BLR and a compact corona.

quotes.quote: A unique X-ray occultation event in NGC 6814 during an observation in 2016 has been reported, providing useful information of the absorber and the corona. We revisit the event with the aid of the hardness ratio (HR) -- count rate (CR) plot and comparison with two other absorption-free XMM exposures in 2009 and 2021. NGC 6814 exhibits a clear ``softer-when-brighter" variation pattern during the exposures, but the 2016 exposure significantly deviates from the other two in the HR -- CR plot.

Recently, Gallo_2021 (hereafter Gallo_2021 ) reported a highly distinct occultation event in NGC 6814 captured in 2016 with a high-quality observation. The ingress , eclipse and egress periods are evident in both the count rate (CR) light curves and hardness ratio (HR) curves, while a transiting partial-covering absorber is also revealed with time-resolved spectral fitting analyses. Gallo_2021 interpreted this event as a single Compton-thin cloud eclipsing the central engine; under certain assumptions, properties of the eclipsing cloud (e.g. location, size, density, velocity), along with the size of the X-ray corona, have been derived.

For comparison, the _ line of the broad H of NGC 6814 is 1918 36 km s^ 1 Bentz_2009 . Therefore, the absorber is likely located in the high-ionization broad line region (BLR) or further inner see Figure 1.1 in Gallo_2023 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6814 as a variable Seyfert whose uncontaminated INTEGRAL detection above 10 keV separates it from RX J1940.2-1025 and whose ASCA/INTEGRAL comparison shows strong flux changes.

quotes.quote: Given the angular separation of 37' between NGC 6814 and the cataclysmic variable RX J1940.2-1025, previous X-ray data of this AGN, obtained with non-imaging collimated instruments, were generally contaminated. In particular, a past single observation at high energy by OSSE b40 is clearly incorrect due to the inability of the instrument to resolve NGC 6814 from the Galactic object. Thanks to the imaging capability of IBIS, the detection of NGC 6814 reported in this paper is the first reliable one above 10 keV. In figure fig1 , the IBIS/ISGRI 20-100 keV map of the sky region around NGC 6814 and RX J1940.2-1025 is shown; this figure indicates the ability of INTEGRAL to discriminate between the AGNs and the Galactic source.

NGC 6814 is a Seyfert galaxy which shows strong variability in its X-ray flux over short (hours, b33 ) as well as long (days and years, b24 ) timescales. The constant C , introduced to take into account a possible cross-calibration mismatch between the two instruments and/or flux changes between the two observations, gives a value of 16 ^ +9 _ 5 . Since from previous works b36, b20 we know that the cross-calibration constant between ASCA/GIS and INTEGRAL/ISGRI is close to 1, we can conclude that, as expected, there was a strong variation between the ASCA and INTEGRAL observations, with INTEGRAL providing a higher flux than ASCA. It is worth noting that since we are dealing with the first high energy data not contaminated by the nearby Galactic source, this flux variation is completely due to the Seyfert galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes variable NGC 6814 light curves, a difficult two-peak emission-line fit, an integrated response peaking near eight days, and a clear isolated velocity-resolved response.

quotes.quote: NGC 6814 Results for NGC 6814 are presented in Fig. fig:result-ngc6814 . The light curves of NGC 6814 show an appreciable amount of variability. The continuum V band light curve has a clear broad peak at HJD 2454000 = 560 days after which it drops off to a slowly rising plateau that extends to shortly after HJD 2454000 = 600 days . The emission line light curve has the same over-all trend as the continuum, but instead of having a plateau after the initial peak, it rises to almost the same level as the initial peak.

The integrated response function peaks around 8 days and has broad flat wings to either side (Fig. fig:result-ngc6814 , lower left panel). The median time delay of = 7.3^ +1.2 _ 1.0 days is consistent with results from cross-correlation ( = 6.5^ +0.9 _ 1.0 days) and ( = 7.4^ +0.1 _ 0.1 days). The velocity-resolved response map for NGC 6814 shows a clear isolated response peak centred on the emission line (Fig. fig:result-ngc6814 , upper right panel).

sources.summary: The paper presents a high-cadence Swift campaign of NGC 6814, finding unusual X-ray/UV/optical variability, flattened lags, and evidence for a non-standard accretion disc.

quotes.quote: We present the first results of a high--cadence Swift monitoring campaign ( 3-4 visits per day for 75 days) of the Seyfert 1.5 galaxy NGC 6814 characterizing its variability throughout the X--ray and UV/optical wavebands. Structure function analysis reveals an X--ray power law ( =0.5^ +0.2 _ 0.1 ) that is significantly flatter than the one measured in the UV/optical bands ( 1.5 ), suggesting different physical mechanisms driving the observed variability in each emission region. The structure function break--time is consistent across the UV/optical bands ( 2.3 d ), suggesting a very compact emission region in the disc.

NGC 6814 z=0.00522 ; Springob+2005 is a Seyfert 1.5 VeronCettyVeron2006 hosted by a nearly face--on grand--design spiral galaxy see Figure 1 of Bentz+2019 Image also available at https://www.nasa.gov/image-feature/goddard/2016/hubble-spies-a-spiral-snowflake https://www.nasa.gov/image-feature/goddard/2016/hubble-spies-a-spiral-snowflake . Its SMBH mass is estimated to be M_ BH = 1.85 0.35 10^ 7 M_ based on H measurements Bentz+2009 , though a range of M_ BH 0.55-6.1 10^ 7 M_ have also been reported using a variety of H and He emission lines Bentz+2010 , with dynamical BLR modelling finding M_ BH =2.6^ +1.9 _ 0.9 10^ 6 M_ Pancoast+2014 . Recently, we presented evidence of a partial X--ray eclipse in NGC 6814 that lasted 42 ks GalloGonzalezMiller2021 .

sources.summary: NGC 6814 is described as an unabsorbed, highly variable Seyfert 1.5; the authors model spectral curvature to reconcile hard and soft X-ray observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 6814 This source is an unabsorbed Sy 1.5, with < 20.7 reynolds97 . It is known to be highly variable, even after accounting for a neighbouring CV which contaminated early observations e.g. see the results of long-term \ monitoring described by mukai03 . Using the PL model with =2.48 from beckmann06 , and extrapolating from their \ 20--100 fluxes to below 10 keV suggests that JEM-X should have detected the source, which it did not. Instead, some spectral curvature probably needs to be invoked. Including a simple \ pexrav parametrization with =1.6 , R=1 and E_ cut =150 keV agrees with 20--40 keV and 40--100 keV fluxes of bird07 , agrees with the mean published \ observations mentioned above, and also matches the <10 keV photon-index found from \ analysis of data in the Tartarus database.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6814 as a low-inclination barred/ringed SABb galaxy where the authors prefer an abnormally low M/L interpretation after comparing photometric and H I rotation constraints.

quotes.quote: NGC 6814 is an SABb-type galaxy with a ring. The inclination of NGC 6814 is low (the photometric axial ratio gives i=17^o ). However, i=8^o is required for v_ max determined from the rotation curve in the H I line to become approximately equal to v_ max disk . Such a low inclination does not correspond to the photometric estimates (see Fig. 2). The presence of a bar hardly may change the result, because its presence is evident only in the central region ( R < 1 kpc), while the disk component of the rotation curve has a maximum at R 3 kpc. Therefore, the conclusion about an abnormally low M/L of this galaxy remains preferable.

sources.summary: The paper measures a sharp 9.7-day JAVELIN lag for NGC 6814, matching prior work and reflecting strong light-curve variability.
