quotes.quote: NGC 6814 Unlike others, the DCF of NGC 6814 shows a clear trend, but a peak seems not to be defined well with a double peak at 4 days and 16 days. On the other hand, JAVELIN lag distribution is sharp with a peak at 9.7^ +0.4 _ 0.4 days. It is close to the mean value of double peak in DCF. The time lag of NGC 6814 from 2010ApJ...716..993B is 9.6^ +2.2 _ 1.7 days, in an excellent agreement with our result. Like NGC 4593, the small error in the time lag peak reflects the strongest variation of the light curve (0.06 mag, excess variability).

sources.summary: The paper finds only faint or no extended mid-IR emission in NGC 6814, compact NLR/coronal-line structure, little nuclear molecular gas, and blowout-region conditions.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies, NGC 6814, NGC 7314, and NGC 7465, only show evidence of faint extended emission at 3-5 _ bk levels. Finally, the PSF-subtracted images of NGC 4941 and NGC 7213 do not show evidence of extended emission.

NGC 4941, NGC 7465 and NGC 7314 have extended optical NLRs, which are detected with optical IFU observations and/or HST imaging see and Sousa et al. in preparation, respectively ErrozFerrer2019, Ferruit2000 . In NGC 6814, the NLR and the coronal line region are rather compact and there is evidence for ionized outflows on these scales 1 , see MuellerSanchez2011 . In these four galaxies we do not detect extended mid-IR emission.

NGC 6814 and NGC 7314 are close to the blowout region, where polar dusty outflows should not be prominent. The CO(3-2) map of NGC 6814 of GB21 (their Fig. 10) shows that there is little molecular gas in the nuclear and circumnuclear regions of this galaxy, in agreement with being in the blowout region and an unresolved mid-IR morphology. NGC 7314, on the other hand, contains more molecular gas with two CO peaks located symmetrically around the AGN in the inner 40 pc (Fig. 13 of GB21). This might indicate that some clearing of molecular gas at the AGN location already took place in the nuclear regions of this galaxy. For this galaxy, the mid-IR emission is mostly unresolved.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6814 as a nearby Seyfert test case to show how aperture size and distance dilute mid-infrared AGN signatures with disk star formation.

quotes.quote: Using MIR observations of the face-on nearby Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 6814, we vary the observing aperture and examine the evolution of the observed AGN/starburst fraction with our MIR diagnostic.

In order to examine how the spatial resolution of a telescope (in our case ISOCAM) may bias the detection of the MIR emission from an AGN and its surrounding star formation regions, we used NGC 6814, a nearby face-on Seyfert 1 galaxy located at 20 Mpc (H _ 0 =75 km ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 )( ), as a test case.

We present in Figure figure1 images of NGC 6814 projected at different distances as well as their corresponding SEDs. Given a fixed aperture of 9'' (3 pixels at 3''/pixel), we note the increase of UIBs associated with star forming regions in the galactic disk by observing distant galaxies. The AGN continuum is still detected at 50 Mpc, but it becomes negligible at 100 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6814 as a barred grand-design Seyfert spiral with H II regions and nuclear dilution in Mg and Fe5270 absorption, followed by moderate radial declines.

quotes.quote: NGC 6814 - SAB(rs)bc This galaxy has a thick bar and a grand design spiral structure (M a rquez et al. 1999), whose spiral arms contain many H ii regions (Gonzalez Delgado et al. 1997). NGC 6814 is another Seyfert galaxy observed by Cid Fernandes et al. (1998). They found that the Mg I+Mg H feature was diluted by 22 in the nuclei, in agreement with a 20 decrease in the EW we observe between 5 and 0 arcsec. The Fe5270 line-strength also appears significantly diluted in the nuclear region. The line strengths of both spectral features show moderate declines with radius.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6814 as an atypical BAT AGN with an unabsorbed power-law fit, strong Fe-K emission, weak soft flux, and large X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 6814 is well fit by an unabsorbed power law but has a strong Fe-K line (EW = 545 eV). There is very little flux in the ASCA observation below 1 keV. The strong emission in Fe K and weak flux at soft energies contribute to give it an unusual location in the plot.
The complex source NGC 6814, for instance, is known to show X-ray variability by at least 10 times over time scales of years 2003ApJ...597..479M .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 6814 as a Seyfert 1.2 and finds its predicted line ratios and BLR extinction consistent with previous AGN continuum-color estimates.

quotes.quote: NGC 6814 : [ O III ] /H = 0.4 in this object, we classify it as a Seyfert 1.2.

The line ratios we observe for the unreddened Seyfert 1s MGC-05--14--012, NGC 3783 and NGC 4593 are in good agreement with these other observational studies; as is the predicted line ratio for NGC 6814, MCG--06--30--015 and MCG-05--23--16, the latter based on the best fit to the H and Paschen lines, and for which we derived a substantial extinction of A_V(BLR) = 7.7 mag.

NGC 6814 : winkler97 estimated A _V = 0.65 0.37 for the nucleus of NGC 6814 based on a comparison of the observed B--V,U--B,V--R and V--I colours of the nuclear continuum to those of unreddened AGN. This is consistent with our estimate of A _V (BLR) = 0.4 0.4 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6814 as a low-inclination late-type Seyfert with a bar and uses WiFeS IFU observations to map its bulge kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 6814 Observations Similar to our sample of galaxies observed with HexPak, NGC 6814 is a nearby, low-inclination, late-type Seyfert galaxy with significant substructure including a bar. Details of the galaxy are shown in Table tab:gal_specs . NGC 6814 was observed with the Wide-Field Spectrograph (WiFeS) in July 2012 under observing program 2120061. WiFeS is an optical, dual-beam, integral-field spectrograph mounted on the Australian National University (ANU) 2.3m telescope at the Siding Spring Observatory Dopita07,Dopita10 . It has a large contiguous 25 38 field of view, and is seeing limited with 1 0 5 spaxels.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 6814 with NRAO and resolved H I work, finding previous measurements mostly consistent and the GBT beam likely sufficient.

quotes.quote: NGC 6814: observed this object with the NRAO 91 m telescope and the 192 channel autocorrelation spectrometer. The low spectral resolution of 22 km s ^ 1 and channel spacing of 11 km s ^ 1 possibly account for the slightly larger value of W _ 20 that they report of 134 km s ^ 1 compared to our value of 105.1 ^ +11.4 _ 8.4 km s ^ 1 . All other previous measurements from , , , are consistent with our measurements. estimate the HI diameter of NGC 6814 to be 7 ' based on their resolved HI map, thus it is likely that the 9 1 GBT beam encompassed the total HI distribution of NGC 6814.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 6814's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 6814 is a face-on grand-design spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0052 ( D 21.2 Mpc) with a Sy 1.5 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . This nucleus is variable by one order of magnitude variable in X-rays koenig_seyfert_1997,mukai_x-ray_2003 and belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. In addition, optical type changes from Sy 1.0 to Sy 1.9 have been reported sekiguchi_spectral_1990 .

sources.summary: The paper confirms gamma-ray emission from NGC 6814, compares two black-hole mass estimates, and rules out an inner hot-flow origin for the observed signal.

quotes.quote: NGC 6814 The ray signal from the direction of NGC 6814 with TS = 25.6 was found by .

For both, the measured F_ 1-10 GeV gives _ 1-10 GeV exceeding the maximum value predicted for the hot flow (in models N with =10^ 3 ), which rules out an origin of the observed ray signal in an inner hot flow.
For the higher value of M , NGC 6814 has a similar _ 1-10 GeV to the ray-loud Seyfert 2 galaxies, a possible explanation is discussed in Sect. sect:discuss .

gmw_00000249.name: NGC 6860
gmw_00000249.overview: NGC 6860 is an inclined barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1006.4523, arXiv:1310.2770] classified as a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1501.02022]. It hosts a Seyfert 1.5 nucleus [arXiv:1006.4523, arXiv:1310.2770] surrounded by an extended narrow-line region and a distorted ring of off-nuclear H II regions [arXiv:1501.02022].

sources.summary: NGC 6860 is presented as a nearby Seyfert 1.5 barred spiral with a complex X-ray spectrum, warm absorption signatures, and an AGN/starburst composite optical nucleus.

quotes.quote: The X-ray spectrum of the Seyfert 1.5 source NGC 6860 is among the most complex of the sources detected in the Swift Burst Alert Telescope all-sky survey. A short XMM-Newton follow-up observation of the source revealed a flat spectrum both above and below 2 keV. To uncover the complexity of the source, in this paper we analyze both a 40 ks Suzaku and a 100 ks XMM-Newton observation of NGC 6860. While the spectral state of the source changed between the newer observations presented here and the earlier short XMM-Newton spectrum -- showing a higher flux and steeper power law component -- the spectrum of NGC 6860 is still complex with clearly detected warm absorption signatures.

NGC 6860 is a southern hemisphere ( = 20:08:46.89, = -61:06:00.70) target optically identified as a Seyfert 1.5 source 1993ApJ...405..186L . The source is nearby ( z = 0.014884 ) and hosted in a barred spiral galaxy (SBab). Optical emission line diagnostics reveal the source as an AGN/starburst composite, with the AGN dominating only in the inner 10 in the optical/IR bands 2006AA...459...55B .

sources.summary: NGC 6860 is a LIRG spiral host with a Seyfert 1 nucleus, surrounding ENLR, distorted off-nuclear H II ring, and prior high-resolution spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: NGC 6860: The spiral host galaxy is classified as a LIRG. The Seyfert 1 nucleus is surrounded by ENLR 15 arcsec (4.3kpc) in diameter surrounded by a distorted ring of off-nuclear \ regions with A-type absorption lines. This galaxy has been studied studied by high spatial resolution optical imaging and optical and near-IR spectroscopy by Lipari93 . The inner ENLR has been imaged in \ by Schmitt03a .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 6860's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 6860 is an inclined barred spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0149 ( D 65.8 Mpc) with a Sy 1.5 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 and circum-nuclear star formation lipari_high-resolution_1993 . It belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample and features nuclear east-west elongated emission ( 6 2 kpc; PA 90 lipari_high-resolution_1993,schmitt_hubble_2003 ). No high-angular resolution radio observations are described in the literature.

gmw_00000250.name: NGC 6890
gmw_00000250.overview: NGC 6890 is a grand-design spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0081 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a weak Seyfert 1.9/2 nucleus [arXiv:0712.0352, arXiv:1310.2770] that exhibits low X-ray luminosity [arXiv:0712.0352] and signs of heavy Compton-thick obscuration [arXiv:0712.0352].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6890 as the weakest Seyfert 2 in its sample, with weak nuclear emission, low fitted X-ray luminosity, and evidence for Compton-thick obscuration.

quotes.quote: NGC 6890: This source is the weakest Sy2 in the present sample with log L _ O III = 40.86 erg s ^ 1 ( < 41 ). The XMM-Newton observation of this object shows weak nuclear emission. The spectrum is described by a power-law ( = 2.54 ^ +0.47 _ 0.43 ) but without intrinsic absorption. The fitted 2 -- 10 keV luminosity is 1.0 10 ^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , consistent with a low luminosity nuclei. The observed 2 -- 10 keV flux of 6.9 10 ^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , however, gives the T ratio ( F_ 2-10 keV /F_ [ O III ] ) of 0.14, suggesting heavy obscuration on nuclear region (Bassani et al. 1999, Guainazzi et al. 2005).

Here, we consider it as a Compton-thick one, and give a a lower limit of 10 ^ 24 cm ^ 2 to N _ H , though longer exposure observations are crucial to shed light on the nature of absorption in X-rays in this object.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 6890, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 6890 The XMM-Newton spectra were grouped by a minimum of 3 counts per bin and were fit wit the C-statistic. The data were best fit by a double absorbed power law model and the Fe K line was marginally detected at the 93 confidence level. Shu et al.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 6890's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 6890 is a low-inclination grand-design spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0081( D 33.8 Mpc) with a Sy 1.9/2 nucleus phillips_nearby_1983,storchi-bergmann_stellar_1990 . The nucleus is detected but unresolved at arcsecond resolution at radio wavelengths ulvestad_radio_1989,morganti_radio_1999 . After its detection with , NGC 6890 was followed up in the MIR with /IRAC, IRS and MIPS.

gmw_00000251.name: NGC 7130
gmw_00000251.overview: NGC 7130 is a peculiar, low-inclination spiral galaxy [arXiv:1404.6470, arXiv:1804.07311] hosting a composite Seyfert 2 and starburst nucleus [arXiv:1303.3511, arXiv:2011.02937]. It features a barred structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0012035], faint tidal tails indicating recent minor interactions [arXiv:1303.3511, arXiv:1404.6470], and a biconic active galactic nucleus outflow [arXiv:2011.02937].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7130's AGN supplies over 80% of the MIR continuum at 12 m, while its PAH emission is nuclear and associated with an unresolved star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: The 11.3 m PAH feature is only clearly detected in the nuclear spectra of two AGN, while it is more common in the Spitzer data. For those two objects the AGN emission in NGC 7130 accounts for more than 80 of the MIR continuum at 12 m while in the case of NGC 1808 the AGN is not dominating the MIR emission. This is confirmed by the correlation between the MIR and X-ray continuum luminosities.

A strong PAH feature at 11.3 m is present only in NGC 1808 and NGC 7130 in the T-ReCS spectra. A hint of the PAH feature is also present in other 4 AGN (NGC 1386, NGC 5643, NGC 7172, and NGC 7582). Thus, for 14 out of 20 sources their high spatial resolution T-ReCS spectra are free of signs of star-formation in the MIR, as traced by the PAHs. From visual inspection of the 2D T-ReCS spectra of these sources, the PAH feature is spatially extended for NGC 1808 and coincident with the nuclear emission for NGC 7130. Moreover, the central UV continuum of NGC 7130 consists of several knots of star-formation distributed in an asymmetric ring within 1 arcsec, not spatially resolved by our data Gonzalez-Delgado98 . Thus, the PAH features detected in NGC 7130 could be associated to this ring.

Our sample includes 8 Compton-thick AGN but only NGC 7130 shows PAH emission in the T-ReCS spectra. However, as explained above, the T-ReCS spectrum of NGC 7130 includes an unresolved ring of star-formation.

sources.summary: NGC 7130 is presented as a luminous infrared Seyfert 2 spiral with disturbed morphology, tidal tails, a Compton-thick AGN, nuclear star formation, and detected CO lines.

quotes.quote: NGC 7130 NGC 7130 is a luminous IR galaxy ( = 10^ 11.35 ) located at 66 Mpc SandersRBGS . Its nuclear activity is classified as Seyfert 2 Yuan2010 . It is a face-on spiral galaxy with a disturbed morphology. Faint tidal tails are observed, although there are no nearby companions. It hosts a Compton-thick AGN with an estimated intrinsic hard X-ray luminosity of 8.3 10 ^ 40 erg s ^ 1 Levenson2005 .

IRS observations show the detection of the [ Ne 5 ] and [ O 4 ] high-ionization emission lines Tommasin2010 . In addition to the powerful AGN, a strong burst of star-formation is taking place in the nuclear region of this galaxy Gonzalez-Delgado2001,Diaz-Santos2010 . In the \ spectrum of NGC 7130 we measured nine CO lines from J_ up =4 to 12.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC7130 as a LIRG with a strong starburst and obscured AGN, using ALMA and X-ray data to explain its CO SLED with PDR and XDR molecular-gas components.

quotes.quote: NGC7130 is a local ( z =0.0161) Luminous Infrared Galaxy ( L_ IR 10^ 11.3 L_ , see 2016MNRAS.458.4297G ) with an ``ambiguous'' nature.

Summarizing, NGC7130 seems a prototype of a strong starburst probably hosting a Compton-thick AGN. The goal of this letter is to definitively assess the impact of the AGN activity on the molecular gas in NGC7130. This will be achieved via the modelling of the NGC7130 CO SLED. A coherent multi-phase (and multi-band) analysis of NGC7130 build upon literature data and new X-ray observations will be presented.

The high-resolution ALMA data allow us to constrain the physical CO emission down to 70 pc scales, while the X-ray data confirm the presence of a central highly obscured AGN. According to our modelling, three components are needed to reproduce the CO SLED: a PDR with parameters typical of the diffuse cold ISM ( log(n/cm^ 3 )=2.0 , T 25 K); a PDR, at relatively higher density and temperatures ( log(n/cm^ 3 )=4.0 , T 40 K) and an extra warm XDR component for ( log(n/cm^ 3 )=3.5 , T 100 K). An XDR driven chemistry for the high-J CO lines is supported by our multicomponent ^2 -fitting the CO SLED.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 7130's central ionized gas, finding a biconic AGN outflow with multiple kinematic components, a possible jet-powered plume, LINER-like off-axis gas, and complex AGN kinematics.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: right ascension RA =21^ h 48^ m 19. ^ s 520 and declination =-34^ o 57^ 04 48 (Epoch J2000.0)

quotes.quote: We present a detailed analysis of the ionised gas in the central regions of NGC 7130, which is an archetypical composite Seyfert and nuclear starburst galaxy at a distance of 64.8 Mpc . We achieve an angular resolution of 0 17 , corresponding to roughly 50 pc. We performed a multi-component analysis of the main interstellar medium emission lines in the wavelength range of MUSE and identified nine kinematic components, six of which correspond to the AGN outflow. The outflow is biconic, oriented in an almost north--south direction, and has velocities of a few 100 km s^ 1 with respect to the disc of NGC 7130. The lobe length is at least 3 ( 900 pc).
The plume is aligned with the radio jet, indicating that it may be jet powered.

Line ratio diagnostics indicate that the outflow gas in the north--south axis is AGN powered, whereas the off-axis component has LINER properties. We hypothesise that this is because the radiation field that reaches off-axis clouds has been filtered by clumpy ionised clouds found between the central engine and the low-ionisation emitting region.

sources.summary: The paper presents IFU spectroscopy of the LIRG NGC 7130, identifying starburst-AGN mixing, a 1.8 kpc extended narrow-line region, luminosity fractions, velocity components, and an outflow.

quotes.quote: We present an integral field spectroscopic study of the Luminous Infrared Galaxy (LIRG) NGC 7130, a known starburst-AGN composite galaxy. We employ standard emission line ratio diagnostics and maps of velocity dispersion and velocity field to investigate how the dominant ionising sources change as a function of radius. From the signatures of both star formation and AGN activity we show that NGC 7130 is a remarkably clean case of starburst-AGN mixing. We find a smooth transition from AGN dominated emission in the centre to pure star forming activity further out, from which we can estimate the radius of the extended narrow line region to be 1.8 0.8 kpc. We calculate that the fraction of \ luminosity due to star formation and AGN activity is 30 2 and 70 3 respectively, and that the fraction of \ luminosity due to star formation and AGN activity is 65 3 and 35 2 respectively.

Our investigation of the velocity field in NGC 7130 supports the notion of starburst-AGN composite activity, with three separate velocity components identified - one tracing pure star-formation and the underling velocity field of the galaxy, one tracing starburst-AGN mixing over the extended narrow line region of the galaxy, and one tracing the strongly blueshifted and high dispersion nuclear region of the galaxy. The emission line and velocity field maps for NGC 7130 also suggest the presence of an outflowing wind in the galaxy; however, our data do not allow us to determine whether this wind is driven by the circumnuclear starburst or the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7130 as a Seyfert 2/starburst composite whose X-ray emission includes a Compton-thick AGN, extended thermal star-formation emission, and substantial starburst bolometric power.

quotes.quote: Observations of the Seyfert 2 and starburst galaxy NGC 7130 with the Chandra X-ray Observatory illustrate that both of these phenomena contribute significantly to the galaxy's detectable X-ray emission. The active galactic nucleus (AGN) is strongly obscured, buried beneath column density N_H > 10^ 24 , and it is most evident in a prominent Fe K emission line with equivalent width greater than 1 keV. The AGN accounts for most (60 ) of the observed X-rays at energy E > 2 keV, with the remainder due to spatially extended star formation. The soft X-ray emission is strong and predominantly thermal, on both small and large scales. We attribute the thermal emission to stellar processes.

In total, the AGN is responsible for only one-third of the observed 0.5--10 keV luminosity of 3 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 of this galaxy, and less than half of its bolometric luminosity. Starburst/AGN composite galaxies like NGC 7130 are truly common, and similar examples may contribute significantly to the high-energy cosmic X-ray background while remaining hidden at lower energies, especially if they are distant.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7130 as a Seyfert 2 LIRG with small soft X-ray excess, powerful starburst contribution, and Compton-thick classification from large Fe K equivalent width.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 2 galaxies in our sample of LIRGs lie on the correlation (NGC 3690 and IC 4518W), or have a small (less than a factor of 3) soft X-ray emission excess (MCG 03-34-064, NGC 5135, and NGC 7130). In Type 2 Seyferts the absorbing hydrogen column density towards the AGN is high and thus most of the soft X-ray emission coming from the AGN is absorbed. Therefore we conclude that when a sufficiently powerful starburst is present it may contribute significantly to the observed soft X-ray emission.
The 3 Seyferts (NGC 3690, NGC 5135, and NGC 7130) that lie within 2 of the expected relation for star-formation are those with powerful starbursts.

The other 3 Seyfert 2s in our LIRGs sample (NGC 3690, NGC 5135, and NGC 7130) might be Compton thick AGN. In fact, NGC 5135 and NGC 7130 have been classified as Compton thick based on their large \ EW (see Levenson2004, Levenson2005 ).

sources.summary: NGC 7130 is described as a peculiar spiral Seyfert LIRG with reprocessed Herschel data and a previously modeled two-component CO SLED consistent after beam correction.

quotes.quote: The other three galaxies (NGC 5135, NGC 7130, and NGC 7469) are spirals, although NGC 7130 and NGC 7469 have peculiar morphologies suggesting recent minor interactions Genzel1995, Bellocchi2012 .

The \ data of two of these galaxies, UGC 05101 and NGC 7130, were already presented by Pereira2013 . To take advantage of the newest pipeline and calibration they were reprocessed with the rest of the sample.

Comparison with two-component models The CO SLEDs of two of our galaxies (UGC 05101 and NGC 7130) were presented by Pereira2013 . In that paper, a model consisting of two components was used to fit the data: a cold component that contributed mainly to the 1 emission, and a warm component with variable \ density and kinetic temperature. Once corrected for the different beam sizes used, the beam-averaged CO column densities derived by both methods agree within the uncertainties, although, this could be a particular case due to the specific SLEDs of these two galaxies.

sources.summary: NGC 7130 is treated as a LIRG dominated by a Seyfert 2 nucleus, with emission-line light centered on the nucleus and star-forming clumps along a spiral arm.

quotes.quote: NGC 7130: prototype of a LIRG dominated by a Seyfert 2 nucleus The light distribution of all emission lines in this galaxy is dominated by the nucleus and its surrounding regions while a few star-forming clumps appear along a spiral arm at distances of about 2.4 to 3 kpc from the nucleus (see Fig. figure:NGC7130 ). The nucleus itself contains a known Seyfert 2-type AGN ( Levenson:2005ApJ618 , AlonsoHerrero:2012p744 ), also identified in our spectra by the presence of coronal [ SiVI ] 1.96 m emission line Piqueras2012A&A546A .

Optical emission line ratios show a smooth transition from AGN-dominated in the centre to star-forming activity in the external regions ( Davies:2014co and references), while the observed UV and mid-IR spectrum of the nucleus indicates the presence of young star clusters GonzalezDelgado:1998ApJ505 and PAH emission characteristic of star formation ( DiazSantos:2010ApJ723 , Esquej:2014ApJ780 ), respectively. In this galaxy, we identify four regions (Fig.3 bottom panels): i) the compact [ SiVI ] emitting region (green points/region) as the AGN-dominated nucleus; ii) the compact and bright Br regions (blue points/regions) as the young star-forming clumps in the spiral arm; and iii) the diffuse AGN ionized region (yellow points/region) where weak [ SiVI ] emission around the nucleus is still detected; and finally iv) the diffuse ionized interstellar medium (white points/regions) in the extranuclear regions where no [ SiVI ] is detected, and along the spiral arm.

The AGN-diffuse medium clearly separates from the AGN- and young-star-dominated regions, covering the highest end of the H _2 /Br ratio while presenting intermediate [ FeII ] /Br values. Lastly, the diffuse non- [ SiVI ] emitting medium covers a very wide range of H _2 /Br values from those of compact young star-forming clumps to AGN-dominated nucleus, and with [ FeII ] /Br values slightly above that for young star-forming regions.

sources.summary: The paper presents AO-assisted MUSE observations of NGC 7130, finding a tiny decoupled inner disc and an AGN outflow plume in its composite starburst/Seyfert nucleus.

quotes.quote: One of the best-known examples of such composite AGN-starburst galaxies is the luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) NGC 7130 (also known as IC 5135), a peculiar Sa deVaucouleurs1991 galaxy at a distance of 65.5 Mpc (so 1^ corresponds to 318 pc). NGC 7130 hosts a Seyfert 1.9 AGN nucleus Veron-Cetty2006 as well as a powerful compact circumnuclear starburst Phillips1983 . The overall morphology of the central region of NGC 7130 is well known, and the alignment of the CO(6-5) emission with darker patches indicating dust concentrations has been described by Zhao2016 .

New IFU data obtained with the AO-assisted MUSE-NFM instrument on the VLT of the archetypical AGN--starburst ``composite'' galaxy NGC 7130, achieving an unprecedented spatial resolution at optical wavelengths of around 0 15 , has led to two discoveries. Firstly, we discover a distinct circumnuclear area of 0 2 in radius of positive and negative velocities which indicate a tiny kinematically decoupled inner disc. Such discs are commonly seen as a remnant of past interactions between galaxies, but the small size and proximity to the composite AGN+starburst core of NGC 7130 adds a new dimension to our understanding of this system.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7130 as a Seyfert 2 peculiar Sa galaxy with circumnuclear starburst, northern-arm photometric bias, low inclination, and challenging nuclear stellar-kinematic extraction.

quotes.quote: NGC 7130 : This Seyfert 2 galaxy Veron-Cetty+06 has been classified as a peculiar Sa galaxy Lu+98 , and hosts a compact circumnuclear starburst Phillips+83 . The HST image only covers the northern arm, which is the brightest in the near-IR. Thus, the determination of the photometric parameters are strongly biased by the northern region of the galaxy. This effect is clearly shown in the evolution of the centre determination, where the northern arm shifts the photocentre ( 6 ) at apertures of 8 kpc.

The low inclination of this target hinders the kinematic analysis and therefore we were only able to extract meaningful kinematic maps in the innermost region (<0.5 kpc). The low inclination complicates the extraction of the stellar kinematics, yielding kinematically derived PA and inclinations that differ from the photometric values.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7130 as a face-on Sy/LINER galaxy with two extranuclear spiral-arm regions and continuum plus line emission concentrated at the nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 7130 This is a face-on galaxy whose nuclear activity is classified as Sy/LINER. We detect 2 extranuclear \ regions located in the spiral arms. The brightest is located to the north, 10 arcsec from the nucleus. The other one is 14 arcsec to the south-east from the nucleus. The continuum and \ emissions are concentrated at the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7130 as an isolated face-on Seyfert spiral where IFS resolves star-forming arms, a nuclear Seyfert component, and possible AGN-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: IRAS F21453-3511 (NGC 7130) NGC 7130 is an isolated face-on spiral galaxy with a previously observed Seyfert 2 nucleus Phillips83 . The AGN dominates the mid-IR spectra in the nucleus and is detected in x-ray as well Petric11,Iwasawa11 . Because of it is nearby and nearly uninclined, our IFS spectroscopy resolve well the AGN and starburst component in this galaxy showing spiral arms dominated by star formation and a strong Seyfert component in the nucleus. The distribution of spaxels in the diagnostic diagram and the line ratio maps show this well, and agree with Monreal10 who also observed this system. Na D absorption shows a blue-shifted component which may be due to an AGN-driven outflow.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA imaging of NGC 7130, finding nuclear molecular gas and dust structures, a 70 pc line-continuum offset, and negligible AGN heating of the observed emission.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report our high-resolution ( 0^ .20 0^ .14 or 70 49 pc) observations of the \ line emission, which probes warm and dense molecular gas, and the 434 \ dust continuum in the nuclear region of NGC 7130, obtained with the Atacama Large Millimeter Array (ALMA). The CO line and dust continuum fluxes detected in our ALMA observations are 1230 74 Jy \ and 814 52 mJy, respectively, which account for 100 and 51 of their total respective fluxes. We find that the \ and dust emissions are generally spatially correlated, but their brightest peaks show an offset of 70 pc, suggesting that the gas and dust emissions may start decoupling at this physical scale. The brightest peak of the \ emission does not spatially correspond to the radio continuum peak, which is likely dominated by an Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN). This, together with our additional quantitative analysis, suggests that the heating contribution of the AGN to the \ emission in NGC 7130 is negligible.

sources.summary: NGC 7130 shows spiral-arm soft X-rays plus a hard excess and Fe line; the Chandra data favor an absorbed AGN model with high column density.

quotes.quote: [ 100 ] NGC 7130 : This galaxy shows clear extended emission in soft X-rays around a strong peak that follows the disk of the face-on optical galaxy, tracing the spiral arms. The spectrum shows a hard excess due to absorption and an iron 6.4 keV line at high energies, which could be due to absorption in the soft band, or due to reflection. A reflection component fitting, using a pexrav model MagZdz1995 results in an iron line with an equivalent width of 0.6 keV, which is too low for a reflection-originated line. Therefore, our data favors an absorption model; which, when imposing a photon index of 1.8, results in a column density of N_ H =3 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and an iron line equivalent width of 0.8 keV; detected with a significance of 2.5 . Based only on the Chandra data, we find it difficult to distinguish between this scenario and a Compton-Thick source with an imposed photon index of =0.0 , as modeled by LevWea2005 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7130 as a resolved KS-relation example, finding two power-law branches that separate central high-density regions from lower-density external disk regions.

quotes.quote: As an example, Fig. ks:ngc7130x shows the SFR surface density as a function of molecular gas surface density for NGC 7130 (similar figures for the rest of the sample are presented in Appendix app:figures_regions ). The KS diagram suggests that the regions follow two different power-laws. These two branches were identified using the Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) fit Friedman in _ SFR and _ H2 , which gives the position of the breaking points (cut-points) for a linear regression with multiple slopes. In this galaxy, the break of a linear regression occurs at _ H_ 2 ( M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 ) =3.35 (for cut on _ SFR , see Figure anexo:ngc7130y ).

The right panel of Fig. ks:ngc7130x shows that the branch with higher gas and SFR densities ( left panel) is located in the central region of the galaxy (at radii up to 0.85 kpc), while the other branch with lower gas and SFR densities is located in the more external disk regions.

sources.summary: The paper reports two bright nuclear mm-wave peaks in NGC 7130 and supporting radio/CO evidence for an AGN at the southeast nucleus.

quotes.quote: 90: NGC 7130 Our mm-wave image at a resolution of 0 3 reveals two bright nuclear peaks ( a and b ). In addition, to the north, a compact component ( ) is seen. studied this object using not only ALMA Band-9 data at a similar resolution of 0 2 which observed CO( J =6--5), but also other supplementary data at different wavelengths (e.g., optical and radio bands). They found a radio core near the SE nucleus ( a ) using VLA 8.4 GHz continuum data provided in , and also a higher 8.4 GHz-to-CO( J =6--5) luminosity ratio than expected for SF regions therein. These support that an AGN is located at the SE position of a .

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 7130 among Seyfert 2 nuclei whose UV morphology indicates a compact nuclear starburst with spatially resolved sub-arcsecond continuum emission.

quotes.quote: In four of the targets (Mrk 477, NGC 7130, NGC 5135 and IC 3639) the morphology suggests that they harbor a nuclear starburst (Figure 1). In these cases, the UV continuum is spatially resolved, showing sub-arcsec structure. The effective radius (50-200 pc) of the UV emission is similar to the size of the NLR. At the position where HST+WFPC2 (F606W) suggests that the nucleus is located, the UV images show very little emission (less than 15 of the total). This result suggests that even at UV wavelengths the S1 nucleus is still obscured.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7130 as a local LIRG classified as Seyfert 2 or LINER, with resolved nuclear mid-infrared emission and extended PAH-bearing star-forming emission.

quotes.quote: The 3 LIRGs host an optically identified AGN (NGC 5135 and IC 4518W are Seyfert (Sy) 2s, whereas the NGC 7130 has been classified as Sy 2 or LINER). From our T-ReCS and NICMOS imaging data we have detected unresolved nuclear emission in NGC 5135 and IC 4518W, implying sizes of < 40-51 pc, respectively, whereas the mid-IR nuclear emission of NGC 7130 appears resolved with a size of 190 pc (see Alonso-Herrero et al. 2006b). In addition we have detected in NGC 5135 and NGC 7130 bright regions within the central 1-2 kpc.

The only exception, NGC 7130, shows extended near and mid-IR emission (Alonso-Herrero et al. 2006b), consistent with the presence nuclear PAH due to SF. The relatively high ionization potential (34.8 eV) of the 10.5 m emission line may be interpreted as an AGN signature, but it has also been detected in very young starbursts (Roche et al. 1991; Verma et al. 2003) where the ionization is dominated by young hot stars.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7130 as a peculiar low-inclination spiral Sy1.9 galaxy with compact central starburst emission and MIR SEDs affected by star formation and obscured AGN emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 7130 is a peculiar low-inclination spiral galaxy with a Sy1.9 nucleus. A compact starburst is located at the center and it is extended over 300 pc Gonzalez98, Levenson05 . Wu09 and Alonso12 found that the arcsecond--scales MIR SED indicates obscured AGN emission with a high SF contribution. Asmus14 also concluded that the nuclear MIR SED is presumably still affected by significant SF emission.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 5135 as a dusty starburst/Seyfert example, finding intermediate-age burst components, young and intermediate stellar-population fractions consistent with prior modeling, and UV wind lines from massive stars.

quotes.quote: Our spectral synthesis find null contributions of the 100 and 290 Myr components, but for NGC 5135 it fits 27 of the continuum at 4861 \ as due to the 640 Myr component, while for IC 5135 18 and 21 are associated with 640 and 900 Myr-old bursts respectively.

For both IC 5135 and NGC 5135 they find 50 , 40 and 10 contributions (at 4800 ) of young, intermediate age and old populations respectively, where the intermediate age component is modeled as a combination of 200, 500 and 1000 Myr bursts. Our condensed population vector (at 4861 ) for these galaxies are (x_ Y/FC ,x_I,x_O) = (60,40,0) and (56,27,17) respectively.

Another emblematic example is IC 5135 (NGC 7130), whose UV spectrum shows wind lines of massive stars and a logarithmic slope typical of dusty starbursts, with little or no sign of a power-law (Gonz alez Delgado 1998), while our synthesis yields x_ FC = 35 , x_Y = 34 and A_V = 0 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7130 as a LIRG with a marginally resolved Seyfert/LINER nucleus, multiple NICMOS central sources, and a Seyfert component contributing 20 percent or less of central N-band emission.

quotes.quote: Three LIRGs (NGC 5135, IC 4518W, and NGC 7130) were observed with the N broad-band filter (central wavelength _ =10.36 m) and NGC 3256 with the Si-2 narrow-band filter ( _ =8.74 m).

The nucleus of NGC 7130 (classified as a Sy or LINER) appears marginally resolved in the T-ReCS images, and the NICMOS continuum images reveal the presence of at least three sources of similar flux in the central 190 pc. This suggests that the putative AGN in NGC 7130 does not dominate (see 4.2) the N band nuclear emission.
The Seyfert nucleus of IC 4518W appears to be dominate the mid-IR emission, whereas for NGC 5135 and NGC 7130 the Seyfert nuclei contribute 20 or less of the N band emission within the central 10 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 5135 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy using optical line ratios and reports its [OIII] luminosity.

quotes.quote: IC 5135 (NGC 7130) : The optical spectrum for IC 5135 has strong [ OIII ] and [ NII ] narrow lines. The log( [ OIII ] /H ) and log( [ NII ] /H ) line ratios, 0.6990 and -0.0231 respectively Vaceli1997 , place it in the Seyfert 2 region of the BPT diagram. Shu2007 report an [ OIII ] luminosity, log( L [ OIII ] ) = 42.55 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 7130, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 7130 The Chandra spectrum of NGC 7130 was best fit by a double absorbed power law with a thermal component for the soft emission and a Gaussian feature at the Fe K energy; we used the C-statistic with the data binned by 2 counts. This source was studied in detail by Levenson et al. (2005) where they fit the AGN spectrum with a double absorbed power law with a gaussian component as well as two thermal components.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 7130's [O III] emission is broad-component dominated, has blueshifted components, and shows a broad-component velocity dispersion similar to H alpha.

quotes.quote: The total [ O iii ] emission is dominated by the broad component in MCG-03-34-064 and in NGC 7130, whereas in NGC 5135 and NGC 7469 the core component dominates the emission. In NGC 5135 based on both the relatively broad [ Si vi ] 1.96 m coronal line and its spatial extent suggested the existence of different kinematical components and the possible presence of AGN induced outflows. found blueshifted components in the Ly and [ O iii ] 5007 lines of both NGC 7130 and NGC 5135. The velocity dispersion of the broad component of the [ O iii ] 5007 line of NGC 7130 is similar to the H broad component 400 km s ^ 1 , Bellocchi2012 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 7130's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 7130 is a peculiar low-inclination spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0162 ( D 68.9 Mpc) with an active nucleus containing both an AGN and a powerful compact starburst thuan_ultraviolet_1984,gonzalez_delgado_ultraviolet-optical_1998,levenson_deconstructing_2005 . The nuclear starburst extends over the central 1 0.3 kpc. The AGN is optically classified as a Sy 1.9 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , Sy 2 phillips_nearby_1983 and LINER kim_optical_1995,veilleux_optical_1995 , and we treat it as AGN/starburst composite.

sources.summary: NGC 7130 is treated as a known starburst-AGN mixing case; the paper compares its mixing sequence to models and derives spaxel AGN fractions and global luminosity contributions.

quotes.quote: We include NGC 7130 as a known example of starburst-AGN mixing whose observed mixing sequence has not yet been compared to theoretical mixing curves (see PaperI ).

We apply the technique described in Section subsec:AGN_fractions to the O III _ corr / O II _ corr vs. \ mixing sequences and thereby derive a second estimate for the AGN fraction of each spaxel in NGC 7130, NGC 6394 and NGC 2410.

In order to test this assumption, we calculate the relative contribution of the star-formation and the AGN activity to the global \ luminosities of NGC 7130, NGC 6394 and NGC 2410, using the technique outlined in Section subsec:AGN_fractions .

sources.summary: NGC 7130 / IC5135 is classified as mixed starburst plus Seyfert 2, with broad forbidden lines and an ENLR inside a tidally distorted barred spiral.

quotes.quote: NGC 7130 / IC5135: In agreement with the classification of Thuan84 , the WiFeS nuclear spectrum shows this as mixed excitation starburst + Seyfert 2. The \ lines are appreciably broader than the H , and there is a strong continuum from young stars mixed with the older stellar component.Long slit spectroscopy has been obtained by Fraquelli03 . This galaxy contains ENLR 9 9 arcsec (2.7kpc) within a bright tidally-distorted barred spiral of \ regions, nicely imaged with HST Malkan98 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7130 as a peculiar barred galaxy whose HRI observation is resolved under one estimate and whose X-ray spectrum is dominated by thermal soft emission.

quotes.quote: As a result, the HRI observation of NGC 7130, for example, is resolved according to the first estimate, but we determine only an upper limit on the extended fraction using the more realistic model.
NGC 7130 NGC 7130 is peculiar and contains a bar, detected at near-infrared wavelengths Mul97 .
The X-ray spectrum is best fit with a single power law and thermal emission.

sources.summary: NGC 7130 illustrates the fully absorbed continuum class: no hard X-ray bump, low apparent foreground absorption, and an AGN inferred to be completely buried.

quotes.quote: The first shows a steadily decreasing spectral intensity from low energies to high energies and no clear evidence for a hard X-ray ``bump'' that would suggest we are seeing a buried AGN directly through large amounts of obscuring matter (e.g., NGC 7130).

We interpret the low foreground column density and lack of a second, absorbed (in the \ bandpass) power-law component in NGC 1068, Mrk 1066, Mrk 266, and NGC 7130 as evidence that the intrinsic AGN is completely buried and fully absorbed by N_H 10^ 24 cm^ 2 .

NGC 7130 and Mrk 477 illustrate the two characteristic continuum shapes of the composite galaxies, depending on whether the intrinsic AGN is completely absorbed, as in the former, or partially emerges to add a high-energy bump, as in the latter.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7130's Seyfert/LINER nucleus marginally resolved, with several central NICMOS continuum sources and an AGN contribution below 20% of the total mid-infrared emission.

quotes.quote: The nucleus of NGC 7130 (classified as a Sy or LINER) appears marginally resolved in the T-ReCS images, and the NICMOS continuum images reveal the presence of at least three sources of similar flux in the central 190 pc. This suggests that the putative AGN in NGC 7130 does not dominate (see 3.2) the N band nuclear emission.
The AGN nucleus of NGC 7130, undetected as a point source, is responsible for <20 of the total MIR emission (see Table 1).

gmw_00000252.name: NGC 7172
gmw_00000252.overview: NGC 7172 is an edge-on spiral or lenticular Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1305.4938, arXiv:1804.07311] located in the Hickson Compact Group 90 [arXiv:1305.4938, arXiv:astro-ph/0008295]. The galaxy features an east-west dust lane crossing its nuclear region [arXiv:1305.4938, arXiv:0906.5368] and exhibits X-ray flux variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0008295, arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7172 as an edge-on Seyfert galaxy with a dust lane, hot nuclear dust, broad recombination lines, and evidence that dust caused Seyfert 2 misclassification.

quotes.quote: NGC 7172 is an edge-on early-type spiral galaxy at a redshift of about 0.0087 and is a member of the Hickson Compact Group 90 (HCG90). It has a prominent dustlane crossing its nuclear region from east to west (see Fig. fig:NGC7172 ). Observations of this galaxy cover a wavelength range from radio over MIR, NIR and optical to X-ray observations.

NGC 7172 was identified with the X-ray source H2158-321 . Polarized observations were not able to detect broad emission lines . However, short term variability measured in the X-ray suggests that there is a BLR around the active nucleus of NGC 7172 .

NGC 7172 shows a strong red continuum emission which can only be attributed to very hot dust close to the sublimation temperature of about 1200K. The dust emission makes up more than 80 of the continuum emission on the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC7172 as an S0 Seyfert 2 in a compact group, with absorbed X-ray spectra, weak Fe emission, spectral variability, and large flux changes.

quotes.quote: NGC7172 NGC7172 is an S0 galaxy (z=0.0087) belonging to a compact group of galaxies (Hickson90). It is classified as a Seyfert-2 galaxy on the basis of its optical spectrum (Sharples et al. 1984). obtained the first X-ray spectrum of the source yielding a photon index of 1.8 (Turner Pounds 1989). The same photon index value was also found in a observation of the source (Smith Done 1996). The data analysis yielded a column density of 1 10^ 23 , and a rather weak Fe line (EW 50 eV).

The data as well as combination with GRO/OSSE observations (Ryde et al. 1997) show a flat spectrum of 1.5 and therefore provided evidence for spectral variability between the and epoch. The 2-10 keV flux has been fairly constant during the 1977-1989 period at a level of about 3-4 10^ 11 . However, Guainazzi et al. (1998) presented evidence for significant short term (hours) and long term (months) variability using two observations separated by a year.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7172 as the Sa Seyfert 2 spiral in HCG90, with asymmetric HI emission, molecular gas, and a disturbed tidal feature toward the group.

quotes.quote: This is a group with mixed morphology, with one spiral galaxy, a (=NGC 7172), which is a Sa, two ellipticals, b and and one irregular, d . Galaxies a through d have X-ray emission (Mulchaey et al., 1998); galaxy a is a Sy2 (Coziol et al., 1998), while galaxies b and have no optical line emission at all and galaxy d is a LINER.

Only galaxy a is detected in \ line emission, see Fig. fig:scg3hi : the gas distribution is highly asymmetric, with more gas in the eastern half of the galaxy disk than in the west. figure 1 Due to the presence of strong radio continuum emission from the nuclear area and beam smearing effects, no significant \ emission is seen in the western disk. Higher angular resolution is required to reduce this effect. Clearly the \ line flux, f(HI) , is reduced by the absorption feature, which leads to an underestimate of the total \ gas mass, M(HI) . The \ emission distribution in the eastern half is disturbed, with a tidal tail protruding towards south, i.e. in the direction of the other group members.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7172 as an obscured edge-on type 2 Seyfert whose BeppoSAX spectra show an absorbed power law, Fe K emission, and weak long-term spectral variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 7172 is an obscured, almost edge on galaxy. It belongs to the compact group HCG90 and has a redshift of z=0.0086. It is classified as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy.

We fit both NGC 7172 observations with an absorbed power law model ( =1.62^ +0.07 _ 0.09 and N _H=9.1^ +0.3 _ 0.6 10^ 22 cm^ 2 ) plus a Gaussian component at 6.4 keV to account for the Fe line. The width of the line is 0.6^ +0.4 _ 0.5 keV and therefore remains unconstrained. Comparison of the two observations reveals weak evidence for spectral variability. The power-law normalization has decreased by about a factor of two between the 1996 and 1997 observations.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 7172 is a highly inclined Seyfert 2 with dust lanes, poor simultaneous SED and spectroscopy fits, and likely extended dust contamination.

quotes.quote: The only galaxy for which we could not get a good fit to both the SED photometric points and the mid-IR spectroscopy was the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 7172. This galaxy is highly inclined (see Table 4), has prominent dust lanes, and thus probably suffers from high extinction in the host galaxy (see e.g. Roche et al. 2007). Since we could not find estimates for the host galaxy extinction, we used the foreground extinction derived from the X-ray column density and the standard Galactic ratio N_ H /A_V = 1.9 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 mag ^ 1 (Bohlin, Savage, Drake 1978). As can be seen from Figure 5, while the model provides a reasonable fit to the 9.7 m silicate feature, the near-IR photometric points are well above the model. One possibility is that there is contamination by extended dust components in this galaxy, and the unresolved flux estimates from the K and L band ground-based data are upper limits.

For three AGN with relatively deep 9.7 m silicate features and hosted in inclined galaxies with prominent dust lanes (NGC 5506, NGC 7172, and NGC 7582), we were not able to fit simultaneously the photometric SEDs and mid-IR spectroscopy.

sources.summary: NGC 7172 is discussed as an OH119-absorption outlier with low Balmer extinction, interpreted as a recent AGN whose OH119 has not reached emission equilibrium.

quotes.quote: However, the correlation greatly improves when removing two outliers, NGC 253 and NGC 7172. These two galaxies can be identified as outliers in the sample because they have deep absorption ( 0.081 m and 0.064 m) at low H /H ratios (2.84 and 3.00). All other galaxies in the sample show strong emission (EW +0.05 m) in this Balmer decrement range. Without NGC 253 and NGC 7172, EW(OH119) has a significant relationship ( = 0.72 and p = 0.00005) with the Balmer decrement.

NGC 7172 was identified in Section subsec:line_ratios as a galaxy with a recent AGN source that needs some Myrs to drive OH119 into emission. This means that even though this galaxy has low dust extinction, the nuclear source was not powerful enough to create OH119 emission until just recently and the doublet needs more time to reach equilibrium in emission.

sources.summary: The paper compares prior reflection and no-reflection models for NGC 7172, finding its model C only slightly better than model B and implying a steeper continuum.

quotes.quote: NGC 7172: There are two published works on the analysis of the same data (Akylas et al. 2001 and Dadina et al. 2001). Akylas et al. 2001 propose a model with no reflection. Their best fit parameters are in agreement with our model B (Table 4). Dadina et al. 2001 find that the inclusion of a cold reflection slightly increase the goodness of the fit.

The best fit slope of the continuum is significantly higher in this model Our results are in agreement with Dadina et al. 2001. Our best fit model C (Table 5) is only slightly better than model B, and predicts a steeper intrinsic continuum ( 1.9 ), than model B ( 1.7 , in agreement with Akylas et al. 2001).

sources.summary: NGC 7172 is presented as a nearly edge-on Type-2 Seyfert with a dust lane; stellar contamination likely affects its SED and silicate-feature modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 7172. NGC 7172 is a Type-2 Seyfert nucleus in a nearly edge-on spiral galaxy. A prominent dust lane in the east-western direction crosses the galaxy. Similar to the case of NGC 4388, we observe a near-IR bump of emission in the SED of NGC 7172, which stellar contamination of the seeing-limited fluxes likely produces ( stellar_contamination ). The N_0 histogram shows a Gaussian shape, with median value N_0 = 5 _ 1 ^ 3 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7172 as an early-type HCG 90 type 2 Seyfert with dust lanes, radio core, infrared and X-ray variability reports, and compact X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 7172 NGC 7172 is an early type galaxy located in the HCG 90 group, that shows dust lanes see also Appendix multiimages sharples1984 . Optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert (see an optical spectrum in Appendix multiimages ), no broad lines have been observed in polarized light lumsden2001 . A radio core was detected with VLA data unger1987 . At IR frequencies, found variations in timesclaes of about three months. The nucleus of this galaxy is not detected at UV frequencies with the OM (see Table obsSey ).

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 7172 has central non-stellar continuum contamination, irregular dust-lane-affected stellar continuum maps, and outer CO dilution linked to its relatively high hard-X-ray luminosity and lower obscuration.

quotes.quote: An observational caveat is that there are three AGNs (NGC 3783, NGC 4593 and NGC 7172) with strong non-stellar continuum contribution in the centre; therefore it is difficult to extract the stellar surface brightness at radii below 50 pc.

The two exceptions are NGC 7172 and NGC 7582, for both of which the maps exhibit extreme irregularities coinciding with known dust lanes Bianchi2007,Smajic2012 . These pass across their nuclei and are clearly visible in optical HST F606W images Malkan1998 . It is difficult to quantify the Ks-band extinction and correct it with present data.

For NGC 3783, NGC 4593, and NGC 7172, they have higher L_ 14-195 and less obscuration with respect to other AGNs, suggesting, even at 1.5 outer regions, the strong non-stellar continuum may marginally dilute the stellar absorption features.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7172 keeps a high cutoff lower limit even after excluding datasets, disagreeing with earlier low tight constraints from NuSTAR-only and nonsimultaneous analyses.

quotes.quote: NGC 7172 -- Removing the \ data from the fit does not change the high lower limit on , and neither does further exclusion of \ data. We find a more robust lower limit, such that no models meet the >5 criterion below it, at >250 keV. We are unable to match the data with the spectral parameters listed by rani+2019 , who constrained \ to 70 10 keV based on \ data alone. A similarly low and tight \ constraint was found by deRosa+2012 from nonsimultaneous and nonoverlapping \ and \ data. In both cases these constraints correspond to _e > 3 .

sources.summary: The paper reports central core and east-west diffuse mm-wave emission in NGC 7172, interpreting the diffuse morphology as disk gas or star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: 92: NGC 7172 In addition to the central core emission, diffuse emission in the east-west direction appears at 3 _ mm , although this is formally insignificant. The morphology appears to trace the galaxy disk e.g., Mal98 . Therefore the diffuse emission would trace gas in the disk, or SF regions. As in NGC 5506 (Section sec:ngc5506 ), the edge-on view to NGC 7172 would enable us to identify that emission. We note that a possible radio counterpart for the core was found with a 0 4 beam in .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7172 has the strongest 18 micrometer and deepest 9.7 micrometer silicate absorption, despite modest X-ray column density, likely affected by a dust lane.

quotes.quote: We note that the nuclear spectra of MCG-05-23-016, NGC 5506, NGC 5728, NGC 7172, NGC 7319, and ESO 137-G034 correspond to physical scales of 57, 55, 53, 38, 55, 116 and 52 pc (at 6 m), respectively.
The only exceptions are NGC 5728 (S _ Sil ^ 18 m =-0.23) and NGC 7172 (S _ Sil ^ 18 m =-0.54).

We find the deepest 9.7 m silicate absorption in NGC 7172 (see also Roche07 ), NGC 5728, ESO 137 G034 and NGC 5506 (S _ Sil ^ 9.7 m of -2.4, -1.9, -1.0, -0.9, respectively). Two of these galaxies (NGC 5728 and ESO 137--G034) have high hydrogen column densities (CT-like source), as measured from the X-rays. On the other hand, NGC 7172 has a modest value of N _ H from X-rays ( Ricci17 ) and ALMA observations ( Alonso-Herrero23 ) relative to the deep silicate bands found in its nuclear region. However, this galaxy has a dust lane ( Sharples84 , see also Fig. 1 of Alonso-Herrero23 ) and, thus, we expect a significant contribution to the silicate strength from this foreground material.

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 7172 as an edge-on lenticular Sy2 galaxy with a prominent nuclear dust lane and mixed assessments of star-formation contamination in its MIR SED.

quotes.quote: NGC 7172 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy with a Sy2 nucleus Veron10 . Smajic12 found a prominent dust lane projected along the nucleus. The arcsecond--scales MIR SED might be affected by significant SF Wu09,Gallimore10 . However, Asmus14 concluded that the nuclear MIR SED is free of SF contamination.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 7172, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 7172 The XMM-Newton observations were best fit by a double absorbed power law with a thermal component and Gaussian feature at the Fe K energy. The normalization of the second power law was consistent between the the 2002 and 2004 observations, yet had to be fit independently for the 2007 observation. We only fit the PN and MOS2 spectra for the 2007 observation as the MOS1 spectrum showed evidence for milder pileup at higher energies from the task epatplot .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7172 as one of the strongest silicate-absorption cases, with S10 about 2.0 and poor fits from the available CLUMPY torus models.

quotes.quote: The most extreme silicate absorption features are seen in NGC 7172 and NGC 7582, while NGC 1194, IRAS 04385-0828, NGC 4388 and NGC 5506 correspond to more moderate cases. Using S_ 10 to quantity the depth of the silicate feature ( S_ 10 = F_ /F_ , , where F_ corresponds to the flux at the deepest point of the absorption feature and F_ , corresponds to the flux in the interpolated continuum) we find that NGC 7172 and NGC 7582 have S_ 10 2.0 and 1.4 , respectively, while the other 4 sources listed above have S_ 10 values between 0.7 and 1 . We find that it is not possible to properly fit NGC 5506, NGC 7172 and NGC 7582 with the set of current CLUMPY models because a combination of deep silicate absorption and SEDs with strong near-IR emission is not available.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 7172's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 7172 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0087 ( D 34.8 Mpc) with a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 and a prominent dust lane projected onto the nucleus ( sharples_ngc_1984 ; see smajic_unveiling_2012 for a recent detailed study). It is variable by a factor of two in X-rays (e.g. akylas_bepposax_2001 ) and belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 7172 as a highly inclined compact-group galaxy with a dust-obscured nucleus, two-sided polar ionization cone, and HII-region lines around its dust lane.

quotes.quote: NGC 7172: A two-sided ionization cone with opening angle 120^ emerges in the polar direction from a highly dust-obscured nucleus in this highly inclined galaxy, a member of the compact group HCG90. The southern cone is the brighter. A line of \ regions is visible on either side of the central E-W dust lane.

gmw_00000253.name: NGC 7212
gmw_00000253.overview: NGC 7212 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1109.1195, arXiv:1505.01166] located within an interacting triplet of galaxies [arXiv:1109.1195, arXiv:0901.0999]. It hosts a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0002460, arXiv:2101.11625] that powers an extended ionization cone [arXiv:1109.1195] and exhibits kinematically decoupled gas and stars [arXiv:1109.1195].

sources.summary: The paper presents integral-field and echelle observations of NGC 7212, finding an extended ionization cone, filamentary ENLR gas, gas-star kinematic decoupling, and interaction-region shock signatures.

quotes.quote: We studied the physical and kinematical properties of the circumnuclear gas in the nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 7212. We analysed this object by means of integral field and echelle spectra and broad-band images. We pointed out for the first time the presence of an extended ionization cone in NGC 7212, with high values of [ O iii ] /H (up to 12), at a large distance from the nucleus (up to 3.6 kpc). The cone is oriented NW-SE, at PA = 150 \ close to the photometric minor axis of the galaxy, with an opening angle of about 70 . The cone is more extended in the North (7 arcsec = 3.6 kpc) than in the South (5.5 arcsec = 2.8 kpc) direction, while in HST [ O iii ] published images only the southern high ionization emission was detected, showing a structure made of clouds or filaments.

NGC 7212 is in an interacting triplet, with two galaxies in a clear on-going merger. We found high values of [ N ii ] /H and [ S ii ] /H towards the interaction region: suggesting a possible combination of ionization by the active nucleus and by shocks. From the 2D velocity maps, we found kinematical decoupling between the stars and gas.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7212 as an interacting Seyfert 2 with clumpy ENLR gas, shocks, low-luminosity AGN flux, complex line profiles, and element depletion across the ionization cone.

quotes.quote: NGC 7212 (z=0.0266) belongs to a compact group of interacting galaxies, with two galaxies in a clear ongoing merger (Wasilewski 1981). Spectropolarimetry studies show the presence of a hidden broad line region (BLR) (Tran Kay 1992), clumpy nuclear morphology, and irregular diffuse emission. Mu n oz Mar n et al. ( ) also claim that these galaxies manifest some cases in which in addition to the extended emission, the UV light stems from the knots or clumps likely produced by star clusters. They identify the bright clumps to the south as star-forming regions with a certain contribution from the AGN. Exploring whether photoionization or shocks dominate in NGC 7212, Bianchi et al. ( ) found a striking resemblance of the [ O III ] structure with soft X-ray emission, a clear indication that shocks are at work.

The AGN in NGC 7212 is characterised by a flux intensity lower than 10 ^ 11 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 eV ^ 1 , at the Lyman limit, similar to that of low luminosity AGN (e.g. Contini ), rather than to Seyfert 2 (e.g. Contini et al. , Ciroi et al. 2003). The AGN flux peaks in correspondence with the maximum starlight flux. In the ENLR of NGC 7212, as in most Seyfert galaxies, the single lines show complex profiles.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7212 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy whose ENLR spectra are mainly photoionized, may include high-velocity shocks, but show no velocity-dependent ionization parameter.

quotes.quote: We studied the properties of the gas of the extended narrow line region (ENLR) of two Seyfert 2 galaxies: IC 5063 and NGC 7212. We analysed high resolution spectra to investigate how the main properties of this region depend on the gas velocity. We divided the emission lines in velocity bins and we calculated several line ratios. Diagnostic diagrams and SUMA composite models (photo-ionization + shocks), show that in both galaxies there might be evidence of shocks significantly contributing in the gas ionization at high V , even though photo-ionization from the active nucleus remains the main ionization mechanism. In IC 5063 the ionization parameter depends on V and its trend might be explained assuming an hollow bi-conical shape for the ENLR, with one of the edges aligned with the galaxy disk. On the other hand, NGC 7212 does not show any kind of dependence.

In the far radio range of NGC 7212 self absorption of the flux is evident. Unfortunately the data for IC 5063 are lacking. We studied the NLR/ENLR gas of two nearby Seyfert 2 galaxies: IC 5063 and NGC 7212. We analysed high resolution spectra to highlight the different kinematic components of the emission lines and to study the properties of the gas as a function of velocity. We produced diagnostic diagrams, we studied the ionization parameter and the physical conditions of the gas resulting from the line ratios and we compared the observations to detailed models of the spectra, obtaining the following results:
NGC 7212 shows an ionization parameter which does not depend on velocity. Therefore, it is not possible to say anything about the real geometrical shape of the ENLR.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 7212 has a southward diffuse cone-like emission component, heavy absorption near the nucleus, and southern clumps identified as star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 7212 (Sy2) The diffuse emission region to the south of the nucleus is removed with f =0.25. However this leaves negative residuals in the nuclear region and next to it to the north. It is expected that those regions suffer from a heavy absorption, as the usual bi-conical structure is shown here as a single cone towards the south. The scale factor needed to remove the small northern emitting zones is just 0.15. As explained in Section sec:NC , this can be accounted for with an internal extinction of A_V = 1.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7212 as an interacting type 2 Seyfert with polarized broad lines, radio nuclear counterpart, hard X-ray point source, and Compton-thick candidate status.

quotes.quote: NGC 7212 This galaxy is interacting with a companion (see the 2MASS image in Appendix multiimages ). It was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy see an optical spectrum in Appendix multiimages veilleuxosterbrock1987 . Broad lines were detected in polarized light tran1992 . At radio wavelenghts, a nuclear counterpart was found together with the interacting galaxy falcke1998 . A point-like source is detected at hard X-rays (see Appendix multiimages ).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7212 extended on arcsecond and VLBI scales, embedded in an interacting system, with a nearby companion and multiple compact radio components.

quotes.quote: NGC 7212 (2204+095) Y : Our map does not resolve the compact double separated by 0.7 arcsec to the north-west as clearly as the 8.4 GHz, VLA A array map of . However, our map does show an obvious extension towards the north (P.A. 10 ^ ), as well as fainter extensions roughly to the south and south-east. The largest angular size of the source is 3 arcsec. We note that the position of the phase calibrator that we used was in error at the time of our observations, but has since been updated by the NRAO; we have incorporated this correction in our image using the AIPS task OGEOM. NGC 7212 is in an interacting system Wasilewski81 and we detect faint radio emission from its closest companion, seen 14 ^ to its north-east (Fig. large_image , right panel).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7212 as part of a triple interacting system whose near-infrared spectrum resembles a Seyfert 2, with flat continuum and high-ionization narrow lines.

quotes.quote: NGC 7212 NGC 7212 is part of a triple interacting system Wasilewski81 .

From our near-infrared data, NGC 7212 presents the typical spectrum of a Seyfert 2 galaxy, similar to that of Mrk 348, but without the K band excess. Its continuum is very flat (see Figure sed ) and several narrow emission lines are present in the spectrum, including all the high-ionization species seen in Mrk 573. The [ S IX ] 1.252 line is strongly blended with the [ Fe II ] 1.257 feature in NGC 7212.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7212 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy with a flat X-ray spectrum, a marginal iron-line detection, and spectral features implying Compton-thick obscuration.

quotes.quote: NGC 7212: The Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 7212 has a flat 0.5-10 keV spectrum, well fitted by a powerlaw with photon index = 0.75^ +0.50 _ 0.55 . An emission line with peak energy E=6.06 ^ +0.35 _ 0.40 (compatible with a K _ iron line at a confidence level of 90 ) is detected at a 2 level, with a best fit equivalent width EW=1.0 ^ +1.3 _ 0.9 keV. From these spectral features we deduce that the source is Compton-thick. The measured 2-10 keV flux is F=9.8 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7212 as a Seyfert 2 outlier with evidence for a featureless continuum component and little compelling evidence for significant current star formation.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, for both NGC 7212 and Mrk 3, imaging (Pogge De Robertis 1993; Kotilainen Ward 1997), spectropolarimetry (Tran 1995), and spectral modelling (Gonz alez Delgado 2001) all point to the existence of an FC component (see Cid Fernandes 2001 for a detalied discussion). None of these studies find compelling evidence for significant on-going star-formation in these galaxies. It is therefore likely that their low W_ CaT values (the smallest amongst Seyfert 2s) are indeed due to dilution by non-stellar light, explaining why they deviate from the region spanned by our purely stellar models in Fig. Fig_CaT_x_CaK_c .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 7212's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 7212 is a system of three interacting galaxies with one containing an AGN wasilewski_new_1981 . In the following we refer to the largest, central galaxy, NGC 7212 NED02 as NGC 7172. This galaxy is an inclined spiral at a redshift of z= 0.0266 ( D 116 Mpc) containing a Sy 2 nucleus de_robertis_analysis_1986 with broad emission lines in polarized light tran_nature_1995 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7212 as a distant Compton-thick AGN in an interacting compact group, with weak azimuthal dependence and the largest kpc-scale extent in the sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 7212 NGC 7212 is located at RA=22:07:01.30 (331.755 ), Dec:+10:13:52 (10.231 ), and z=0.0266 (D _ lum 115 Mpc), in a compact group of three interacting galaxies (e.g., Mun07 ).
Of the five sources, NGC 7212 is the only one to not exhibit a strong azimuthal dependence.

For NGC 7212, the FWHM in arcsec as a function of energy appears to be very much consistent with the other CT AGN in this sample, although the extent in kpc is the largest in the sample (Figure fig:fwhm ; bottom).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7212 as Compton thick, medium-band extended, and exceptional because continuum modeling gives a lower luminosity than Fe-line estimates.

quotes.quote: Ris00 suggested that NGC 7212 is Compton thick from analysis of a low signal-to-noise ASCA spectrum, finding an apparently flat continuum and a prominent Fe line.
NGC 7212 is significantly extended only in the medium band.

Indeed, the values of L_ AGN,2-10 based on the continuum modeling are about an order of magnitude lower than the reasonable estimates we derived from the Fe line luminosities ( subsec:feka ), with the exception of NGC 7212.

gmw_00000254.name: NGC 7213
gmw_00000254.overview: NGC 7213 is a nearly face-on Sa galaxy [arXiv:1205.3524, arXiv:1905.00994] hosting a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1212.2853, arXiv:2212.10973] classified as a Seyfert 1 or LINER [arXiv:1410.6241, arXiv:1501.02022]. The galaxy features a decoupled warped gaseous disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382] and exhibits spiral inflows toward its supermassive black hole [arXiv:1410.6241, arXiv:1501.02022].

sources.summary: The paper treats 7 as a face-on Sa LLAGN/Seyfert-LINER source with a massive black hole, low accretion rate, hard-state-like X-ray behavior, and a LINER-like SED.

quotes.quote: 7 (z=0.005839) is a face on Sa galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus (AGN). It has been classified as a type I Seyfert by phillips79 , based on its H linewidth (full width at zero intensity of 13000 km s ^ 1 ), but also as a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) by filippenko84 , based on the study of a variety of optical emission lines which were observed to have a full width at half maximum of 200 to 2000 km s ^ 1 . 7 hosts a black-hole with a mass of about 10^8 M _ woo02 .

Its bolometric luminosity of L_ bol =9 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 starling05 suggests a rather low accretion rate of 0.07 per cent of the Eddington luminosity (L _ Edd ). As noted by lobban10 , this value is intermediate between those usually found in type I Seyfert galaxies and LINER's and it is significantly less than the predicted 2 per cent L _ Edd `critical' rate at which the `soft state' transition appears in black hole X-ray binaries (BHXRBs) maccarone03 . Finally, based on its radio properties, 7 belongs to a rare class of extragalactic sources lying between the radio loud and radio quiet AGN (it is one of the 20 sources of the roy97 sample).

The X-ray spectral behaviour 7 is also peculiar. starling05 using the reflection grating spectrometer, on board XMM-Newton , found several emission features with no absorption lines; contrary to what is usually observed in type I Seyfert galaxies.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 7213 as a LINER/Seyfert 1 observed with GMOS-IFU, where gas and stellar kinematics reveal spiral inflow signatures and measurable inflow rates.

quotes.quote: The case of NGC 7213 is illustrated in Fig. n7213 . The nucleus of this galaxy is classified as LINER/Seyfert 1, and we have obtained GMOS-IFU data of the inner 500 pc. The excellent signal-to-noise ratio of the data has allowed us to derive the stellar kinematics, revealing high velocity dispersion ( 200 ) and rotation (amplitude of 50 ). Gas emission is observed over the whole FOV, presenting also rotation, but with an amplitude much larger than that of the stars. The leftmost panel of Fig. n7213 shows the gas velocity field, that can be compared with the model fitted to the stellar velocity field, shown in the second panel. The third panel (to the right) shows the difference between the velocity field of the gas and that of the stars.

We note that these residuals follow a spiral pattern that is correlated with a dusty spiral seen in a structure map built from an HST F606W image of the galaxy, shown in the rightmost panel of the figure. Under the assumption that the gas is in the plane of the galaxy (this is justified in the paper), we can only conclude that the excess blueshifts and redshifts are due to gas inflows along the spirals. The estimated mass inflow rate along these spirals are 0.4 at 400 pc and 0.1 at 100 pc of the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7213 deviates in bulge-to-total ratio, has strong nuclear excess luminosity, is nearly face-on, and should not show a clear central velocity-dispersion drop.

quotes.quote: We find NGC 7213 deviates from the relation between the B/T and Hubble type. Its bulge parameter coupling problem has been discussed and tested in Section 4.4 of Weinzirl2009 . Given the Sa morphology, a B/T of 0.3 only occurs when we fix n _ bulge = 1, which cannot be distinguished from the outer disk, thus we decide to adopt the solution of n _ bulge = 2.57 with _ e; bulge = 13.7 .

We apply this toy model to all objects and find that in its simplest form above, it can already explain the observed trend of velocity dispersion for NGC 7213 (AGN), which is presented Figure fig:model_disp . This galaxy has strong nuclear excess stellar luminosity. By checking its galactic plane inclination, we find NGC 7213 is close to face-on (26 ), which is consistent with one of the assumptions in our simple toy model. Inspection of Figure fig:model_disp shows that the bulge contribution still dominates within the whole FOV, with the result that the kinematic signature of the young stars is dominated by that of the old stars, and hence the lower velocity dispersion from the young population is hidden by the dynamically hot galactic bulge. The increase in velocity dispersion at smaller radii is reduced, but not enough to be manifested as a drop in velocity dispersion.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 7213 as a nearby face-on LINER with a decades-long X-ray FRED flare, favoring a delayed tidal disruption event over disk-instability models.

quotes.quote: Basic properties of NGC 7213 NGC 7213 is a nearby face-on LINER (low-ionization nuclear emission-line region; Phillips1979,Filippenko1984 ) located at a luminosity distance d_ L =22.8 Mpc, a distance derived from a flat cosmology ( H_0=67.8 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 and _ M =0.308 from Planck-Collaboration2016 ) with a corrected redshift z_ corr.3K =0.005145 to the reference frame defined by the 3K cosmic microwave background http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu .

We summarize the main observational characteristics of this X-ray flare of NGC 7213 as follows: This flare lasts nearly 40 years. The peak bolometric luminosity of this flare is 0.01 L_ Edd . The X-ray light curve shows a FRED profile (see lc ). The e folding decay time-scale is approximately 8116 d ( 22.2 yr).

We then examined the possible variability models proposed in the literature, and find that either the newly triggered AGN scenario (driven by radiation-pressure instability) or the thermal-viscous DIM fails to explain some key properties observed in NGC 7213, thus we ague them unlikely. For examples, the radiation-pressure instability is incapable to produce the FRED profile, and decay time-scale from the thermal-viscous DIM is several orders of magnitude longer than that observed in NGC 7213. A delayed TDE of a main-sequence star is favoured.
