sources.summary: The paper identifies blueshifted highly ionized iron lines in NGC 7213 and argues they trace a hot wind from the LLAGN accretion flow.

quotes.quote: In this work, we identify significant Fe XXVI Ly and Fe XXV K emission lines from high-resolution Chandra grating spectra of the LLAGN in NGC 7213, a nearby Sa galaxy hosting a 10^8 M_ SMBH, confirming previous work. We find that these lines exhibit a blueshifted line-of-sight velocity of 1100 km s^ 1 and a high XXVI Ly to XXV K flux ratio implying for a 16 keV hot plasma. By confronting these spectral features with synthetic X-ray spectra based on our custom magnetohydrodynamical simulations, we find that the high-velocity, hot plasma is naturally explained by the putative hot wind driven by the hot accretion flow powering this LLAGN. Alternative plausible origins of this hot plasma, including stellar activities, AGN photoionization and the hot accretion flow itself, are quantitatively disfavored.

Lying at a distance of 22.75 Mpc ( z =0.00584, from the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu ), NGC 7213 is classified as a Sa galaxy with both Syfert and LINER (low-ionization nuclear emission line region) signatures, presumably powered by a central SMBH with a mass of 10^8 M_ estimated from stellar velocity dispersion 2002ApJ...579..530W . Using optical integral-field spectroscopic observations of the inner galaxy, estimated a black hole mass of 8_ 6 ^ +16 10^7 M_ from the M_ BH relation of . Hereafter we adopt M_ BH =10^8 M_ as the fiducial black hole mass. The bolometric luminosity of the LLAGN is estimated to be 9 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 by and 1.7 10^ 43 erg s^ 1 by based on broadband spectral energy distribution fitting.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7213 as a low-luminosity AGN with little internal absorption, significant iron lines, and Fermi/LAT constraints on nonthermal protons.

quotes.quote: NGC 7213 ( z = 0.00584 ), with L/L_ Edd 10^ 3 2005MNRAS.356..727S,2012MNRAS.424.1327E . does not show spectral indications for an inner, optically thick disc 2005MNRAS.356..727S,2003A&A...407L..21B,2010MNRAS.408..551L and has been described by the hot-flow model e.g. by 2016MNRAS.463.2287X . Its supermassive black hole mass is relatively poorly estimated to M = 8_ 6 ^ +16 10^7 M _ from the stellar velocity dispersion 2014MNRAS.438.3322S and 5 10^7 < M/M_ < 2 10^8 from the reverberation of the H line 2017MNRAS.472.2170S .

NGC 7213 The X-ray spectrum of NGC 7213 is dominated by intrinsic emission with a weak contribution of other components, therefore, we used the full energy ranges of all detectors. We built the spectral model with the internal X-ray emission described by kerrflow and we included additional components indicated by previous studies of NGC 7213 2003A&A...407L..21B,2005MNRAS.356..727S,2008MNRAS.389L..52B,2010MNRAS.408..551L . The definition of our model is given in the header of Table tab:fit . We do not find evidence of any internal absorption; the upper limit for the column density of an additional absorber is 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper detects soft X-ray emission lines in NGC 7213, including a blueshifted O VIII component, and models them as shock-heated circumnuclear gas from hot AGN wind feedback.

quotes.quote: Direct observational evidence for these hot winds with temperature around 10 keV, has been obtained through the detection of highly ionized iron emission lines with Doppler shifts in two prototypical low-luminosity AGNs, namely M81* and NGC 7213. In this work, we further identify blueshifted H-like O/Ne emission lines in the soft X-ray spectra of these two sources. These lines are interpreted to be associated with additional outflowing components possessing velocity around several 10^3 km/s and lower temperature ( 0.2-0.4 keV).

For NGC 7213, the best-fit photon index is =1.877 0.019 with C stat=2152 for 1834 d.o.f.. We also check the spectral variability between two RGS epochs of NGC 7213 spectra. While photon flux declines 45 over the 8-year interval, which is in agreement with the descending trend described by 2018MNRAS.475.1190Y . In NGC 7213, we two marginally resolved emission lines centered on 0.563_ 0.007 ^ +0.002 keV and 0.574 0.04 keV, which could be associated with the forbidden and resonance component He-like O VII K line respectively. Besides, an additional emission line has been detected at 98.4 confidence level 3^ +3 _ 2 10^3 km/s blue-shifted with respect to the rest-frame energy of O VIII Ly line.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 7213 as the sample's highest-surface-brightness LINER/Seyfert, with positive Mg and Fe5270 disk gradients, possible H II-ring links, and decoupled gaseous and stellar disks.

quotes.quote: NGC 7213 - SA(s)0 LINER This well-studied LINER/Seyfert galaxy has the highest surface brightness and earliest Hubble-type in our sample. NGC 7213 is also unique among our galaxies in having positive and Fe5270 gradients in the disk. Interestingly, Cid Fernandes et al. (1998) found no dilution of the Mg I+Mg H EW in the heart of NGC 7213, but strong dilution of features at shorter wavelengths. The EW of their Mg feature increases by about 50 from 25 arcsec to the nucleus.

We found that our ebbs at a radius of 15 arcsec before increasing by 35 (in EW) toward the core. Fe5270 shows much less pronounced enhancement toward the core but a comparable steep positive gradient in the outer disk. The positive gradients may be associated with a ring of H ii regions located at 20 40 arcsec in radius (Evans et al. 1996). Corsini et al. (2003) note that the gaseous and stellar disks of NGC 7213 are decoupled within 40 arcsec.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7213, a nearby face-on Seyfert 1/LINER, as an AGN with hybrid radio/X-ray correlations caused by changing hot-accretion regimes, predicting spectral softening above a critical luminosity.

quotes.quote: The active galactic nucleus (AGN) NGC 7213 shows a complex correlation between the monochromatic radio luminosity and the 2--10 keV X-ray luminosity , i.e. the correlation is unusually weak with p 0 (in the form ^p ) when is below a critical luminosity, and steep with p>1 when is above that luminosity. Such a hybrid correlation in individual AGNs is unexpected as it deviates from the Fundamental Plane of AGN activity. Interestingly, a similar correlation pattern is observed in the black hole X-ray binary H1743--322, where it has been modelled by switching between different modes of accretion. We propose that the flat - correlation of NGC 7213 is due to the presence of a luminous hot accretion flow, an accretion model whose radiative efficiency is sensitive to the accretion rate.

We study here NGC 7213, a nearby face-on Seyfert 1/LINER with a number of interesting properties. The source redshift is z_ =0.005839 , and its BH mass is estimated as =9.6^ +6.1 _ 4.1 10^7 \ Blank2005 .

We predict that NGC 7213 will enter into a two-phase accretion regime when 1.5 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 . When this happens, we predict a softening of the X-ray spectrum with the increasing flux and a steep radio/X-ray correlation.

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 7213 as a face-on Sa Seyfert/LINER LLAGN, finding radio/X-ray behavior consistent with fundamental-plane scatter and possible jet-fed radio lobes.

quotes.quote: NGC 7213 is a face-on Sa galaxy hosting a Seyfert 1.5 nucleus located at a distance of 25 Mpc assuming H _ 0 =71kms ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . Narrow low ionisation emission lines are observable in its nuclear spectrum, also making it a member of the LINER class Liner_ref . The radio properties of NGC 7213 are intermediate between those of radio-loud and radio-quiet AGN.

Previous radio studies at 8.4 GHz have not resolved any jet emission from the nucleus on scales 3 mas to 1 arcsec ( , , ).The Long Baseline Array (LBA) is an Australian six station VLBI instrument LBA ; LBA observations at 1.4 and 0.843 GHz have shown some evidence for larger scale emission that could be due to jet-fed radio lobes Blank_LBA .
Although the average radio and X-ray luminosities for NGC 7213 are in good agreement with the plane, we show that there is intrinsic scatter with respect to the plane for the individual data points.

sources.summary: NGC 7213 is highlighted as a unique Seyfert 1 whose spectrum lacks Compton reflection and whose resolved iron K line is unambiguously produced in the BLR.

quotes.quote: In order to test this hypothesis, NGC 7213 represents a unique opportunity. The X-ray spectrum of this source has no evidence for Compton reflection, a unique result among bright Seyfert 1s bianchi03b,bianchi04,lob10 . The observed neutral iron K line, therefore, cannot be produced in a Compton-thick material, like the disc or the torus.

Indeed, the iron line is resolved in a Chandra High-Energy Transmission Grating observation, with a measured FWHM which is in perfect agreement with the value measured for the broad component of the H in a simultaneous optical observation bianchi08b . Moreover, the observed equivalent width (EW) of the iron line is in agreement with an origin in the BLR, under reasonable assumptions on the geometrical distribution of the clouds, their covering factor, and their column density bianchi08b . Therefore, NGC 7213 is the only Seyfert 1 galaxy whose iron K line is unambiguously produced in the BLR.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7213 as a face-on Seyfert 1 galaxy, finding nuclear silicate emission, PAH-rich larger-scale structure, and CLUMPY torus properties consistent with AGN unification.

quotes.quote: NGC 7213 is a Sy1 of morphological type Sa viewed at a face-on angle. The broad components of the H and H motivate the Sy1 classification, on the other hand low ionisation lines like 6300 O i are relatively strong Filippenko1984 . In the same paper the authors attribute this low ionisation characteristic to high electron densities ( n_e 10^7 ) in the photoionized gas. StorchiBergmann1996 have identified H emission from the nucleus and a ring of H ii regions at a radius of 20 from the nucleus, (see fig. 6 in StorchiBergmann1996 ), and also noted a change in the profile of the H in relation to the observations of Filippenko1984 .

T-ReCS high spatial resolution data reveal silicate absorption at 9.7 in the central tens of parsecs of the Seyfert 2 NGC 1386, and silicate emission in the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 7213. Finally, by fitting CLUMPY models to the T-ReCS nuclear spectra we estimate the torus physical properties for both galaxies, finding line of sight inclinations consistent with the AGN unified model.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7213 as the lone low-luminosity AGN with long-term correlation data, finding harder-when-brighter X-ray behavior consistent with the model.

quotes.quote: In the low luminosity branch, to our knowledge currently there is only one source that has the correlation data, which comes from the long-term \ observations of NGC 7213 (Emmanoulopoulos et al. \ Emmanoulopoulos2012 ). The multi-waveband spectral energy distribution of this source, especially the absence of the big-blue-bump, also favors the truncated disc model (Emmanoulopoulos et al. \ Emmanoulopoulos2012 ). Fig. \ fig-ngc7213 shows the correlation of this source. Although the X-ray luminosity only spans by a factor 4 -5, it clearly shows a ``harder when brighter'' spectral behaviour, consistent with our model.
The mass of the black hole is 9.6^ +6.1 _ 4.1 10^7 (Bell et al. \ Bell2011 ). As shown by the red dashed line in Fig. \ fig-ngc7213 , it quantitatively agrees very well with the observations.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 7213 with a double-Gaussian broad-line profile, derives its BLR extinction in this study, and excludes it from the M-sigma fit because of LINER-like properties and failed H fitting.

quotes.quote: For fitting the BLR profiles, we used a single Gaussian model for some of AGN, but a second Gaussian profile was required to characterize the BLR profile for the following galaxies MCG-05-14-12, MCG-06-30-15, NGC 3783, NGC 4593, NGC 4235, NGC 6814 and NGC 7213. For the broad-line profiles that required double Gaussian models, both Gaussian profiles are combined with each other, and the resulting FWHM is estimated from the new, combined profile.

We note that A_ V (BLR) of NGC 7213 is obtained in this study using the same approach provided by Allan . This method can only be used to type 1 AGNs, and a more detailed explanation for extinction calculation is given by Allan .

We exclude NGC 7213 from this fit since it shows LINER-like properties; also the H fit for this galaxy fails. The resulting M_ BH _ relation for the LLAMA AGNs is then: (M_ BH /M_ ) = 8.14 ( 0.20) + 3.38 ( 0.65) _ 200 km s^ 1 , and the intrinsic scatter of this relation is = 0.32 0.06 .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 7213 appears unresolved in mid-IR images, has no extended PSF-subtracted emission, low wind-launching parameters, and disk-dominated model geometry.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies, NGC 6814, NGC 7314, and NGC 7465, only show evidence of faint extended emission at 3-5 _ bk levels. Finally, the PSF-subtracted images of NGC 4941 and NGC 7213 do not show evidence of extended emission.

Finally the low nuclear column density and Eddington ratio of NGC 4941 amd NGC 7213 place these galaxies in a region in Fig. fig:dustywinds where dusty winds are not launched. This is in agreement with the unresolved mid-IR morphology.

The NGC 6814 and NGC 7213 geometry Figure fig:CAT3D-WINDNGC7213 shows the specific model mid- and far-IR images for NGC 6814 and NGC 7213 at i=15^ . In the mid-IR a large fraction of the emission comes from the inner regions of the disk since the radial distribution of the clouds in the disk is moderately steep ( a=-1 ). Moreover, because in this model the wind component is not prominent the effects of self-obscuration from the cone walls appear to be small.

sources.summary: The paper studies 7 as a low-luminosity AGN with a steady X-ray spectral shape, ionized iron lines, and classifications spanning type I Seyfert and LINER behavior.

quotes.quote: We present the X-ray spectral results from the longest X-ray multi-mirror mission-Newton observation, 133 ks, of the low luminosity active galactic nucleus 7. The hardness ratio analysis of the X-ray light curves discloses a rather constant X-ray spectral shape, at least for the observed exposure time, enabling us to perform X-ray spectral studies using the total observed spectrum. Apart from a neutral emission line, we also detect narrow emission lines from the ionised iron species, Fe xxv and Fe xxvi .

7 (z=0.005839) is a face on Sa galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus (AGN). It has been classified as a type I Seyfert by phillips79 , based on its H _ linewidth (full width at zero intensity of 13000 km s ^ 1 ), but also as a low-ionisation nuclear emission line region (LINER) by filippenko84 , based on the study of a variety of optical emission lines which were observed to have a full width at half maximum of 200 to 2000 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: NGC 7213 is a large SA galaxy with a bright LINER or Seyfert 1 nucleus, old stellar continuum, broad emission, edge H II regions, and inferred black-hole inflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 7213: This large SA galaxy contains a very bright LINER or Seyfert 1 nucleus with broad H . An old stellar continuum is prominent in the WiFeS spectrum. Broad \ can be traced over 9 9 arcsec (0.9kpc). A number of \ regions are visible towards the edge of the field - see also Evans96 . Recently Schnorr-Muller14 have presented high spatial resolution ( 60 pc) integral field spectroscopy of the inner 0.8 1.1 kpc of this galaxy using the Gemini Multi-Object spectrograph, and have inferred a black hole mass of 10^8 M _ , and a mass inflow of a few tenths of a solar mass per year.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 7213 as a local AGN with only a lower limit on its cutoff energy, a very hot low-accretion-rate corona, and inefficient Compton cooling.

quotes.quote: For the case of NGC 7213, Ursini et al. 2015 recently found a high-energy cutoff of E _c>140 keV and, assuming a spherical geometry, tested the Comptonization model compps on the data set. The best fitting scenario is indeed a very high coronal temperature ( kT_e=295^ +70 _ 250 keV), with an optical depth =0.2^ +0.7 _ 0.1 . One interesting note must be stressed for these objects in which a lower limit for the cutoff energy is found (and therefore a very high coronal temperature). Three of the twelve sources discussed here showed lower limits for E _c (or extremely high values) and have very low accretion rates: NGC 5506 (E _c =720^ +130 _ 190 keV: Matt et al. 2015), NGC 2110 (E _c >210 keV: Marinucci et al. 2015) and NGC 7213 (E _c>140 keV: Ursini et al. 2015).

The corresponding accretion rates (calculated as m =L_ Bol /L_ Edd ) are m =0.007-0.14 , m =0.0025-0.037 and m 0.001 , respectively and we speculate that, in this particular objects, the low accretion rate leads to an inefficient Compton cooling mechanism.

sources.summary: The paper reports a Suzaku relativistic Fe K line in NGC 7213, contrasting with previous upper limits and requiring different inclination and inner-radius assumptions.

quotes.quote: Previous work on NGC 7213 ( Lobban+10 ) has not revealed the presence of a relativistic emission line, whereas our analysis shows this component to be present in the Suzaku spectra. In the work of Lobban+10 the same Suzaku observation of NGC 7213 was considered. In their work, a diskline model was used assuming an inclination of the disk of 30 ^ and the inner radius of 6r _g , but an upper limit was found for the flux of the relativistic Fe line. However, in our work different combinations of inner radii and inclinations were tested. We do detect a relativistic Fe K line with an equivalent width of 233 _ 80 ^ +80 eV adopting an inclination of 80 ^ and inner radius of 6r _g .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7213 as a nearly face-on Seyfert 1 Sa galaxy with a bright nucleus, faint accretion disk, spiral arms, nuclear dust extinction, and unresolved radio core.

quotes.quote: NGC 7213 is a Sa galaxy harbouring a Seyfert 1 AGN Malkan1998 . The galaxy has a bright nuclear point source with a very faint and featureless accretion disc. The galaxy is almost face on, hence giving a symmetrical appearance. The morphology of the galaxy is characterised by multiple spiral arms with severe dust extinction in the nuclear region. The 8.4 GHz VLA radio map of the galaxy shows an unresolved core Schmitt2001 with signs of variability Blank2005 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7213 as a Sy1 source without absorption variations, preferring smooth gas and clumpy dust in a thin disk whose inclination leaves the accretion disk unobstructed.

quotes.quote: Panel 4 of Figure fig:cartoon_Sy1 (e.g. NGC 7213): Sy1 source without absorption variations, low covering factor, and small half opening angles of the dusty structure. A combination of a smooth distribution of gas and a clumpy distribution of dust is preferred for this object. A geometrically-thin disk of gas and dust, where gas is smoothly distributed while clouds hold most of the dust, is able to explain the observed signatures. The smooth distribution prevents from observing absorption variability of the source. Furthermore, the inclination angle is consistent with the unobstructed view of the source.

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC 7213 among outliers to the proposed dust composition, with residuals near the 10 micron feature and requiring MieOl2, CDEOl1, CDEOl3, and DL2 for an acceptable 10 micron silicate-feature fit.

quotes.quote: We can see that five objects (Mrk 1210, NGC 7213, PG 0804+761, PG 1211+143, and PG 1351+640) are outliers in this distribution with ^2_ >2 . The middle panel of Figure fig:all_residuals shows the residuals of the spectral fits for our 49 objects using this combination, and it highlights these five objects. Comparing these residuals with the ones obtained in the best fits (top panel), we can see that the main difference is that the latter have larger ones ( >10 ) at 9 m < < 12 m for NGC 7213, PG 0804+761, PG 1211+143 and PG 1351+640.

For NGC 7213, PG 0804+761, PG 1211+143, and PG 1351+640, the residuals of the particular combination differ from the ones of the best fit mainly at 8 m < < 13 m , meaning that the former can not reproduce well the 10 \ m silicate feature in the spectra of these objects. To obtain an acceptable fit model, NGC 7213 needs MieOl2, CDEOl1, CDEOl3, and DL2 instead of MieOl01 and MieOl3; PG 0804+761 needs CDEOl1, DL01, and Min3 instead of MieOl01 and MieOl3; PG 1211+143 needs CDEOl1 instead of MieOl01; and PG 1351+640 needs CDEOl1, DL01, and Min3 instead of MieOl3.

sources.summary: The paper interprets NGC 7213's gas kinematics as a geometrically decoupled warped gaseous disk, notes counterrotating gas, and links its disturbed H I environment to ongoing acquisition or merging.

quotes.quote: Major-axis kinematics The only noteworthy features observed in the major-axis kinematics of the ionized-gas and stellar component are the counterrotation of gas with respect to stars found in NGC 7213, and the reversal of gas rotation measured in the outermost regions of NGC 7377.

A geometrical decoupling between the gaseous and stellar disks characterizes NGC 7213 in the radial region we covered with our spectroscopic observations ( 40'' ). Indeed, the orientation of the circumnuclear ring-like structure observed in the map of the H emission obtained by Hameed Devereux (1999) does not coincide with that of the stellar disk as derived from the near-infrared photometry by Mulchaey, Regan Kundu (1997).

A giant filament of ionized gas is located at 20 kpc south of the galaxy (Hameed Devereux 1999) and the HI velocity field reveals NGC 7213 to be a highly disturbed system undergoing an acquisition event (Hameed et al. 2001). Therefore we conclude that the kinematic and photometric properties of NGC 7213 are consistent with a warped gaseous disk in the context of an ongoing merge process.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7213 as the prior individual-AGN harder-when-brighter comparison, noting extensive RXTE observations and a relatively simple power-law spectrum.

quotes.quote: In addition to the data from this work, we also include data from NGC 7213 Emmanoulopoulos2012 in the discussion, to allow comparison with what was, until now, the only confirmed detection of harder-when-brighter behaviour in an individual AGN. NGC 7213 is not part of the Palomar sample, and has not been observed extensively by Swift . However, it has been observed extensively (882 times) by RXTE and is known to possess a relatively simple power law spectrum Emmanoulopoulos2013b . We therefore do not include NGC 7213 within our discussion of Swift spectral fitting (Section spectralFitting ) or of the properties of the Palomar sample, but take the results of the RXTE spectral fitting from Emmanoulopoulos2012 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7213 as a comparison galaxy, describing its O VII luminosity, S0 classification, distance, AGN and black hole properties, star formation rate, and likely AGN-related outflow.

quotes.quote: In terms of emission, we have previously discussed NGC 7213, which has an observed luminosity of L_ OVIIr = 1.5 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 with RGS starling05,salvestrini20 . This is a S0 at D=23 Mpc, with a bolometric L_ AGN = 1.7 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , a SMBH with mass 10^8 at _ edd 10^ 3 , a SFR of 1.0 , and a strong outflow. A significant fraction of the emission from this galaxy may be due to the AGN itself, and/or to an AGN-driven outflow. We include NGC 7213 in Figure fig_obs , as a potentially `normal' galaxy against which we can compare.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 7213 as a LINER with correlated X-ray/radio variability, low accretion rate, and hard-state behavior that makes such correlation expected.

quotes.quote: Recently Bell2010 have studied the X-ray and radio variability of the liner NGC 7213 and find a weakly significant correlation between those bands.

Bell2010 find a significant correlation between X-ray and radio emission in the LINER NGC 7213 with factors of 2 or more variability in both bands. Given its very low accretion rate, NGC 7213 is thought to be a hard state system so correlated radio/X-ray variability is expected.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7213 as a narrow-line case where ionised reflection is detected, but with smaller reflection strength and inclination pegging that weakens robustness.

quotes.quote: Evidence for a broad and ionised reflection component at 95 confidence is found in five observations: MCG+8-11-11, Mrk 110, NGC 7469(1), Mrk 6 and NGC 7213, with the first three being significant even at 99 confidence.
NGC 7213 differs from the well-fit observations in requiring a smaller reflection strength of R = 0.11 .
The inclinations of the discs in NGC 7213 and NGC 7469(1) peg at the model limits i = 0 and 85 respectively and thus the detections in these cases cannot be considered robust.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 7213's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 7213 is a face-on lenticular galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0058 ( D 23 Mpc) with a borderline Sy 1.5/LINER nucleus phillips_optical_1979,filippenko_ngc_1984 . It has been studied well in X-rays and also belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample (see bell_x-ray_2011 and emmanoulopoulos_harder_2012 for a recent detailed study). The nucleus is unresolved at radio wavelengths down to milliarcsecond scales, has a flat radio spectrum, and has an intermediate radio-loudness bransford_radio-luminous_1998,schmitt_jet_2001,thean_high-resolution_2000, blank_8.4-ghz_2005 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7213 as a low-luminosity AGN comparison, noting that one tenuous-flow extension can approximate it but its observed slope is steeper than the hadronic model.

quotes.quote: We also use fits to the - _ 2-10 anticorrelation in a large sample of low-luminosity AGNs from Gu Cao (2009) and in NGC 7213 from Emmanoulopoulos et al. \ (2012).

The two model points located close to Cen A are for the models (with =0.5 ) with very tenuous flows, having 0.01 and kT_ e 1 MeV; extension of this model to lower L can approximately reproduce the relation assessed in NGC 7213 if the average slope of the X-ray spectrum is considered, however, these X-ray spectra strongly deviate from a power-law.

Finally, we note that the slope of the - _ 2-10 correlation observed in individual AGNs, NGC 4151 (the change between dim and bright states) and NGC 7213, is slightly steeper than predicted by the hadronic models.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 7213 as a low-luminosity AGN with tentative X-ray/radio correlations, week-scale radio lags, significant radio variability, and a compact-jet interpretation.

quotes.quote: Bell2011 reported tentative evidence for an X-ray/radio correlation in the low-luminosity AGN (and possible hard-state analogue) NGC 7213, with 8.4 GHz radio lagging the X-rays by a few weeks (and 4.8 GHz radio lagging by two weeks longer still), assuming that the correlation is real. Such a lag is consistent with a relatively compact jet in this AGN, which has a black hole mass around 10^ 8 M _ . Although NGC 7213 showed significant radio variability and some evidence for an X-ray/radio correlation, the lower mass and higher accretion rate radio-quiet Narrow Line Seyfert 1 AGN, NGC 4051 shows only marginal evidence for radio variability with 25 fractional rms, with no clear evidence for a correlation with X-rays which are highly variable Jones2011 .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 7213 strongly constrains hot-flow models despite no significant LAT signal, with X-ray spectra and gamma-ray limits ruling out some parameter regimes.

quotes.quote: NGC 7213 Despite the large uncertainty in M , NGC 7213 gives interesting constraints on the hot flow model; crucially, it is observed at _ 2-10 keV 10^ 4 , between the X-ray bright Seyfert 1 galaxies discussed above and NGC 4258.
The likelihood analysis of the Fermi /LAT data does not reveal any significant signal around NGC 7213 (TS < 1 ), and we computed the ULs given in Table tab:gamma .

Given this constraint, we used _ 0.2-1 GeV predicted for thermal distribution of protons to rule out a flow with a high (equipartition) value of around a high- a black hole for NGC 7213 and and NGC 4151; for the latter this result is model dependent.

sources.summary: NGC 7213 is used as a high-resolution nuclear-spectra comparison where PAH subtraction of IRS data is judged to approximate the nuclear emission well.

quotes.quote: For instance, in rus14 there is no PAH emission detected using high resolution nuclear spectra from T-ReCs when compared with IRS observations of NGC 7213 and NGC 1386.

To illustrate the effect of the starburst subtraction method, we show in Figure high the high resolution data from Michelle and T-ReCs of the galaxies Mrk 3, NGC 1386 and NGC 7213, compared to the IRS spectra.
However, in the cases of NGC 1386 and NGC 7213, we believe that the PAH subtraction approach represents a good approximation of the nuclear emission.

gmw_00000255.name: NGC 7314
gmw_00000255.overview: NGC 7314 is an inclined, barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1610.06243, arXiv:1310.2770] hosting an active galactic nucleus with classifications ranging from Seyfert 1 to Seyfert 2 [arXiv:1109.1660, arXiv:2212.11434]. The galaxy exhibits rapid, high-amplitude X-ray variability [arXiv:1610.06243, arXiv:astro-ph/0504279] and contains a circumnuclear star-forming region [arXiv:2212.11434] alongside an extended optical narrow-line region [arXiv:2107.00244].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7314 as a nearby Seyfert with complex X-ray absorption, variability, mixed optical classifications, and archival spectra interpreted in an AGN unification context.

quotes.quote: We aim at studying the complex absorbed X-ray spectrum of the Narrow Line Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 7314. We collected available public X-ray data from the archives of , , and . The spectra were analyzed using the fitting package SPEX .

NGC 7314 is a nearby ( z=0.004763 , Koribalski et al. ) and bright ( F(2-10 keV )=3.7 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , this study) Seyfert galaxy cataloged in the Piccinotti sample (Piccinotti et al. ). This source has been optically classified as a Seyfert 2 (e.g. Morris Ward ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7314 as a barred spiral Seyfert 1.9 with extreme X-ray variability, broad Fe K emission, and optical/NIR BLR components.

quotes.quote: NGC 7314 is a barred spiral galaxy (SABb) hosting a Seyfert 1.9 active nucleus zoghbi13 , confirmed by the the detection of a broad H in the HST spectrum. Moreover, a clear evidence was found for a broad H in the polarized flux lumsden04 . The X-ray behaviour of the source is extreme, with a strong variability on all observed timescales and it is thought to be a type 2 counterpart to the NLS1 class dewangan05 . Furthermore, X-ray observations (from ASCA , Chandra and XMM ) revealed a broad component in the Fe K at 6.4 keV. Interestingly, the line response to the rapid variations of the continuum is different for the narrow and the broad components, suggesting different origins of the corresponding gas ebrero11 .

The best fit of the most relevant emission lines of NGC 7314 are shown in Figure fig:NGC7314 . In the X-shooter optical spectrum, beside the narrow and broad components of H and H (FWHM = 1097 and FWHM = 1330 , respectively) evidence was found of intermediate components. In particular, in the H + [ O III ] region two intermediate components with a blueshift of 47 and 67 respectively were found.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7314 as a Seyfert 1.9/2 AGN with typical Seyfert-2 line fluxes, broad nuclear H emission, exceptionally rapid X-ray variability, and a steep NLS1-like primary power law.

quotes.quote: NGC 7314 has an optical spectrum and line fluxes typical of a Seyfert 2 (Morris Ward 1988; Winkler 1992; Joguet et al. 2001). An HST spectrum of the nuclear region of NGC 7314 revealed broad component of H , consistent with a Seyfert 1.9 classification (Hughes et al. 2003). NGC 7314 is well known for its rapid, large amplitude X-ray variability (Turner et al. 1987; Yaqoob et al. 1996). Yaqoob et al. (2003) detected variable, redshifted narrow lines from He-like and H-like iron using HETG spectrum of NGC 7314.

Among the three AGNs, NGC 7314 shows the most remarkable rapid variability throughout the observation. The most extreme event occurred around 35500 after the start of the observation, the count rate increased by 1.25 20 counts s^ 1 within just 300 .

The rapid X-ray variability observed from NGC 7582, NGC 5506 and NGC 7314 are similar to that of NLS1s. In addition to the extreme variability, the three AGNs show steep 2-12 primary power-law (NGC 7582: = 2.26_ 0.17 ^ +0.14 ; NGC 5506: = 1.98_ 0.02 ^ +0.05 ; NGC 7314: = 2.19_ 0.06 ^ +0.09 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds only faint or unresolved mid-IR emission in NGC 7314, despite an optical NLR and molecular gas peaks that may indicate nuclear gas clearing.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies, NGC 6814, NGC 7314, and NGC 7465, only show evidence of faint extended emission at 3-5 _ bk levels. Finally, the PSF-subtracted images of NGC 4941 and NGC 7213 do not show evidence of extended emission.

NGC 4941, NGC 7465 and NGC 7314 have extended optical NLRs, which are detected with optical IFU observations and/or HST imaging see and Sousa et al. in preparation, respectively ErrozFerrer2019, Ferruit2000 . In NGC 6814, the NLR and the coronal line region are rather compact and there is evidence for ionized outflows on these scales 1 , see MuellerSanchez2011 . In these four galaxies we do not detect extended mid-IR emission.

NGC 6814 and NGC 7314 are close to the blowout region, where polar dusty outflows should not be prominent. The CO(3-2) map of NGC 6814 of GB21 (their Fig. 10) shows that there is little molecular gas in the nuclear and circumnuclear regions of this galaxy, in agreement with being in the blowout region and an unresolved mid-IR morphology. NGC 7314, on the other hand, contains more molecular gas with two CO peaks located symmetrically around the AGN in the inner 40 pc (Fig. 13 of GB21). This might indicate that some clearing of molecular gas at the AGN location already took place in the nuclear regions of this galaxy. For this galaxy, the mid-IR emission is mostly unresolved.

sources.summary: The paper presents a multiwavelength study of NGC 7314's nucleus, finding a type 1 Seyfert spectrum, ionization-cone morphology, compact radio emission, circumnuclear star formation, and variable X-rays.

quotes.quote: For that reason, we performed a multiwavelength analysis of the nuclear region of the nearby Seyfert galaxy NGC 7314, using an optical data cube obtained with the Integral Field Unit from the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph, together with Hubble Space Telescope images, X-ray data from the XMM--Newton and the Nuclear Spectroscopic Telescope Array and radio data from Atacama Large Millimeter/Submillimeter Array. The goals were to study the nuclear and circumnuclear emission, the emission of the AGN and the gas kinematics. The optical spectrum shows the emission of a Seyfert nucleus, with broad components in the H and H emission lines, characterising a type 1 AGN, with a spectrum rich in coronal emission lines. The spatial morphology of the [ O iii ] 5007 suggests the presence of an ionization cone, west of the nucleus, meanwhile the east cone seems to be obscured by dust.

X-rays analyses showed that there are variations in the flux; however, we did not detect any variations in the column density along the line of sight. Its variability may be a consequence of changes in the AGN accretion rate.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 7314 among low-noise iron-line PSD sources, reports a low BPL fit probability, and excludes it from mean soft-band residuals because of PSD quality.

quotes.quote: The Poisson noise dominates the observed variations at frequencies higher than 10^ 3 Hz, except from four sources, namely MCG--6-30-15, NGC 4051, Ark 564, and NGC 7314. The iron-line band PSD in these sources is well defined over the lowest sampled frequency decade, i.e. \ between 10^ 4 and 10^ 3 Hz. This is not surprising, as these sources are among the X-ray brightest in our sample (hence the Poisson noise level is low) and they probably host a low mass BH as well, implying large amplitude variations at high-frequencies.
In fact, the null hypothesis probability for the best-fitting BPL model fit to the iron-line PSDs of NGC 7314 and Ark 564 is rather low ( p_ null =1.6 and 0.6 per cent, respectively).
We decided not to include NGC 7314 during the estimation the mean soft-band residuals, due to its poor quality of its soft-band PSD see emmanoulopoulos16 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7314 as a source whose lag spectrum can be fitted by a combined continuum plus reverberation model, with constraints affected by its black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: The only exceptions were Ark 564 and NGC 7314, whose observed time-lag spectra we also fitted to a combined model A+B as well for reasons we discuss in Sect. subsec62 below.

The best-fit h values for Ark 564 and NGC 7314 are 83r_ g and 100r_ g (which is the highest allowed fitting value for h ), respectively. The X-ray source height is consistent with the value of 100r_ g for Ark 564. The lower limit on the best-fit h value is 28r_ g and 82r_ g for Ark 564 and NGC 7314, respectively.

The quality of the combined model A+B fit is similar to that of model A: ^2_ min / dof =14.6/18 , and ^2_ min / dof =26.6/20 in the case of Ark 564 and NGC 7314, respectively. This result shows that the observed iron line vs continuum time-lags of Ark 564 and NGC 7314 can be fitted well by a combination of a continuum plus reverberation component, the latter of which corresponds to a low h and high a value.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7314 as a Seyfert 1 comparison object with stronger covariance in spectral-fit parameters but a still constrained folding energy.

quotes.quote: Next, in Figure fig:NGC7314_conts we show the same plots but for the Seyfert 1 NGC 7314. Here there is noticeably more covariance between parameters, especially when one of them is the folding energy. Unlike NGC 3227, the error bars on folding energy are quite uneven, with the best-fit value lying towards the lower end of the confidence interval. Despite the misshapen contours, the folding energy is still constrained.

sources.summary: NGC 7314 shows a significant but narrow excess, dips in the iron-line region, and a red-wing excess even though much of the iron line is not variable.

quotes.quote: NGC 4151(2), Mrk 766(3), MCG-6-30-15(2) and NGC 7314(1) show significant, but narrow excesses.
Three observations, NGC 4593, NGC 7314(1) and NGC 7469(2) appear to have dips in the iron line region of their rms spectra.
NGC 7314(1) has both variability dips and an excess across the red wing while the rest of the iron line does not appear variable.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 7314's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 7314 is an inclined late-type barred spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 18.3 2.1 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a Sy 1.9-2 nucleus with polarized broad emission lines hughes_atlas_2003,morris_spectrophotometry_1988,lumsden_spectropolarimetry_2004 . It is highly variable in X-rays by up to a factor of four turner_dramatic_1987 and belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. report an unresolved radio core at arcsecond resolution, while report a double source with 4 360 pc separation along a PA 170 .

sources.summary: NGC 7314 is the Seyfert 2 member of the sample; it shows one occultation, BLR-located absorption, and an Fe-line equivalent width inconsistent with the Seyfert 1 relation.

quotes.quote: We detect three occultations in NGC 3227 and one occultation in NGC 3783, NGC 7314, and NGC 3516, together with the well-known occultations in Mrk 766.

We also find a good anti-correlation with a slope of -187 62 between the known masses of the supermassive black hole in the center of the galaxies with the equivalent width (EW) of the 6.4 keV Fe line for the five Seyfert 1 galaxies of our sample, while the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 7314 shows an average EW value of 100 11 eV inconsistent with the above anti-correlation.

proposed that neutral gas grazing the clumpy torus in NGC 7314 and crossing our line of sight is responsible for the variations in the absorption properties in NGC 7314, which is consistent with our findings but our study shows that the absorber is located in the BLR.

gmw_00000256.name: NGC 7469
gmw_00000256.overview: NGC 7469 is a barred spiral luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2203.04585, arXiv:2402.17479] hosting a highly variable Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.07901, arXiv:2302.09152]. The central engine drives a multiphase ionized outflow [arXiv:1612.07508, arXiv:2209.01210] and is surrounded by a circumnuclear starburst ring [arXiv:1504.03743, arXiv:2209.06741].

sources.summary: NGC 7469 remains formally difficult to fit; the higher signal-to-noise observation shows strongly blurred reflection, possibly Kerr-like, hidden in lower-quality data.

quotes.quote: Despite the multiple complexities, the fits to MCG-6-30-15(1,2) and NGC 7469(2) remain unacceptable. It should be noted that these spectra have very high signal-to-noise ratio, so that any residual systematic uncertainty e.g. in the instrumental calibration may contribute to the poor fit. Also, while these spectra have formally poor fits, none is ``catastrophic" by our previous definition ( _ ^ 2 >2.0 ).

Another interesting example of similar nature is NGC 7469, where one observations shows a narrow line, but the second observation with higher signal--to--noise exhibits strongly blurred reflection, possbly even indicitive of a Kerr black hole. We can explain the apparent lack of broad line in the lower S/N case by the fact that the reflection component is very strongly blurred and therefore very diffficult to detect. Indeed the reflection component in this case is apparently so strongly blurred that it is very difficult to tell whether it is truly reflection from the inner disk or a curved continuum. What is clear, however, is that data with ultra-high signal--to--noise ratio is required to detected such components. The component required in the second observation, which has 360,000 counts, is not detected in the first, which has 80,000, but the best--fit parameters of the reflection in the 80,000 count spectrum are entirely consistent.

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA dense-gas tracers to study NGC 7469, finding local disc instability in the star-forming ring and a characteristic instability scale matching star-cluster complexes.

quotes.quote: We present an unprecedented measurement of the disc stability and local instability scales in the luminous infrared Seyfert 1 host, NGC 7469, based on ALMA observations of dense gas tracers and with a synthesized beam of 165 132 pc. While we confirm that non-circular motions are not significant in redistributing the dense interstellar gas in this galaxy, we find compelling evidence that the dense gas is a suitable tracer for studying the origin of its intensely high-mass star forming ring-like structure. Our derived disc stability parameter Q accounts for a thick disc structure and its value falls below unity at the radii in which intense star formation is found. Furthermore, we derive the characteristic instability scale _c and find a striking agreement between our measured scale of 180 pc, and the typical sizes of individual complexes of young and massive star clusters seen in high-resolution images.

The central kpc region of the barred Seyfert 1 host NGC 7469 displays a number of morphological and kinematic features that provide interesting clues on the dynamical behaviour of the interstellar medium and its connection with star formation across the disc in this galaxy. Previous high resolution studies carried out by and have shown that the gas rich central region displays intense star formation in a R 0.5-1 kpc ring-like structure contributing to up to 2/3 of the galaxy's bolometric luminosity ( L_ bol = 3 10^ 11 L_ ). The ring-like structure is dominated by young ( <100 Myr) and massive ( 10^5 10^7 \ M_ ) super star clusters, with the enclosed mass of 2.7 10^9 \ M_ Mauderetal1994,DiazSantos2007 . The individual clusters are grouped in 150 -- 200 pc-scale star forming complexes DiazSantos2007 .

sources.summary: The paper presents 70 days of CARMA 95 GHz monitoring of NGC 7469, finding genuine rapid core variability consistent with a compact coronal origin linked to X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: In Sect. sec:summary we discuss the results and draw conclusions. tab1.tex NGC 7469 ( z=0.01588, D_L=71.2 Mpc) was drawn from a recent AGN sample selected based on the high X-ray brightness and documented variability behar15 . Its 95 GHz flux density is in excess ( 1.5 ) over the steep spectrum at low-radio frequencies see behar15 . pereztorres09 reported seven 8.4-GHz observations of NGC 7469 over 8 years and its radio core (0 5) flux density was consistently between 12-13 mJy, i.e., no significant variability. We observed continuum emission in the 3 mm (95 GHz) window from the nucleus of NGC 7469 with the CARMA telescope. We monitored NGC 7469 over a period of 70 days with a cadence ranging from 1 day to a few days.

This variability amplitude of NGC 7469 at 95 GHz is larger and on shorter time scales than that reported at 8.4 GHz by pereztorres09 for the same AGN with annual monitoring over 8 years. The 95-GHz fractional variability amplitude is consistent within the errors with the X-ray value and, moreover, the time scale for variability in the two bands is similar, i.e., of the order of 1 day. In conclusion, we statistically confirmed that NGC 7469 has a variable core at 95 GHz. The variability and the radio-sphere size ( 10^ 4 pc) are consistent with a compact core akin to the X-ray source.

sources.summary: The paper models archival HCN data for NGC 7469 and finds that both complex and simple mass-distribution models imply a thick disk with significant intrinsic dispersion.

quotes.quote: We also make use of HCN (1-0) data for NGC 7469 presented in , which have a beam size of 2.0 . We adopt a distance of 58 Mpc and an inclination of 45 .

When applying our kinematics modelling procedure as described above, the best-fit values for the disk size and disk thickness are 2.41 and 0.43 respectively. And, as summarised in Table tab:model_int , accounting for rotation and dispersion, we estimate the dynamical mass to be M_ dyn [ M_ ] = 9.30 . In comparison, when using a single Gaussian profile to define the mass distribution (hereafter called the `simple model'), we leave the disk size, disk thickness, and mass scale as three free parameters.

There are two key results here. The first is that the disk size and thickness are consistent between the two models, as is M_ dyn . The second is that the model implies the disk is thick, with an intrinsic /v 0.4 .

sources.summary: The paper presents a 20-year optical monitoring campaign of Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 7469, describing its interaction, circumnuclear star-forming ring, broad emission lines, variable continuum, and line variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 is probably interacting with another galaxy, IC 5283, that is about 60-70 Mpc far away see, e.g. mar94 . Around the active nucleus, a bright circumnuclear ring is located at an angular distance of 2.5 arcsec (or 1 kpc) from the nucleus. The stellar ring shows star-forming activity starburst region, see wil86,wil91,dia07,iz15 , with possible supernova explosions. E.g. the supernova SN 2000ft has been observed in the circumnuclear region in the radio col01,per09 and optical light col07 The spectrum of NGC 7469 shows broad emission lines superposed with strong narrow lines, which is typical for Seyfert 1 galaxies.

We present the results of the long-term (20-year period, from 1996 to 2015) optical spectral monitoring of the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 7469. The variation in the light-curves of the broad He II 4686 H and H lines, and the continuum at 5100 \ and 6300 \ have been explored. The maximum of activity was in 1998, and the variability in the continuum and lines seems to have two periods of around 1200 and 2600 days, however these periodicities should be taken with caution because of the red-noise.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 7469 has a PAH-profile turnover at its starburst ring and estimates stellar light contributes about 20 percent of the nuclear radiation field.

quotes.quote: A prominent example of the change in behaviour at distances > 5,000 is NGC 7469 (green labelled curve in Figure fig:pahvsrsub ). This source has a well known starburst ring at about 2 arcsec distance from the AGN genzel1995,diazsantos2007 , which coincides with this turnover. As such, the turnover in this source indicates that local star formation activity clearly is the dominant contributor to the PAH emission at these radii.

For NGC 7469, we can make a similar assessment. davies2007 report a K band luminosity of the stellar component with the same 0.8 arcsec aperture. Given their approximate conversion to bolometric stellar luminosity, we can estimate L_ NSC ( NGC 7469 ) 8 10^ 43  erg s ^ 1 within 3000 r_ sub . Using the X-ray luminosity of the AGN in Table  tab:lxrsub and the bolometric conversion by marconi2004 , we obtain a bolometric luminosity of the AGN of L_ AGN ( NGC 7469 ) 3 10^ 44  erg s ^ 1 . From that, we estimate a stellar contribution of 20 per cent to the total radiation field.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7469 as a Seyfert-1 AGN and luminous infrared galaxy, modelling its multi-component AGN wind and linking diffuse soft X-rays to its nuclear starburst ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 is a Seyfert-1 AGN at the redshift of 0.016268 Spri05 with a SMBH mass of about 1 10^ 7 M_ Pete14 . The host galaxy of is classified as a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG). The nucleus of is surrounded by a well-known starburst ring, which has been detected in multiple energy bands from radio to optical/UV (e.g. Wils91,Maud94 ). Previous high-resolution UV and X-ray spectroscopic studies reported about the presence of a nuclear wind in this AGN.

The results of our photoionisation modelling and thermal stability analysis suggest that the absorber components in are consistent with being a thermally-driven wind from the AGN torus. Finally, from analysis of the zeroth-order ACIS/HETG data, we discover that the X-ray emission between 0.2--1 keV is spatially extended over 1.5--12 . This diffuse soft X-ray emission is explained by coronal emission from the nuclear starburst ring in . The star formation rate inferred from this diffuse soft X-ray emission is consistent with those found by far-infrared studies of .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7469's RGS spectrum, finding multiphase emission-line regions, three warm-absorber phases, starburst plasma, and plasma distances from near the black hole to beyond the torus.

quotes.quote: We aim to investigate and characterise the photoionised X-ray emission line regions within the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 7469. We applied the photoionisation model, PION , within the spectral fitting code SPEX to analyse the 640 ks reflection grating spectrometer (RGS) spectrum of NGC 7469 gathered during an XMM-Newton observing campaign in 2015. We find the emission line region in NGC 7469 to be multiphased, consisting of two narrow components with ionisation parameters of log = 0.4 and 1.6. A third, broad emission component, with a broadening velocity of v_b 1400 km s 1 and an outflow velocity of v_ out 4500 km s 1 is required to fit the residuals in the O VII triplet at around 22 .

The collisionally ionised plasma from the star burst region in NGC 7469 has a plasma temperature of 0.32 keV and an outflow velocity of -280 km s 1 , which is consistent with previous results in this campaign. In addition, we model the photoionised plasma of the warm absorber (WA) in NGC 7469 and find that it consists of three photoionised phases with different values of , N_H and v_ out . The upper bound distances of these WA components are 1.9, 0.3, and 0.6 pc, respectively, consistent with archival results. The environment of NGC 7469 is a complex mix of plasma winds absorbing and emitting X-rays.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7469 as a barred Seyfert LIRG with a circumnuclear disk and starburst ring, using ALMA CO and [C I] observations to trace AGN-affected gas chemistry.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Position of the nucleus & & (2) _ ICRS & 23 ^ h 03 ^ m 15 ^ s .617 & _ ICRS & +08 52 26 .00

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 is an active barred spiral galaxy located at D = 71.2 Mpc ( z = 0.01641 and 1 = 334 pc) Based on the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED). A standard cosmology of H_0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _M = 0.3, and _ = 0.7 is assumed throughout this paper. . It hosts a luminous type-1 Seyfert nucleus with an absorption-corrected 2--10 keV luminosity of L_ 2-10keV = 1.5 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 2014ApJ...783..106L , as evidenced by broad Balmer emission lines 2014ApJ...795..149P . The time-variability in UV to X-ray bands 2000ApJ...544..734N,2000ApJ...535...58K , as well as fast ionized outflows emanating from the nucleus 2007A&A...466..107B,2020arXiv200204031C confirm the genuine existence of AGN in this galaxy. NGC 7469 is also categorized as luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) owing to its high IR luminosity L_ 8-1000 m = 10^ 11.6 L_ , 2003AJ....126.1607S . There is a 300 pc diameter circumnuclear disk (CND) at the center e.g., 2004ApJ...602..148D,2015ApJ...811...39I , which is surrounded by a luminous starburst ring with a radius of 500 pc e.g., 2003AJ....126..143S,2007ApJ...661..149D .

In this paper we present high resolution ( 130 pc) ALMA observations of multiple CO and C ^0 lines toward the central kpc region of the luminous type-1 Seyfert galaxy NGC 7469. The region consists of the CND (central 1 ) and the surrounding SB ring (radius 1 .5 ). All of the targeted emission lines, namely CO(1--0), CO(2--1), CO(3--2), ^ 13 CO(2--1), and [ C 1 ] (1--0), are successfully detected both in the CND and the SB ring.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7469 as a resolved example where AGN-ionized regions have higher velocity dispersion and broad Gaussian components trace high-velocity outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:resolved_BPT_NGC7469 clearly shows that the pixels or regions ionised by the AGN are skewed towards higher w_ 80 values in NGC 7469. Star forming regions show a mean w_ 80 value of 240 150 km s ^ 1 , while the AGN ionised regions show a higher fraction of w_ 80 values greater than 600 km s ^ 1 , with a mean of 360 140 km s ^ 1 . Similar distributions have previously been observed in the case of MaNGA galaxies in wylezalek20 where the AGN ionised pixels show a much higher velocity compared to star formation ionised pixels. The bottom right panel in Fig. fig:resolved_BPT_NGC7469 shows that even with the finer sampling of higher spatially resolved MUSE data (compared with the lower resolution and coarsely sampled MaNGA sample), the previous literature results hold true. We note that this does not mean that the spaxels with AGN ionisation will necessarily show higher w_ 80 value, as from figure fig:resolved_BPT_NGC7469 it is clear that even spaxels with AGN ionisation can show lower w_ 80 values. However, the AGN ionised regions will tend to show a collectively higher ionised gas velocity dispersion than the star forming pixels.

Figure fig:narrow_broad_distribution shows an example of the narrow and broad Gaussian width distributions in NGC 7469, which supports this assumption for the analysis presented in this paper. The top left panel of Fig. fig:narrow_broad_distribution shows the centroid distribution of the narrow Gaussian component which shows a smooth rotation-like profile about an axis oriented approximately along the S-W direction. Such a smooth profile is not present in the centroid map of the broad component (top right panel in Fig. fig:narrow_broad_distribution ). Furthermore, the bottom panel in Fig. fig:narrow_broad_distribution shows the width (FWHM) distribution of the individual narrow and broad Gaussian components across the MUSE FoV. The narrow component width is always less than 250 km s ^ 1 , while the broad component reaches values of >600 km s ^ 1 in some pixels, suggesting that this component is indeed tracing the high velocity outflowing gas possibly driven by the AGN e.g., luminari21 .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7469 with VLT/MUSE and Chandra, resolving ionized outflows, confirming a starburst wind, and finding a 2 kpc AGN-driven outflow with measured mass and energy rates.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 7469 possesses a prominent nuclear starburst ring and a luminous active galactic nucleus (AGN). Evidence of an outflow in the innermost nuclear region has been found in previous works. We detect the ionized gas outflow on a larger scale in the galaxy using the archival VLT/MUSE and Chandra observations. The optical emission lines are modeled using two Gaussian components, and a non-parametric approach is applied to measure the kinematics of [ O iii ] and H emitting gas.

Line ratio diagnostics and spatially resolved maps are derived to examine the origin of the outflow. The kpc-scale kinematics of [ O iii ] is dominated by a blueshifted component whereas velocity map of H shows a rotational disk with complex non-rotational substructure. The starburst wind around the circumnuclear ring is confirmed, and we find evidence of an AGN-driven outflow extending to a radial distance of 2 kpc from the nucleus, with a morphology consistent with a nearly face-on ionization cone. The previously reported circumnuclear outflow resembles part of the bright base. We derive mass and energy outflow rates for both the starburst wind and the AGN-driven outflow.

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST/MIRI spectroscopy of NGC 7469's Seyfert nucleus, finding a stratified AGN outflow, warm molecular gas, weak nuclear PAH emission, and starburst ring context.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 (IRAS 23007+0836; UGC 12332) contains a Seyfert 1.5 nucleus surrounded by a bright, circumnuclear ring of star formation with a radius of 1.8” (580 pc). The total infrared luminosity of NGC 7469 is 4.5 10^ 11 L _ . It is a nearly face-on spiral, about 26 kpc away in projection from an inclined companion galaxy (IC 4283) with a highly disturbed morphology. NGC 7469 has a supermassive black hole mass of 10^7 M _ , measured through reverberation mapping peterson04 .

The mid-infrared Spitzer spectrum of NGC 7469 shows a mix of strong Poly-cyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbon (PAH) emission features, high-ionization lines, and warm molecular gas, indicative of a complex, composite source powered by an active nucleus and a starburst Inami13, Stierwalt14 . High resolution observations of CO( 1-0 ), CO( 2-1 ) and [ CI ] in the inner 2 kpc region with ALMA show largely rotational kinematics and a large mass of molecular gas Izumi20,Nguyen21 . With a central AGN, a circumnuclear starburst ring, a high velocity outflow, and a dusty, gas rich ISM, NGC 7469 is an ideal laboratory for studying the co-evolution of galaxies and supermassive black holes at high resolution with JWST .
We interpret the results as indicative of a decelerating, stratified, AGN driven outflow emerging from the NGC 7469 nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST NIRSpec observations of NGC 7469, describing its Seyfert nucleus, star-forming ring, inner-kiloparsec gas excitation, rotation-dominated kinematics, and nuclear spiral interpreted as gas inflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 is an active galaxy hosting a Seyfert 1.5 nucleus landt08 located at 70.6 Mpc ( z=0.01627 ) with a (R')SAB(rs)a morphology RC3 . The central kiloparsec region of NGC 7469 shows a bright AGN surrounded by a star-forming ring e.g. davies04,diaz-santos07,song21 .

In this Letter, we report the JWST NIRSpec-IFS observations of NGC 7469. With the superb spatial and spectral resolutions of NIRSpec, it is now possible to study in detail the inner kiloparsec of this galaxy. This allows us to unveil the intricate connection between the feeding, feedback, and gas excitation in the inner kiloparsec of this luminous Sy 1.5 galaxy.

NGC 7469 kinematics is dominated by rotation (with amplitudes 150 km s ^ 1 at low-velocity dispersion ( 60-80 km s ^ 1 ), especially in the star-forming ring region. We identify a nuclear spiral in NGC 7469 in the velocity dispersion map. We interpret the nuclear spiral and the non-rotational motions (observed after modeling the rotation field) as a gas inflow to the nucleus of the galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 7469 is a barred spiral LIRG with a type 1 AGN, compact nuclear starburst, and ring; the paper finds AGN-dominated nuclear dust with moderately obscured hot dust.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 is a barred spiral galaxy hosting a type 1 AGN e.g. Osterbrock1993 with a compact nuclear starburst and starburst ring e.g. Diaz-Santos2007, Garcia-Bernete2022b, Lai2022, Lai2023 . The AGN drives a wide, highly ionised outflow towards the SE e.g. Garcia-Bernete2022b, Vivian2022, Bianchin2023 , coinciding with a gap in the star-forming ring. This object has been subject to much analysis of the PAH population with JWST Garcia-Bernete2022b, Zhang2023, Lai2023 .

NGC 7469 We find the nuclear dust continuum to be dominated by the AGN, with relatively weak PAH emission and no significant contribution from stellar continuum, consistent with previous analysis of this target e.g. Alonso-Herrero2020, Garcia-Bernete2022b, Armus2023 . The dust distribution shows a large spread of extinction with an average extinction of _ 9.8 1 with a significant hot dust component with T > 400 K. Considering its classification as a type 1 AGN, it is expected to have clear lines of sight to very hot dust close to the sublimation radius at T > 1000 K. Interestingly, this dust is moderately obscured which may suggest that there is some intervening dust that obscures the very hot inner regions of the AGN torus.

sources.summary: The paper re-analyzes NGC 7469 UV/X-ray monitoring with a Comptonized disc-corona model, finding reprocessed UV seed emission, correlated corona parameters, and near-Eddington accretion.

quotes.quote: We present a re-analysis of the simultaneous 30 day IUE/XTE observation of NGC 7469 done in 1996. Our main progress in this paper, in comparison to previous spectral analysis (Nandra et al. ), is to adopt and fit directly to the data a detailed model of the Comptonized spectrum. This firstly allows to fit simultaneously the data from the UV to the hard X-ray band in a self-consistent way and secondly it gives direct constraints on the physical parameters of the disc-corona system, like the temperature and optical depth of the corona.

Our results are completely consistent with a slab geometry where all the observed UV emission is supposed to cross the corona but more photon-starved geometries also give acceptable fits. Whatever the geometry is, the UV seed photon emission appears to be dominated by the reprocessing of the X-rays. We also found very interesting correlations between the different model parameters, the most important one being the anticorrelation between the corona temperature kT_e and the UV flux F_ UV .
During the observations, NGC 7469 appears to accrete near its Eddington limit. This source could then be an example of magnetically dominated disc-corona system as recently proposed by Merloni ( ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7469 as a Seyfert 1 spiral and candidate UHECR source, using gamma-ray upper limits to constrain possible proton luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 is a Seyfert 1 spiral galaxy placed at 68 Mpc from the Earth. It has been observed by for 3.4 h, no excess was seen and an upper limit on the integral flux of gamma-ray was also calculated. The method developed in this paper allows us to calculate an upper limit on the proton luminosities of Pictor A and NGC 7469, for energies above 10^ 18 eV, based on the upper limits on the integral flux of gamma-ray obtained by .

NGC 7469 NGC 7469 is a well known spiral galaxy with a Seyfert 1 nucleus embedded in a ring of starburst activity. At radio frequencies, its structure shows an unresolved central component surrounded by a ring of star forming regions . The upper limit on the integral gamma-ray flux of NGC 7469 obtained by at 99.9 CL is I_ ^ UL (E>E_ ^ th =330 \ GeV ) = 1.38 10^ 12 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . Also for this source, the upper limit obtained by offers a valid constraint if UHECR protons are emitted by the source.

sources.summary: For NGC 7469, freeing relativistic-line parameters improves the Suzaku fit, and the resulting equivalent width agrees with earlier Beppo-SAX and Suzaku analyses.

quotes.quote: However, in the case of NGC 7469 residuals around 6.4 keV are still present even if a relativistic Fe line is included (and detected at more than 95 confidence). This could be due to the fact that we have used fixed parameters in the fits. We tested this by letting the inner radius, the inclination and the emissivity index free to vary in the relline model component for this source. We found a much better fit in this case with a ^2/d.o.f. = 601.13/665, compared to that with frozen parameters ( ^2/d.o.f. = 637.81/667).

Beppo-SAX observations of NGC 7469 revealed the presence of both the relativistic Fe K line (EW = 121 _ 100 ^ +100 eV) and the associated Compton hump ( DeRosa+02 ). Our results on this source are consistent with this work (EW = 81 _ 24 ^ +27 eV). The same Suzaku spectrum was also analyzed by Patrick+12 , who fitted the data with a relativistic line with an equivalent width consistent within the errors with our result (EW= 91 _ 8 ^ +9 eV).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7469 has baseline problems and H I self-absorption, with possible blending from IC 5283 complicating previous line-width measurements.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469: Our data for NGC 7469 contained large baseline fluctuations across the whole continuum in a significant amount of the scans which were not included in the final, accumulated spectrum. The absorption profile present in the HI spectrum of NGC 7469 (see Fig. \ batmen ) persists throughout the literature, causing significant uncertainty in the line flux measurements. utilized the Effelsberg 100 m telescope with spectral resolution of 13.2 km s ^ 1 and channel spacing of 11 km s ^ 1 , near insufficient to identify the emission profile separate from the noise level. Observations with the Arecibo telescope (e.g., mw1984, bf1979, ho2008_data ) all show self-absorption which is commented on in their analyses.

NED lists the radial velocity of IC 5283 as 4804 km s ^ 1 , very near the velocity of the blueshifted flank of NGC 7469, with an angular separation of only 1 3 (well within the GBT L-Band beam). Thus it is likely that most previous studies have the emission from this companion galaxy blended with that of NGC 7469. When comparing our spectrum to those of , , and , we note that their higher S/N spectra show an emission bump on the blueshifted wing of the profile, while our spectrum does not.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7469 has superlinear HCN and CN intensity-ratio trends localized around its AGN, plus a type-1 AGN, starburst ring, XDR, and circumnuclear disk.

quotes.quote: The HCN (4-3)/HCN (3-2) intensity ratio in NGC 7469 has a slope of 1.34 0.05 . We attribute the variation within NGC 3256 to excitation associated with the northern and southern nuclei. In NGC 7469, the variations are localized to the region surrounding the active galactic nucleus. At our resolution ( 700 pc), IRAS 13120-5453 shows little variation in the HCN intensity ratios. Individual galaxies show nearly constant CN (2-1)/CN (1-0) intensity ratios. We find an offset to lower CN (2-1)/CN (1-0) intensity ratios ( 0.5 ) in NGC 3256 compared to the other two galaxies ( 0.8 ). For the CN (3-2)/CN (2-1) intensity ratio, NGC 7469 has a superlinear trend of 1.55 0.04 , with the peak localized toward the active galactic nucleus.

NGC 7469 hosts a nuclear luminous type-1 AGN ( L_ 2-10keV = 1.5 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ; Liu2014 ) and has a starburst ring surrounding the central portion of the galaxy Izumi2015, Izumi2020 . The AGN is creating an XDR 50 pc in size in the nuclear region, and this XDR is surrounded by a circumnuclear disk of star-forming gas Izumi2020 . Izumi2015 find the integrated intensities of HCN (4-3), HCO ^ + (4-3), and CS (7-6) are higher in the nucleus than in the starburst ring and predict that the HCN abundance is enhanced in the nucleus due to increased sub-mm radiation near the AGN Izumi2013, Izumi2016 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 7469 as an emission-dominated AGN galaxy with H2O line deficits, ambiguous 75 micron non-detection, and possible low-luminosity AGN/starburst-ring geometry effects.

quotes.quote: As delineated by the equivalent width of the OH 65 m doublet fis14,gon15 , 6 galaxies (NGC 1068, NGC 7469, NGC 253, NGC 6240, IRAS 13120-5453, and NGC 4945) have overall weak far-IR molecular absorption features but usually strong emission in the atomic/ionic fine-structure lines ( EQW(OH 65 m) <20 km s ^ 1 ), and will be referred to as ``emission-dominated galaxies'' (EDGs), while the rest (with EQW(OH 65 m) >20 km s ^ 1 ) have stronger far-IR molecular absorption features and will be referred to as ``absorption-dominated galaxies'' (ADGs). The extragalactic submm SLEDs, with the H _2 O line fluxes normalized to that of the 3_ 21 3_ 12 1163 GHz line in Fig. sleds b, show a rather common U shaped pattern for the low-excitation ( E_ up 300 K) lines: the 1163 GHz line is usually the strongest line followed by the 988 GHz line, except in NGC 1068, NGC 6240, and probably NGC 7469 where this sequence is interchanged.

NGC 1068 and NGC 7469 are ambiguous sources, because the H _2 O 75 m line is neither detected in absorption, as expected in the scenario of radiative pumping, nor in emission, as expected in case of shock excitation. As shown in Fig. sleds d, both sources are underluminous in both H _2 O 1163 GHz and 988 GHz lines relative to L_ IR , with a stronger deficit in the 1163 GHz line. These galaxies are thus not representative of the bulk of (U)LIRGs observed with SPIRE and fitted by , but may well represent a population of low-luminosity AGNs ( 10^ 11 L_ ) with starburst rings under-sampled by Herschel /PACS and SPIRE. The PACS continuum-subtracted spectra around the H _2 O 3_ 21 2_ 12 75 m line in NGC 1068 and NGC 7469 are shown in Fig. ngc1068 . The net flux of the line in both sources is below 3 ( 42 64 and 73 30 Jy km s ^ 1 ), but hints of a P Cygni profile are seen in the spectrum of NGC 1068 with the absorption and emission features nearly cancelling each other.

sources.summary: The paper uses JWST/NIRSpec observations of NGC 7469 to map 3.3 micron PAH emission, finding altered grain properties, PAH suppression near the AGN, and enhanced fractional PAH power in the starburst ring.

quotes.quote: We present James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) Near Infrared Spectrograph (NIRSpec) integral-field spectroscopy of the nearby luminous infrared galaxy, NGC 7469. We take advantage of the high spatial/spectral resolution and wavelength coverage of JWST /NIRSpec to study the 3.3 \ neutral polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) grain emission on 60 pc scales. We find a clear change in the average grain properties between the star-forming ring and the central AGN. Regions in the vicinity of the AGN, with / > 0.25, tend to have larger grain sizes and lower aliphatic-to-aromatic (3.4/3.3) ratios indicating that smaller grains are preferentially removed by photo-destruction in the vicinity of the AGN.

We find an overall suppression of the total PAH emission relative to the ionized gas in the central 1 kpc region of the AGN in NGC 7469 compared to what has been observed with Spitzer on 3 kpc scales. However, the fractional 3.3 \ to total PAH power is enhanced in the starburst ring, possibly due to a variety of physical effects on sub-kpc scales, including recurrent fluorescence of small grains or multiple photon absorption by large grains. Finally, the IFU data show that while the 3.3 \ PAH-derived star formation rate (SFR) in the ring is 8 higher than that inferred from the [ NeII ] and [ NeIII ] emission lines, the integrated SFR derived from the 3.3 \ feature would be underestimated by a factor of two due to the deficit of PAHs around the AGN, as might occur if a composite system like NGC 7469 were to be observed at high-redshift.

sources.summary: The paper compares IC 4329A with NGC 7469, reanalyzing its simultaneous millimeter/X-ray monitoring and finding no robust periodicity or consistently determined lag.

quotes.quote: The first of these was dedicated to the RQ Seyfert 1 NGC 7469 located at the same distance as IC 4329A ( z=0.016 ) and having lower mass M_ SMBH 9.1 10^6 M_ Peterson7469 . This object was known for a significant variability at 95 GHz, by a factor of two within 4--5 days as found in CARMA (Combined Array for Research in Millimeter-wave Astronomy) observations by Baldi15 . Later, behar20 provided the simultaneous mm/X-ray monitoring of NGC 7469. The observations were taken in the 0.3--10 keV range with Swift /XRT and in the mm band at 95 and 143 GHz frequencies with the Institut de Radioastronomie Millimetrique (IRAM) 30-m single-dish radio telescope.

Using the wavelet analysis, we do not find any evidence of a periodicity in the X-ray light curve of NGC 7469. Concerning the time delay between X-ray and mm emission in the simultaneous NGC 7469 observations, behar20 cautiously stated that the mm variability appears to precede the X-ray flux by 14 days. We have repeated the correlation analysis of NGC 7469 data using the same tools as described in Sect.  cor .

sources.summary: The paper reports extra-nuclear MIR emission, star-forming blobs, weak diffuse emission, and possible star-formation contamination near the AGN in NGC 7469.

quotes.quote: In addition to the central point source, in three sources -- NGC 1097 , NGC 5135 , NGC 7469 - we observed clear extra-nuclear MIR emission. In all of these objects we find distinct blobs of star formation (SF), in addition NGC 5135 and NGC 7469 also exhibit a weak diffuse component. Typical distances between SF blobs and the AGN are 8 2 ( 700 pc) in NGC 1097, 1 5 ( 400 pc) in NGC 5135 and 1 3 ( 400 pc) in NGC 7469.

In the latter two objects the proximity of the SF components to the AGN does not allow to rule out a significant contribution of SF to the measured flux of the central point source. By comparing the fluxes of individual SF blobs and of the AGN, we can estimate upper limits for such contamination. This yields 15 for NGC 5135 and 10 for NGC 7469.

sources.summary: NGC 7469 is treated as a peculiar spiral Seyfert LIRG; the paper uses nuclear SINFONI data and adds star-forming-ring emission to recover missing near-infrared H2 flux.

quotes.quote: The other three galaxies (NGC 5135, NGC 7130, and NGC 7469) are spirals, although NGC 7130 and NGC 7469 have peculiar morphologies suggesting recent minor interactions Genzel1995, Bellocchi2012 .

The observations where carried out during different periods, i.e., 60A (NGC 7469), 77B (NGC 5135 and NGC 7130), 80B (IRASF 05189--2524), and 82B (NGC 34), with a spectral resolution of R 4000, using different plate scales that yield different FoVs. For further details of the NGC 7469 data, see , and for NGC 5135 and NGC 7130, please refer to .

For NGC 7469, the FoV of SINFONI data is 0.8 0.8 \ (0.2 0.2 kpc), so only the nuclear emission is included. NGC 7469 has a kpc scale star-forming ring, thus to account for the missing near-IR H _2 emission we added the emission measured in the star-forming ring by DiazSantos2007 to the nuclear fluxes (Table tab:h2fluxes ). This correction increases the fluxes by a factor of 2.

sources.summary: The paper reports a multiwavelength campaign on NGC 7469, using a deep RGS spectrum to resolve AGN outflow components, ionized absorption, and circumnuclear star-formation emission.

quotes.quote: We conducted a multiwavelength six-month campaign to observe the nearby Seyfert galaxy \ with several observatories in order to better understand and quantify the outflow in this AGN. We report on the time-integrated line-resolved X-ray spectrum of \ obtained with the Reflection Grating Spectrometer ( ) on board . We use the \ spectrum to discern the many AGN outflow components. A global fit is applied to obtain their physical parameters. We find that the AGN wind can be well described by three narrow velocity components at -650, --950, and --2050 .

We also find a collisionally-ionised component at kT = 0.35 keV that we associate with the circum-nuclear star formation activity of , as it follows the L_ FIR / L_ X 10^4 relation found in star forming galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7469's PAH image flux lower than expected, reports no MIR evidence for previously optical circumnuclear star formation, and notes its nuclear SFR estimate is below literature values.

quotes.quote: Apart from the four galaxies with larger fluxes on the PAH images, we find two sources, namely NGC 5128 and NGC 7469, which have fluxes in the PAH images lower than the expected from the inferred continuum slope. This effect can be due to an overestimate of this slope, or to some of the emission from the molecular band ``leaking'' into the reference filter.

In the opposite direction, NGC 6240 and NGC 7469 have previous detections of circumnuclear star formation in the optical, but we find no evidence for it in our analysis. This could be either due to the detection limit imposed here or, in the case of NGC 7469, because the star formation structure seen by previous authors is outside the pupil used to isolate the nuclear source see Soifer2003 .

On the other hand NGC 253 and NGC 7469 have reported SFRs for the nuclear region with spatial resolutions comparable to the ones in this paper, with values of 2.8 0.3 M _ yr ^ 1 and 2.6 - 5.1 M _ yr ^ 1 respectively Ott2005, Davies2007 . Our estimates for these targets are 1.1 dex and 0.6 dex smaller, therefore justifying the conservative uncertainty of 1 dex mentioned above.

sources.summary: NGC 7469N is the northern component imaged in a Seyfert merger system; its compact nucleus, diffuse continuum, and offset line-flux peak suggest an off-nucleus star cluster.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469N NGC 7469 is a Seyfert 1 system with a disturbed companion 26 kpc away. It features a 10 ^7 M _ black hole measured virially from reverberation mapping surrounded by a star-forming ring . The southern nucleus has been observed to features coronal line biconical outflows .

Here we have imaged the northern component of this merger system with OSIRIS. At 0 035/spaxel resolution, the nucleus appears compact with extended diffuse emission in the K band continuum.
