Interestingly, the peak of the flux is offset ( 0 2 , or 65pc) from the continuum peak; its rotational kinematics likely signifies an off-nucleus star cluster rather than outflowing ionized gas.

sources.summary: The paper derives a 15.9-day lag for NGC 7469, argues it exceeds earlier H values, and estimates a black hole mass of about four million solar masses.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 The DCF peaks at 13 days and the JAVELIN result shows 15.9^ +0.3 _ 1.7 days time lag. Conflicting time lag measurements are presented in previous studies, with 4.9^ +1.7 _ 1.3 days time lag of H emission line 2004ApJ...613..682P , and 10.9^ +3.5 _ 1.3 days using H emission line in a more recent study by 2014ApJ...795..149P . We note that the H time lag from our analysis is significantly larger than the values in previous works 2014ApJ...795..149P, 2017MNRAS.466.4759S .

However, considering that the general trend of the H and H time lag ratio of (H ): (H )=1.54:1.00 2010ApJ...716..993B , our result is in agreement with the 2014ApJ...795..149P result. Taking our value and the mass estimators of 2014ApJ...795..149P and 2015ApJ...806..109J , the M_ BH estimate from H is about 4 10^ 6 M_ . This value agrees well with the most recent H based M_ BH 2014ApJ...795..149P .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7469 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy, measuring X-ray/UV reverberation lags, filtering long-timescale variations, and concluding it likely has a high accretion rate.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2019 Using a month-long X-ray lightcurve from /PCA and 1.5 month long UV continuum lightcurves from spectra in 1220 1970 , we performed a detailed time-lag study of the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 7469. Our cross-correlation analysis confirms previous results showing that the X-rays are delayed relative to the UV continuum at 1315 by 3.49 0.22 days which is possibly caused by either propagating fluctuation or variable comptonisation. However, if variations slower than 5 days are removed from the X-ray lightcurve, the UV variations then lag behind the X-rays variations by 0.37 0.14 days, consistent with reprocessing of the X-rays by a surrounding accretion disc. A very similar reverberation delay is observed between /XRT X-ray and /UVOT UVW2, U lightcurves. Continuum lightcurves extracted from the /GRISM spectra show delays with respect to X-rays consistent with reverberation.
We conclude that NGC 7469 does have a high accretion rate, nearer to 50 percent than 5 percent.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7469 as an active-core barred spiral with a circumnuclear starburst ring, central PAH-band emission, and comparatively short naphthalene half-life.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 is an active core spiral galaxy with a large-scale stellar bar (several kpc) identified in the near-IR Knapen2000 . Its circumnuclear region is surrounded by a 1".5-2".5 starburst ring Soifer2003 . In the mid-IR, the most central region of NGC 7469 ( 1" of the nucleus) is dominated by emissions in the K band (2.2 m) and the 3.3 m band Izumi2015 , the latter indicating a strong presence of PAHs in a region very close to the nucleus. NGC 7469 is at 67.9 Mpc and has a luminosity of log L _X = 43.19 0.07 erg s^ 1 Jensen2017 .

For NGC 7469, the ratio between the PAH injection time and the average half-life of naphthalene is as large as 10 ^ 6 , even though the X-ray luminosity of this source is higher than that of NGC 7582 by a factor of 4.6. This is due to the fact that the column density N_H in NGC 7469 is particularly thin ( _x = 0.03 , see Table tab:agn ), around 450 times smaller than that of NGC 7582 ( _x = 13.64 ).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7469 with JWST/MIRI, describing it as a merging LIRG with a Seyfert nucleus, outflow, circumnuclear starburst ring, and possible companion-triggered activity.

quotes.quote: We present James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) Mid-InfraRed Instrument (MIRI) integral-field spectroscopy of the nearby merging, luminous infrared galaxy, NGC 7469. This galaxy hosts a Seyfert type-1.5 nucleus, a highly ionized outflow, and a bright, circumnuclear star-forming ring, making it an ideal target to study AGN feedback in the local Universe.

NGC 7469 (Arp 298, Mrk 1514, IRAS F23007+0836) is a LIRG ( L_ 8-1000 = 10^ 11.6 L_ ) located at a distance of D _ L =70.6 Mpc. It is classified as a Seyfert 1.5 Landt2008 galaxy with a supermassive black hole mass of 1.1 10 ^ 7 M _ Peterson2014, Lu2021 and X-ray luminosity of L_ 2-10 keV = 10^ 43.19 erg s ^ 1 Asmus2015 . NGC 7469 hosts both a rapidly accreting black hole and a circumnuclear starburst ring with a radius of 500 pc e.g. Song2021 , consisting of two distinct stellar populations of young ( 5--6 Myr) and intermediate ages ( 14--35 Myr) Diaz-Santos2007, Bohn2022 . A highly perturbed galaxy, IC 4283, lies 26 kpc (79 ) away in projection; interaction with this companion may have triggered the starburst and AGN activity in NGC 7469.

sources.summary: The paper reports optical reverberation monitoring of the highly variable AGN NGC 7469, finding changing UV-optical lag spectra over the campaign and evidence for BLR and dynamical-corona contributions.

quotes.quote: We present the results regarding the analysis of an intensive monitoring of the Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN) NGC 7469. We observed the source for 4 months with almost daily cadence in the ugriz bands, using the IO:O on the Liverpool Telescope. We measured the lags with respect to the u band and found a clear change of the lag spectrum between the first and the second half of the campaign. Given that the source varies on different timescales during these two segments, it is likely that different components are dominating the variability at different times.

We presented the results regarding the analysis of the interband lags of the highly variable AGN NGC 7469. Our measurements, in line with previous observations of other AGN, indicate that the UV to optical lags are strongly dependent on the the timescale of the variability, suggesting a significant contribution from the BLR.

sources.summary: JWST and ALMA observations show NGC 7469's AGN heats warm molecular gas and drives ionized outflow, while star formation remains concentrated in a clumpy circumnuclear ring.

quotes.quote: We combine mid-infrared diagnostics obtained from integral-field unit observations taken with MIRI/MRS on JWST with cold molecular gas information derived from ALMA observations of CO(1--0) emission to investigate the star formation rate and efficiency within the central 1.5 \ kpc 1.3 \ kpc region of the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 7469 on 100 pc scales. The active nucleus leaves a notable imprint on its immediate surroundings by elevating the temperature of the warm molecular gas, driving an ionized gas outflow on sub-kpc scales, and selectively destroying small dust grains. These effects, nevertheless, have relatively little impact on the cold circumnuclear medium or its ability to form stars. Most of the star formation in NGC 7469 is confined to a clumpy starburst ring, but the star formation efficiency remains quite elevated even for the nuclear region that is most affected by the active nucleus.

At a luminosity distance We adopt a redshift of 0.01627 Springob et al. 2005 and a concordance cosmology with H_0=70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _m=0.3 , and _ =0.7 Hinshaw et al. 2013 . of 70.6 Mpc, the SBa spiral galaxy NGC 7469, one of the six sources originally observed by , hosts a type 1 (broad-line) AGN with a black hole mass of 1 10^7 M_ estimated from reverberation mapping of the H line Peterson et al. 2014, Lu et al. 2021 and an X-ray (2--10 keV) luminosity of 5 10^ 43 erg \ s^ 1 Ricci et al. 2017 . Included in the Great Observatories All-Sky LIRG Survey (GOALS), NGC 7469 has an IR luminosity of 10^ 11.65 \ L_ Armus et al. 2009 powered by both its AGN ( Inami et al. 2013, Stierwalt et al. 2014 ) and substantial additional star formation activity ( 40-80 M_ yr^ 1 ; Perez-Torres et al. 2009, Pereira-Santaella et al. 2011, Mehdipour et al. 2018 ), which is concentrated in a nuclear region with radius 60 pc Davies et al. 2007 and a circumnuclear starburst ring of radius 500 pc ( Wilson et al. 1991, Miles et al. 1994, Diaz-Santos et al. 2007, Song et al. 2021 ).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7469, a face-on early spiral with a Seyfert nucleus and two star-forming rings, deriving gas and cluster properties from MUSE data.

quotes.quote: In this second paper of a series, we analyse the circumnuclear environment of the almost face-on galaxy NGC 7469, an early spiral (SABa) hosting a Seyfert 1 nucleus. The galaxy shows two prominent star- forming rings, one of them very close to their active galactic nucleus, within 1.5 arcsec from the galaxy centre, and a second incomplete ring with elliptical appearance and with dimensions of 21 and 13.2 arcsec for its major and minor axes respectively. We have used publicly available observations obtained by the IFS MUSE spectrograph as part of the first Science Verification run.

For each observed ring HII region we have derived: (1) the number of Hydrogen ionising photons per second, Q(H _0 ); (2) the electron density of the emitting gas per cubic centimeter, n _e ; (3) the ionisation parameter, u; (4) the corresponding angular radius, ; (5) the filling factor, ; and (6) the mass of ionised hydrogen in solar masses, M(HII). The inner ring regions seem to be more compact, showing lower sizes and higher filling factors and electron densities than the outer ring ones. The characteristics of the outer ring regions in NGC 7469 are more luminous than those in NGC 7742, the first galaxy studied in this series.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 7469 has multi-wavelength optical/UV light curves, time lags consistent with disk reprocessing, and wavelength-dependent lags.

quotes.quote: There are four objects, Fairall 9, NGC 4151, NGC 5548, and NGC 7469, that have multi-wavelength light curves well observed at the optical/UV bands. The multi-wavelength light curves are used to estimate time lags for the four objects.

It can be seen from columns (4) and (5) of Table 3 that the measured time lags are marginally consistent with those predicted by the standard accretion disks for NGC 4151 and NGC 7469. This implies that the optical and UV emissions are likely to be the reprocessed X-rays for NGC 4151 and NGC 7469. In addition, the time lags decrease slightly as spin parameter a_ increases.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7469 may have star-formation contamination, torus fits requiring many clouds, and the largest discrepancy with interferometric torus-size estimates.

quotes.quote: It is then likely that the mid-IR nuclear fluxes and spectroscopy of NGC 7469 contain a contribution from star formation. A similar situation may be the case for NGC 3227.

In some cases (e.g., NGC 3227, NGC 7469) it appears as if to compensate for limited values of _V , the fits are achieved with more clouds along radial equatorial rays, close to the maximum value allowed by the models of N_0=15 .

The galaxy with the largest discrepancy is NGC 7469. Tristram et al. (2009) inferred s_ 12 m =10.5 pc, whereas our fitted torus size is about 5 pc. We note however, that the interferometric observations of NGC 7469 were affected by large uncertainties in the determination of the total flux that may have compromised the derived physical size of the torus of this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7469 as a major scatter contributor whose revised SPEAR lag lowers the AGN Hubble-diagram scatter.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 alone contributes 0.09 dex (0.22 mag) to the total scatter. The lags of most of our objects were recalculated by 2011ApJ...735...80Z using the stochastic process estimation for AGN reverberations (SPEAR) method. The only object with a significantly discrepant lag they found was NGC 7469. A more recent observation of this source (C. Grier, private communication) has also given a value of / F consistent with the SPEAR value 2011ApJ...735...80Z . Using the SPEAR value for its lag (see Fig. fig:Hubble_diagram ) the scatter in the diagram is reduced to 0.18 dex (0.45 mag), and the reduced ^2 drops from 4.0 to 2.9.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7469 as the calibration case for comparing global AKARI and narrow-slit nuclear 3.3 PAH measurements, deriving a 0.04 nuclear-to-global scaling factor.

quotes.quote: For example, a recent AKARI study by imanishi10 reported the 3.3 PAH luminosity of NGC 7469 as 6.3 10^ 41 while the ground-based spectroscopy with a 1.6'' slit width by imanishi04 presented an upper limit of 2.7 10^ 40 , indicating that the nuclear PAH emission is a small fraction of the global PAH emission.

Although the nuclear-to-global starburst ratio can vary for individual objects, we used the case of NGC 7469 to estimate the difference. As discussed in 3.1, NGC 7469 was observed with a ground-based spectrograph with a narrow-slit and the AKARI IRC, respectively. The flux ratio between nuclear and the upper limit of the global is 0.04. Thus we took this ratio as a calibration factor and scale down the AKARI measurements by a factor of 0.04.

sources.summary: The paper uses FAST spectroscopy of NGC 7469, finding core-dominated [O III] emission and noting prior detection of two [O III] line components.

quotes.quote: Long-slit spectroscopy was available for CGCG 468-002-NED01 and NGC 7469 taken with FAST on the Mt. Hopkins Tillinghast 60-inch telescope as part of different observing programs. The slit width was 3 \ and the spectral resolution was _ inst 75 km s ^ 1 . None of the spectra are flux calibrated, but they can be used to measure the velocity dispersion of the emission lines as well as their ratios.

The total [ O iii ] emission is dominated by the broad component in MCG-03-34-064 and in NGC 7130, whereas in NGC 5135 and NGC 7469 the core component dominates the emission. In NGC 5135 based on both the relatively broad [ Si vi ] 1.96 m coronal line and its spatial extent suggested the existence of different kinematical components and the possible presence of AGN induced outflows. found blueshifted components in the Ly and [ O iii ] 5007 lines of both NGC 7130 and NGC 5135. The velocity dispersion of the broad component of the [ O iii ] 5007 line of NGC 7130 is similar to the H broad component 400 km s ^ 1 , Bellocchi2012 . In NGC 7469 also detected two components in the [ O iii ] 5007 line.

sources.summary: The survey presents NGC 7469 as a Seyfert 1 with a starburst ring, weaker SiO constraints, strong CN/HCN abundances, and AGN abundance ratios differing from NGC 1068.

quotes.quote: Among the galaxies in our sample, M 51, NGC 1068, and NGC 7469 host an AGN (the first two are classified as Sy 2, and the latter as Sy 1, Osterbrock93 ). Both NGC 7469 and NGC 1068 have a starburst ring at 1 kpc from the central AGN, and have very similar observed properties (see e.g. Davies04 and references therein). This might be particularly significative in the case of NGC 7469 due to its farther distance, although estimations of the emission coming from starburst regions at our frequencies are only available for NGC 1068 (see details in Sect. ngc1068 ).

In the case of NGC 7469, where lines are weaker, we cannot conclude from the upper limit to the SiO abundance whether the gas chemistry is different. CN and HCN abundances stand out in NGC 7469, NGC 1068, Mrk 231, and M 82, being the difference between these galaxies and the rest of the sample larger in HCN.

sources.summary: NGC 7469 is treated as an AGN-dominated Seyfert 1 with circumnuclear star formation; ALMA detects nuclear continuum and molecular emission but not vibrationally excited HCN.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 (z=0.0164) is included in both the Center for Astrophysics (CfA) huc92 and 12 m rus93 Seyfert galaxy samples, and is classified optically as a Seyfert 1 vei95,yua10 . It displays circum-nuclear starburst activity at 1--2 arcsec ( 0.3--0.7 kpc) in radius around the central AGN soi03,gal05,dia07,reu10 . Although the 3.3 m PAH emission feature, a signature of starbursts, is detected in large aperture ( > 4'') infrared spectroscopy ima10 , ground-based infrared < 1''-wide slit spectroscopy targeting the nuclear region shows no detectable 3.3 m PAH emission feature iw04 , suggesting that nuclear starburst activity is very weak and undetectable.

In NGC 7469, continuum emission is also detected in the three starburst regions, which we denote as SB1, SB2, and SB3. We created their moment 0 maps by summing ten velocity channels at the expected frequencies of the HCN and HCO ^ + v _ 2 =1f J=3--2 lines, but there is no signature of these emission lines at all.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7469 as a face-on Sc Type-I AGN host with broad-line emission, high equivalent width, blue disk, and above-average star formation behavior.

quotes.quote: One of these galaxies is NGC 7469 (lower-right triad of panels at fig:examples ), a face-on Sc galaxy host of a Type-I AGN with the FWHM of the rms spectrum reaching thousands of km/s e.g Seyfert.1943, Peterson.and.Wandel.2000 and with 100 . Rembold.etal.2017 have shown that the hosts of the strongest AGNs (those with higher luminosity) present an increasing contribution of young stellar populations at their centre, which could account for the loci of this central spectrum in all BPTVO diagnostic diagrams and the high value of at the current spatial resolution of our data.

Moreover, only two AGN hosts are above the SFMS, NGC 1667 (Type-II AGN host) and NGC 7469 (Type-I AGN host). The reason could be that both of them are face-on galaxies with a very blue disk. Furthermore, they are considered strong AGNs in our sample ( > 10 ) and also both exhibit high (above 20 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7469 as the LIRG in a stage-A merger and a bright unobscured AGN with XCLUMPY results adopted from prior work.

quotes.quote: IRAS F23007+0836 S/N (NGC 7469 and IC 5283): The system is a stage-A merger with the two galaxies NGC 7469 and IC 5283 separated by 79 7 (26.2 kpc). NGC 7469 is a LIRG, whereas IC 5283 has a smaller IR luminosity of log L_ IR / L_ = 10.79 Ricci2017bMNRAS . NGC 7469 hosts a bright unobscured AGN detected by Chandra/High Energy Transmission Grating Spectrometer Scott2005 , XMM-Newton Blustin2003 , Suzaku Walton2013,Iso2016 , and NuSTAR e.g., Ricci2017bMNRAS,Middei2018,Ogawa2019 . Recently, Ogawa2021 perform an spectral analysis with XCLUMPY, using the NuSTAR and XMM-Newton spectra. Their results are in good agreement with the previous works.

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST mid-infrared observations of NGC 7469, resolving its Seyfert nucleus, star-forming ring, ionized outflow, and shock-heated inner ISM.

quotes.quote: The nearby, luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) NGC 7469 hosts a Seyfert nucleus with a circumnuclear star-forming ring and is thus the ideal local laboratory for investigating the starburst--AGN connection in detail. We present integral-field observations of the central 1.3 kpc region in NGC 7469 obtained with the JWST Mid-InfraRed Instrument. Molecular and ionized gas distributions and kinematics at a resolution of 100 pc over the 4.9-7.6 m region are examined to study gas dynamics influenced by the central AGN. The high-ionization line is resolved and shows a broad, blueshifted component associated with the outflow. Regions of enhanced velocity dispersion in and 180 pc from the AGN that also show high L( H _2)/L( PAH ) and L ( )/ L ( ) ratios to the W and N of the nucleus pinpoint regions where the ionized outflow is depositing energy, via shocks, into the dense interstellar medium between the nucleus and the starburst ring.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7469 as a local active galaxy in its bare-AGN sample and analyzes XMM-Newton/Swift X-ray spectra, including continuum, soft-excess, and spectral-fitting behavior.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 is a well-studied nearby (z=0.0163) Seyfert 1 AGN which is most popular for its variability and excess brightness in X-ray domain Markowitz2004 . This source shows wavelength-dependent continuum delays of several light days, which indicates the presence of an accretion disk around the central black hole Collier1998 and the reverberation mapping results in a black hole mass estimation of 1.0 10^7 M _ Peterson2004 . As NGC 7469 is a popular source in the X-ray domain, it is observed in almost all X-ray missions. For this work, we considered only the XMM-Newton and Swift /XRT observations. The details of the observations are given in Table table:obs_log and the data extraction procedure is described in Section sec:data reduction .

sources.summary: The paper finds three blobs around NGC 7469's central component, likely within an annular starburst ring and coincident with 350 GHz components.

quotes.quote: 95: NGC 7469 Around the strongest central component ( a ), there are three blobs ( b , , and d ). These are likely to be encompassed by an annular star-burst ring with radii of 1 5--2 5 found at different wavelengths , and are spatially coincident with significant 349.7 GHz components presented at an 0 50 0 40 resolution by . At that 350-GHz band, the three components have flux densities per beam of 1.0--1.7 mJy beam ^ 1 Izu15 . With Band-6 ALMA data, different from ours, also measured their flux densities at 260--266 GHz with a similar beam size of 0 57 0 52. By correcting for the slight difference in beam size, the spectral slopes between the two bands are estimated to be 2.6 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7469 as a well-observed Seyfert whose X-ray/UV variability supports thermal Comptonization, while additional rapid hard-X-ray variability points to coronal heating beyond changes in seed photons.

quotes.quote: In one particularly well-observed case, NGC 7469, we see a relationship between the X-ray and UV variability, which indicates that the dominant emission process for the X-rays is thermal Comptonization. Variations in the X-ray emission are related to changes in the UV seed photons, but it appears there must also be a mechanism - perhaps that which heats the Comptonizing corona - that induces rapid variability intrinsic to the X-rays.

In one source, NGC 7469, however, there is clear evidence that there is an additional variability mechanism on top of changes in the Compton seed. This must be related to the process that heats the Comptonizing corona, and apparently affects the hard X-rays more than the soft. Another intriguing phenomenon in this source is the observation of a dramatic increase in the X-ray variability amplitude at the time when the spectrum is hardest - in contrast to the behavior observed when comparing objects.

sources.summary: The paper models gas and stellar structure in NGC 7469, finding nuclear and circumnuclear rings, X-ray-irradiated gas emission, and an asymmetric compact stellar cluster.

quotes.quote: This model consists of a broad disk component, a circumnuclear ring, and a previously unknown nuclear ring at a radius of 0.2 ; it matches both the radio and near infrared data at their different resolutions. We conclude that the 1-0 S(1) does not trace the mass distribution, but that the emission probably originates in gas irradiated by X-rays from the AGN. The lower panel in Fig. fig:ngc7469 shows that we have resolved the nuclear stellar cluster in NGC 7469 using two different methods.

We find that the stellar cluster is asymmetrical, with FWHM of 0.12 \ (30 pc) and 0.22 \ (60 pc) at the 2 PAs. Using a combination of starburst models, the CO equivalent width, the mass model, and the K-band flux density, we can show that in this region the mass is dominated by stars rather than gas.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7469 as a Seyfert 1 with a strong circumnuclear starburst whose infrared spectrum is PAH-dominated but still shows weak AGN ionization.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 was one of the original Seyfert galaxies sey43 , subsequently classified as Seyfert 1, and later found to have a strong circumnuclear starburst wil86 . The IRS spectrum in Figure 6 shows a continuum similar to that of the pure starburst NGC 7714, including strong PAH features. However, there is a weak [ Ne V ] line indicative of some contribution to ionization from the AGN. There is no evidence of silicate absorption or emission. This is a clear example of how a starburst signature can dominate the infrared spectrum even if an AGN dominates the optical classification.

sources.summary: The paper uses deep XMM-Newton spectra of NGC 7469 to characterize its multiphase ionized warm absorber, finding broad ionization, two velocity regimes, and X-ray-dominated outflow energetics.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we use a 160 ks XMM-Newton RGS spectrum to get the most accurate view yet of the ionised outflow (warm absorber) in NGC 7469 as seen in X-rays, finding that there is a wide range of ionisation, with log in the range 0.5 3.5 erg cm s ^ 1 , and two main velocity regimes, at 580 720 and 2300 km s ^ 1 , with the highest velocity gas being the least ionised. The total absorbing column density in the X-rays is of order 3 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 . We find that the lowest ionisation phase of the absorber is probably identical with one of the phases of the UV absorber discovered in previous studies. We show that both X-ray and UV absorbers are consistent with an origin near the base of a torus wind, where matter is being launched and accelerated. Calculating the mass outflow rate and kinetic luminosity of all the absorber phases, we demonstrate that the X-ray absorbing gas carries respectively 90 and 95 of the mass and kinetic energy output of the ionised outflow.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7469 as a barred spiral LIRG with a Seyfert 1 nucleus and circumnuclear starburst, where VLA monitoring followed SN 2000ft and found no other luminous radio supernova.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 is a well known barred spiral galaxy located at a distance of 70 Mpc (Sanders et al. 2003; H_0 = 70 ; at the distance of NGC 7469, 1 arcsec corresponds to a linear size of 333 pc) containing a luminous, QSO-like, Seyfert 1 nucleus surrounded by a dusty circumnuclear starburst of 5'' (1.6 kpc) in diameter (Wilson et al. 1991, Malkan et al. 1998, Genzel et al. 1995, Scoville et al. 2000, D az-Santos et al. 2007). NGC 7469 is also a LIRG with L _ IR (8-1000 m) = 4.5 10^ 11 (obtained from Sanders et al. 2003 for our adopted distance to NGC 7469). During the course of a Very Large Array (VLA) monitoring program aimed at the detection of RSNe in NGC 7469, Colina et al. (2001a) detected a strong compact radio source in the circumnuclear starburst of this galaxy, which was later identified with a RSN (Colina et al. 2001b).

We also searched for recently exploded core-collapse supernovae in our VLA images. Apart from SN 2000ft ( S_ 1760 Jy at its peak, corresponding to 1.1 28 ), we found no evidence for any other radio supernova (RSN) more luminous than 6.0 26 , which suggests that no other Type IIn SN has exploded since 2000 in the circumnuclear starburst of NGC 7469.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7469 with reflection, a neutral iron line, distant cold reflector, and weak ionized absorber while noting poorly constrained reflection parameters.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469: In addition to the PLC+RDC continuum, there is a strong narrow emission line consistent with neutral iron, so we again include a distant cold reflector. We also include a weak, moderately ionised absorber at the redshift of the galaxy, based on the most prominent absorption component detected by when modelling the high resolution \ RGS spectrum. As the absorber has an extremely weak effect on the spectrum in this case, we only allow the column to vary, fixing the ionisation parameter at = 2.73 , and find \ = 1.7^ +1.2 _ 0.8 10^ 21 , consistent with that obtained previously. In this case, when allowing C_ PIN/XIS to vary it is not possible to constrain all the reflection parameters of interest, so we present the results with the inclination fixed at 45 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7469 as a monitored Seyfert 1 galaxy and uses its emission-line widths and variability radii to infer the vertical structure of its broad-line region.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 7469 has been the target of a large international variability campaign with ground-based telescopes in the optical and with the IUE satellite for the UV spectral lines. In 1996 from June 2 to July 30, optical spectrophotometric observations of NGC 7469 were completed (Collier et al. collier98 ) on 54 nights. From 1996 June 10 to July 29, the IUE satellite monitored the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 7469 continuously in an attempt to measure time delays between the UV continuum and the broad UV emission-line fluxes (Wanders et al. wanders97 ).
The `narrow' line Seyfert galaxies NGC 7469 and NGC 3783 show H line widths (FWHM) of 2170 to 3100 km s ^ 1 and C iv 1549 line widths of 3690 to 4300 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE observations of NGC 7469 to map H ii regions, stellar ages, oxygen abundance, and the environment of the type Ia supernova SN 2008ec.

quotes.quote: We present MUSE observations of galaxy NGC 7469 from its Science Verification to show how powerful is the combination of high-resolution wide-field integral field spectroscopy with both photometric and spectroscopic observations of supernova (SN) explosions. Using STARLIGHT and H ii explorer, we selected all H ii regions of the galaxy and produced 2-dimensional maps of the H equivalent width, average luminosity-weighted stellar age, and oxygen abundance. We measured deprojected galactocentric distances for all H ii regions, and radial gradients for all above-mentioned parameters. We positioned the type Ia SN 2008ec in the Branch et al. diagram, and finally discussed the characteristics of the SN parent H ii region compared to all other H ii regions in the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7469 as a barred Sy1 with a nuclear starburst and ring, possible nuclear outflow signatures, and bright circumnuclear PAH emission.

quotes.quote: Finally, NGC 7469 is a barred Sy1 galaxy ( Osterbrock93 ) that hosts a nuclear SB ( Davies07 ; 50-65 pc) and is surrounded by a 1.6 kpc SB ring (e.g.
The MIRI/MRS [ S IV ] 10.51 m (ionisation potential, IP: 34.8 eV) velocity and dispersion maps (shown in Appendix moremaps ) show the signatures of a potential nuclear outflow in NGC 7469.

In the case of NGC 7469 we also studied the PAH emission originating in the circumnuclear SF ring. All the selected SF regions in NGC 7469 show bright PAH emission with practically identical PAH profiles.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7469 as a strong type-1 AGN with a central XDR, finds agreement with prior global CN/CO ratios, and notes enhanced central ratios.

quotes.quote: In contrast, found an increase in the CN/CO ratio in the centres of NGC 7469 and NGC 1808, two galaxies with strong Seyfert nuclei.

The global ratios measured by Wilson2023 are 0.176 , 0.103 , and 0.045 in IRAS 13120, NGC 7469, and NGC 3256, respectively. Our global ratios of 0.17 and 0.10 agree with their results for IRAS 13120 and NGC 7469, but our global ratio in NGC 3256 is lower ( 0.02 ).
NGC 7469 has a strong nuclear type-1 AGN Liu2014 , with a sphere of influence of 3 pc with M BH = 1.06 10^ 7 M Peterson2014 , and a central XDR Izumi2020 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a 4 kpc halo around NGC 7469's compact nuclear radio component, steep radio spectral indices, and a compact VLBI component with northeast extension.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469 (2300+086) Y : Our image shows a halo 4 kpc in extent around the dominant compact component, which is coincident with the optical nucleus Clements81 , similar to the images in . determine the spectral index to be _ 5 GHz ^ 15 GHz 0.9. The features seen in this source are also seen by and in their lower resolution images. The 8.4 GHz map presented by also shows a point source surrounded by faint emission.

sources.summary: NGC 7469 is described as lacking iron-region variability, showing an apparent rms dip, and requiring improved modeling because a blurred reflector improves the fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469(2) and NGC 4593 are not variable across the iron line region, but neither was found to possess an obvious broad iron line component.
Three observations, NGC 4593, NGC 7314(1) and NGC 7469(2) appear to have dips in the iron line region of their rms spectra.
The time-averaged spectrum of NGC 7469(2) was not fit well with a reflection component, though there is a significant improvement when a blurred reflector is included.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7469 as a bare Seyfert, measuring intermediate spin and a broad Fe line.

quotes.quote: We methodically model the broad-band Suzaku spectra of a small sample of six 'bare' Seyfert galaxies: Ark 120, Fairall 9, MCG-02-14-009, Mrk 335, NGC 7469 and SWIFT J2127.4+5654.
We also present new spin constraints for Mrk 335 and NGC 7469 with intermediate values of a=0.70^ +0.12 _ 0.01 and a=0.69^ +0.09 _ 0.09 respectively.

In NGC 7469 we measure a broad Fe line featuring a low emissivity index of q=1.7^ +0.4 _ 0.6 , an inclination of i^ =23^ +15 _ 7 and EW=91^ +9 _ 8 eV, providing good agreement with the previous measurements made within Model C.

sources.summary: The paper finds significant broad ionised reflection in NGC 7469(1), but notes pegged inclination limits and tests showing Model ION would fit fake high-S/N spectra.

quotes.quote: Evidence for a broad and ionised reflection component at 95 confidence is found in five observations: MCG+8-11-11, Mrk 110, NGC 7469(1), Mrk 6 and NGC 7213, with the first three being significant even at 99 confidence.
The inclinations of the discs in NGC 7213 and NGC 7469(1) peg at the model limits i = 0 and 85 respectively and thus the detections in these cases cannot be considered robust.
Fitting the fake spectra with Model ION gave a good fit to Mrk 6, NGC 5506(1) and NGC 7469(1) at 99 confidence level.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7469 as an intermediate AGN/starburst LIRG whose IRS continuum has comparable AGN and starburst components up to 20 microns, with a dominant AGN contribution at reference wavelengths.

quotes.quote: NGC 5135 and NGC 7469 are intermediate cases.
The AGN and SB components of NGC 7469 have similar contributions within the IRS slit up to 20 m, but then at > 20 m the SB component clearly takes over.

There are only six galaxies with a dominant AGN contribution within the IRS slit at the four reference continuum wavelengths: CGCG 468-002-NED01, MCG 03-34-064, IC 4518W, NGC 6156, NGC 7469, and NGC 3690.

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC 7469 as an X-ray-variable radio-quiet AGN with high-frequency radio measurements and a positive 95 GHz excess compared with lower-frequency spectra.

quotes.quote: Six of the targets, namely NGC 3783, NGC 5548, NGC 7469, NGC 3227, Ark 564, and Mrk 766, are highly variable in the X-rays according to the extensive monitoring of markowitz04 .
We found measurements from 1.4 GHz up to the highest radio frequency available of 22 GHz for NGC 5506 and NGC 7469.

sources.summary: NGC 7469 contributed 169 HII regions, had the highest mean H equivalent width among the six galaxies, and clearly showed the two-peak sSFR cumulative-distribution behavior.

quotes.quote: After these cuts, the number of Hii regions that we are left with is: IC 1158 (292), NGC 6754 (423), ESO 362-18 (91), NGC 7742 (323), NGC 7469 (169), NGC 2906 (241).
The mean H EW values for the six galaxies are quite similar, from 26.00 ( ^ 12.76 _ +26.01 ) for NGC 6754 to 49.52 ( ^ 36.27 _ +107.06 ) for NGC 7469.
Interestingly, and with different strengths, all sSFR CDs show the same behavior of a two-peak distribution. In NGC 2906, NGC 7469 and NGC 7742 this is clearly seen.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7469 as a Seyfert 1 with circumnuclear starburst, finding over 30,000 WR stars plus strong central star formation and Seyfert activity.

quotes.quote: This object is the nucleus of NGC 7469 , a well known Seyfert 1 galaxy with a circumnuclear starburst Cutri84,Heckman86,Wilson86 , interacting with its companion IC 5283 .

The derived values span a wide range, from a few dozens in regions within the closest galaxies ( NGC 4630 and UGC 6320 ) to more than 30 thousands in regions belonging to the most distant objects ( NGC 6090 and NGC 7469 ).
Actually, in one of the two (NGC 7469) a direct evidence has been found of the existence of strong star formation with large number of WRs and Seyfert activity within the central 2 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 7469 as a Seyfert 1 in the long-term AGN monitoring sample, finding lower line-profile and flux variability than the strongest variables.

quotes.quote: Here we present some interesting findings of the long-term (more than a decade) optical monitoring campaign of a sample of seven type 1 AGN of different spectral types: Seyfert 1 galaxies (NGC 5548, NGC 4151, NGC 7469), Narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy - NLSy 1 (Ark 564), double-peaked line radio loud (3C 390.3) and radio quiet (Arp 102B) galaxy, and a luminous quasar (E1821+643), a supermassive binary black hole candidate.

From Table 1 and Figure fig:1 , we can see that: NGC 4151 and NGC 5548 are strongly variable Seyfert 1.5, NGC 7469 is also a Seyfert 1 but with lower variability in line fluxes and profiles, Arp 102B and 3C 390.3 have broad double-peaked lines, Ark 564 is a NLSy1, and E1821+643 is a quasar with strong red-asymmetry in line profiles, but almost no variability.

If we compare the normalized light-curves shown in Figure fig:1 , the strongest variable objects are NGC 5548, NGC 4151, and 3C 390.3 (see also in Table 1 the variability parameter F _ var which is 40 ), followed by Arp 102B and NGC 7469 (F _ var 20 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7469's electron density as spatially correlated with star-forming regions plus a southeast extension that may indicate obscured star formation.

quotes.quote: In the case of NGC 7469, shown in the second row from top in Fig. fig:density_maps_2 , in addition to the spatial correlation with the regions, the electron density in this target also shows an extension towards the South-East with respect to the central AGN. This site also seems to be a region of high extinction, indicating the possibility of obscured star formation in this region. This suggests that, in cases like NGC 7469, density maps could also possibly hint towards sites of obscured star formation as well in some objects.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7469 as a pure OH-emission source with no HCN-vib detection, low HCN-vib-to-infrared upper limit, no OH 65 micron absorption, and likely AGN-dominated cleared surroundings.

quotes.quote: Turning to the two sources that show pure emission in the OH 119 m doublet, I Zw 1 and NGC 7469, we note that they lack HCN-vib detections and that the upper limits to their L_ HCN-vib /L_ IR ratios are among the lowest in our sample. Furthermore, NGC 7469 has also been observed in the OH 65 m doublet, another tracer of obscured nuclei, but no absorption was detected gon15 . This is consistent with the results of vei13 that the sources with pure OH emission are the ones where an AGN dominates the luminosity and that these objects represent a phase where the feedback has subsided after clearing a path through the dusty surroundings.

sources.summary: The paper uses UV observations of AGN NGC 7469 as a low-redshift gravitational-redshift test, estimating z^m_g/z_g^p = 0.98 +/- 0.30.

quotes.quote: Results for NGC 5548 and NGC 7469 using H emission lines to estimate the FWHM At low cosmological redshift, we have estimates (based on UV observations) of the Fe III 2039-2113 gravitational redshifts for the nearby AGN NGC 5548 and NGC 7469 (Mediavilla et al. 2018). Taking average values of the FWHM_ H ( 6612 1646 km/s for NGC 5548 and 4267 719 km/s for NGC 7469, Yu et al. 2020), we obtain z^m_g/ z_g^p =0.88 0.35 for NGC 5548 and 0.98 0.30 for NGC 7469 (see red open hexagons in Figure fig ). Notice that the uncertainties of these single object measurements, are comparable to those obtained from the statistics of cluster galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper reports low AGN mid-infrared contribution for NGC 7469, star-formation-dominated diagnostic placement, and planned JWST/MIRI observations for the Seyfert.

quotes.quote: The two intermediate Seyferts, NGC 4602 and NGC 7469, which have values of / \ around 0.1, have a fractional contribution of the AGN to the mid-IR of 12 and 5 , respectively.

The two targets with the lowest / \ and / \ ratios, NGC 7469 and NGC 7496, have f _ AGN 0 and lie in the area occupied by the star-formation-dominated sources of and the starburst galaxies collected by see their Sect. 2.2 Fernandez-Ontiveros2016 , as shown in Fig.

We note that our sample is ideal for identifying sources to be targeted by integral field unit spectrographs and that MIRI observations of the Seyfert NGC 7469 in our sample are already planned within the Early Release Science program 1328 (PI. Armus L).

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 7469 as a Seyfert/AGN galaxy whose central AGN affects surface-density estimates, while its luminous AGN does not produce an enhanced CN/HCN ratio at 300 pc resolution.

quotes.quote: Our final sample of 9 galaxies (Table table:physical ) is quite heterogeneous, and includes galaxies with a prominent active galactic nucleus (AGN) or Seyfert nucleus (Circinus, NGC1808, NGC3627, NGC 7469), luminous and ultraluminous infrared galaxies (U/LIRGs: IRAS 13120-5453, NGC3256, VV114), galaxies with a starburst nucleus (M83, NGC 1808), and spiral galaxies (NGC3627, NGC3351).

We also make no correction for contamination from synchrotron emission, which means that the star formation rate surface densities will be overestimated near AGN, particularly in NGC 7469 and Circinus but also possibly in other galaxies containing an AGN (Table table:physical ).

In contrast, NGC 7469 does not show an enhanced CN/HCN ratio near its luminous AGN, likely because the 300 pc resolution of the data mixes emission from the AGN and surrounding regions within the beam.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7469 as the sample Type 1/Sy1 AGN whose nucleus and outflow show high-ionization lines and are excluded from the star-formation shock correlation.

quotes.quote: The 4.530 \ transition, with an IP of 75 eV slightly lower than , is only detected in the nucleus and outflow of the Type 1 AGN NGC 7469 with both lines having comparable intensities (see also Bianchin2023 ).

Actually, for the regions selected before, there is a very good linear correlation between the Hu-12 and the \ luminosities (excluding the nucleus and outflow of NGC 7469) which spans almost 2 dex and has a Spearman correlation coefficient of 0.94 (Fig. fig_flux_relation_hu12 top ).
The outflow and nucleus of the Sy1 NGC 7469 lie close to the grid with ionization parameters, U , between 2.00 and 2.25 in the range of Seyfert AGN (e.g., PerezDiaz2022 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7469 as a luminous infrared source with a circumnuclear multiwavelength ring and a resolved young nuclear star cluster younger than about 60 Myr.

quotes.quote: NGC 7469, This object is an luminous infrared source. Its circumnuclear (1 125 .5-2 125 .5) ring structure has been observed at radio, optical, near-infrared, and mid-infrared bands (see references in Davies, Tacconi Genzel 2004). Using the 2.3 m CO 2-0 absorption and continuum slope, Davies, Tacconi Genzel (2004) resolved the nuclear star cluster (0 125 .15-0 125 .20) and found that the age of the cluster is less than about 60 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper applies PCA to XMM-Newton X-ray spectroscopy of the nearby Seyfert NGC 7469, confirming distinct soft and hard continuum variability components.

quotes.quote: AGN; X-rays; PCA; spectroscopy We apply Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to study the variability of the X-ray continuum in the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 7469.
NGC 7469 is a bright nearby Seyfert (z = 0.0164) which was observed for 150 ks by XMM-Newton in November/December 2004.

On the subject of whether PCA tells us anything about the spectrum of NGC 7469 that we did not already know via less sophisticated methods (there is a soft and a hard component, varying in different ways), we conclude that it does not provide us with new information; the method does however give a model-independent confirmation of current knowledge.
